title: Khmer (Cambodian) Translator - Elections
description_text:
About Prisma International, Inc. - Prisma is a dynamic and growing language services provider committed to delivering exceptional translation solutions to clients worldwide. We pride ourselves on our dedication to linguistic excellence and client satisfaction. 
We are seeking experienced, professional KHMER (CAMBODIAN) translators to join our exclusive ELECTIONS team. In this independent contractor role, you will provide high quality written translations between English and Khmer. Successful applicants will have past Elections experience, exceptional time management skills and a commitment to linguistic excellence.
MINIMUM REQUIREMENTS - You must meet ALL of the following requirements:
Reside in the United States
18+ years of age
Five (5) years of professional experience providing written translations to local, state or federal U.S. government offices as a contractor, sub-contractor, employee, freelancer or volunteer. Two (2) of the five (5) years of experience must have been acquired within the last five years. Note: The resume you submit must clearly outline your five years of government-related translation experience and include dates. Elections experience is a plus. 
Availability to support Prisma after business hours (evenings) and on weekends and holidays during an Election cycle.
Willing to abide by Prisma's Master Service Agreement (MSA) and related Addenda.
High proficiency in English and a native speaker of the target language(s).
Reliable desktop PC or laptop with dedicated, secure, high-speed internet connection.
Experience using modern CAT tools (e.g. XTM, Trados, etc.).
Adobe Acrobat on your device and the ability to use Adobe's markup tools on a PDF file.
***At least ONE (1) of the following credentials*** You will be asked to provide proof.
Current voting member in good standing of the American Translators Association (ATA).
Current member in good standing of the American Association of Language Specialists
Vietnamese translators - certified through the Society of Translators and Interpreters of British Columbia
Certified and registered on the California Judicial Council Master List
Categorized as “Certified” or “Professionally Qualified” by the Administrative Office of the United States Courts
Accreditation by a regional or national accrediting agency recognized by the United States Secretary of Education
Accreditation by the Western Association of Schools and Colleges
If you do not have at least one of the above-named certifications / accreditations, we may consider applicants with ALL of the following:
Native speaker of the target language.
Educated in both English and the other language. Your resume must outline your educational background in a language-related field of study.
5+ years of professional experience translating for local, state or federal government offices as a contractor or sub-contractor. Two (2) of the five (5) years of experience must have been acquired within the last five years. 
TASKS & RESPONSIBILITIES:
Provide written translations using a Computer-Assisted Translation (CAT) tool. We provide XTM to you.
Adhere to all project work instructions, glossaries and reference materials.
Ensure the source content's concept, style and register are culturally and linguistically appropriate for the target language.
Perform language quality check steps (before delivery) to ensure the target translation is error-free and true to the meaning of the source document.
Deliver completed translation jobs by the given deadlines and project budgets.
Review formatted target language PDF files for accuracy; concisely mark any corrections needed. Using Adobe mark-up tools is required.
Communicate in a timely and professional manner with Prisma team members.
Participate in quality management processes, e.g. Corrective Action Reports (CARs), Root Cause Analyses (RCAs), process improvement and solution implementation.
Take all measures necessary to protect and keep private sensitive information shared with you to carry out translation projects (e.g. PHI, PII, SBU).
Adhere to policies and processes defined in Prisma's Master Service Agreement (MSA) and the Prisma Vendor Guide.
OTHER ATTRIBUTES:
Familiarity with voting materials: voter information guides, ballot measures, flyers, etc.
Excellent communication skills and quick responses to Prisma requests.
Ability to accommodate fast turnarounds for last-minute urgent corrections.
High attention to detail - essential for proofreading and verifying names, numbers, dates, etc. and identifying font corruption in a non-English formatted document (PDF).
Integrity and impartiality for handling sensitive content.
Awareness to understand cultural differences; ensure translated materials are appropriate and clear to specific communities.
Advanced ability to translate complex or specialized jargon into clear, concise language.
Ability to ask clarifying questions about technical or unclear content to ensure accuracy.
See full list of minimum requirements above.
ai_core_responsibilities: Provide high-quality written translations between English and Khmer, ensuring accuracy and cultural appropriateness. Adhere to project instructions and deliver completed jobs by deadlines.
ai_requirements_summary: Applicants must have at least five years of professional translation experience, including two years within the last five years, and must reside in the United States. High proficiency in English and Khmer, along with relevant certifications, are also required.
linkedin_org_locations: 1128 Harmon Place, Suite 310, Minneapolis, 55403, US
linkedin_org_description:
Prisma is a provider of enterprise technology and language solutions. We leverage the power of culture, language and technology to globalize your brand. 

For more than 30 years Prisma has been helping clients communicate globally. Our comprehensive service offerings span from strategic to operational solutions, including the LEX™ translation management platform, translation and localization services, cultural marketing, global brand strategy, and global business consulting.

Let us help you engage your global customers – in any language, culture, and markets.

title: Associate Sales Representative - Brooklyn, NY
locations_alt_raw: New York, New York, United States
locations_derived: New York, New York, United States
description_text:
The Kestra team has over 400 years of experience in the external and internal cardiac medical device markets. The company was founded in 2014 by industry leaders inspired by the opportunity to unite modern wearable technologies with proven device therapies. Kestra’s solutions combine high quality and technical performance with a wearable design that provides the greatest regard for patient comfort and dignity. Innovating versatile new ways to deliver care, Kestra is helping patients and their care teams harmoniously monitor, manage, and protect life.
An Associate Sales Representative is responsible for helping the local territory team(s) – supporting sales activities and customer support. This position contacts and consults with a variety of clients in a mixture of clinical settings. 
ESSENTIAL DUTIES
Develop relationship and present to physicians and hospital stakeholders to persuade potential clients to use the Assure Cardiac Recovery System.
The primary call points are to a variety of customers including general practitioners (GPs) hospital physicians, nurse practitioners (NP’s), Physician Assistants (PA’s) and nurse managers.
Collaborate and partner with the local Territory Manager(s).
Responsible for helping manage Assure Patient Specialists (ASPs); educating customers; in-servicing local health care professionals and support staff; and working with select service agencies on fitting needs and protocols. 
Establish and maintain effective channels of communication with Territory Manager and Sales Management
Adhere to Pledge of Confidentiality 
Information regarding a patient of this company shall not be released to any source outside of this company without the signed permission of the patient. Furthermore, information will only be released internally on a need-to-know basis. All Team Members will not discuss patient cases outside the office or with anyone not employed by this company unless they are directly involved with the patient’s case.
COMPETENCIES
Passion: Contagious excitement about the company – sense of urgency. Commitment to continuous improvement.
Integrity: Commitment, accountability, and dedication to the highest ethical standards.
Collaboration/Teamwork: Inclusion of Team Member regardless of geography, position, and product or service.
Action/Results: High energy, decisive planning, timely execution.
Innovation: Generation of new ideas from original thinking.
Customer Focus: Exceed customer expectations, quality of products, services, and experience always present of mind.
Emotional Intelligence: Recognizes, understands, manages one’s own emotions and is able to influence others. A critical skill for pressure situations.
QUALIFICATIONS
Education/Experience Required:
Bachelor’s degree
A minimum of 2+ year(s) of experience in one of the following areas:
Direct or B2B selling medical devices in hospital settings
Develop and present training and education materials
Must reside in the assigned territory
Excellent written and verbal communication skills 
Advanced organizational and prioritization skills, with strong ability to meet strict deadlines 
Ability to collaborate within a Team environment and across functions
Preferred:
Experience in a Medical Education-related field 
Ability to be a self-starter, seek new and better methods, and work with minimum supervision
Capability to discern relevant facts and in turn, effectively resolve issues by making good decisions (compliance, quality, integrity, ethics, and critical thinking ability)
Prior experience selling cardiac products and/or durable medical equipment (DME) in clinical or hospital settings
Work ENVIRONMENT 
Fast paced field role
Noise volume typical of being in the field or clinical setting
Extended hours when needed
Drug-free
Frequent repetitive motions that may include wrists, hands and/or fingers, such as keyboard and mouse usage
Frequent stationary position, often standing or sitting for prolonged periods of time
Frequent computer use
Frequent phone and other business machine use
Ability to lift up to 40 pounds unassisted, at times from in and out of vehicle
PHYSICAL DEMANDS
Frequent repetitive motions that may include wrists, hands and/or fingers, such as keyboard and mouse usage
Frequent stationary position, often standing or sitting for prolonged periods of time
Frequent computer use
Frequent phone and other business machine use
Ability to lift up to 40 pounds unassisted, at times from in and out of vehicle
TRAVEL
Occasional travel, domestic, may be required, less than 50%
OTHER DUTIES:
This job description is not designed to cover or contain a comprehensive listing of activities, duties or responsibilities that are required of the Team Member. Duties, responsibilities, and activities may change or new ones may be assigned at any time with or without notice.
Kestra offers a very competitive benefit package including Medical, Dental, 401K with Match, etc.
Pay equity is an important part of Kestra’s Culture. Our compensation ranges are guided by national and local salary surveys and take into consideration experience level and internal equity. Each role is benchmarked based on the job description provided If your qualifications and/or experience level are outside of the posted position, we encourage you to apply as we are growing fast and roles that are coming soon may not be posted.
Salary: Annualized at 70,000, plus bonus and uncapped commission. Depending on experience and location.
At this time, we are unable to offer relocation assistance at Kestra Medical Technologies.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Associate Sales Representative is responsible for supporting sales activities and customer support in the local territory. This includes developing relationships with physicians and hospital stakeholders to promote the Assure Cardiac Recovery System.
ai_requirements_summary: A bachelor's degree and a minimum of 2 years of experience in direct or B2B selling of medical devices in hospital settings are required. Excellent communication skills and the ability to collaborate within a team environment are also essential.
linkedin_org_slogan: Kestra's mission is to provide innovative, intuitive medical technologies to protect and support at-risk patients.
linkedin_org_locations: 3933 Lake Washington Blvd NE, Suite 200, Kirkland, WA 98033, US
linkedin_org_description:
At Kestra, we stand for patients at-risk and the people who care for them. Together we work to protect, support, and empower them with wearable technologies, tools, and better answers as they navigate life’s most challenging medical events.

Cardiovascular disease is the number one cause of death globally. We’re taking a fresh approach to cardiac monitoring and delivery of definitive therapy with wearable products and applications. We aim to make the entire recovery process easier for patients by bringing comfort and assurance to them—along with advanced clinical insights and tools to their medical care teams.

Kestra Medical Technologies, Ltd. is a commercial-stage wearable medical device and digital healthcare company focused on transforming patient outcomes in cardiovascular disease using monitoring and therapeutic intervention technologies that are intuitive, intelligent, and connected. 

Visit us at www.kestramedical.com

title: Mental Health Therapist
description_text:
Since 1905, The Bradley Center has continually served children and families in need. Today, the residential treatment facility provides 24 hour care and trauma-informed treatment to children who have a history of trauma and who may present with a wide range of challenges. 
Therapists at Bradley receive training in evidence-based interventions, carry a small caseload, develop treatment plans with the clients and families, and participate in both team and individual supervision. This is an excellent opportunity for a new therapist to gain valuable experience and launch a career. We also offer the opportunity to receive the supervision necessary for licensure. 
Primary Responsibilities:
Provide individual and family therapy 
Provide treatment education and guidance including the development of individualized treatment plans
Adhere to treatment plan objectives
Attend monthly treatment planning meetings
Communicate with family and external partners 
Documentation/behavioral data tracking
Qualifications best suited for the position:
Initiative
Ability to build rapport with clients
Patience and creativity while working with residents at their own pace
Excellent verbal and written communication skills
Professional demeanor, sound judgment, dependability, and a strong work ethic
Master's degree in social work, psychology, counseling, art or music therapy or other related field
Minimum of one year experience working with youth is a human services or psychiatric setting preferred
Valid driver's license and car insurance
Must be at least 21 years of age
Student loan repayment program 
$1500 sign on bonus
$2000 relocation assistance
Referral bonuses
4 weeks of Paid Time Off in the first year of employment
Health, dental and vision Coverage
401(k)
Life insurance
Free parking and meals
Opportunity for licensure supervision
Advanced training opportunities and continuing education credits
We are committed to advocacy for children with special needs, care and compassion, and focusing on the inherent strengths of children, youth and families without regard to race, religion, sexual orientation, national origin, age, or gender. The Bradley Center is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All applicants will be considered for employment without attention to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, veteran or disability status.
ai_core_responsibilities: Therapists provide individual and family therapy, develop treatment plans, and participate in treatment planning meetings. They also communicate with families and external partners while adhering to treatment plan objectives.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have a Master's degree in a relevant field and preferably one year of experience working with youth in a human services or psychiatric setting. A valid driver's license and car insurance are also required.
linkedin_org_locations: 5180 Campbells Run Raod, Pittsburgh, PA 15205, US
linkedin_org_description:
For over 100 years Bradley has been providing hope to children and families in need of comprehensive and caring services. 

Bradley was founded by The United Methodist Women in 1905 as The Elizabeth A. Bradley Home for Children and operated for many years as an orphanage and as interim housing for children whose parents were separated or divorced. In 1972, it was incorporated as The Bradley Center to serve abused, neglected and dependent children. 

In 1991, Bradley was reorganized by a new non-denominational, community-based Board of Trustees and executive management. 

The Bradley Center has since evolved into an accredited, regional behavioral healthcare and child welfare system that provides hope to young girls and boys. 

Bradley is committed to advocacy for children and dedicated to the restoration of productive relationships among children, their families and the community whenever possible. 

The Bradley Center implements the Sanctuary Model of Trauma Informed Care developed by Dr. Sandra Bloom. Sanctuary is a treatment and organizational model that is based on understanding trauma. Every staff and resident in care focus on "SELF"​ - Safety (physical, psychological, social and moral), Emotion management, Loss (traumatic experiences and separation), and the Future (how can things get better?). 

Bradley has earned the quality distinction of accreditation by the Joint Commission on Accreditation of Healthcare Organizations. It is also licensed by the Pennsylvania Departments of Education and Welfare. 

Bradley's Vision is to be recognized nationally by families, communities and professionals as a premier behavioral healthcare, education and child welfare organization that promotes the physical, social, emotional, educational and spiritual growth of the children, youth and families that it serves.

title: Japanese & Tagalog Translators - Elections
description_text:
About Prisma International, Inc. - Prisma is a dynamic and growing language services provider committed to delivering exceptional translation solutions to clients worldwide. We pride ourselves on our dedication to linguistic excellence and client satisfaction. 
We are seeking experienced, professional JAPANESE and TAGALOG translators to join our exclusive ELECTIONS team. In this independent contractor role, you will provide high quality written translations between English and your native language. Successful applicants will have past Elections experience, exceptional time management skills and a commitment to linguistic excellence.
MINIMUM REQUIREMENTS - You must meet ALL of the following requirements:
Reside in the United States
18+ years of age
Five (5) years of professional experience providing written translations to local, state or federal U.S. government offices as a contractor, sub-contractor, employee, freelancer or volunteer. Two (2) of the five (5) years of experience must have been acquired within the last five years. Note: The resume you submit must clearly outline your five years of government-related translation experience and include dates. Elections experience is a plus. 
Availability to support Prisma after business hours (evenings) and on weekends and holidays during an Election cycle.
Willing to abide by Prisma's Master Service Agreement (MSA) and related Addenda.
High proficiency in English and a native speaker of the target language(s).
Reliable desktop PC or laptop with dedicated, secure, high-speed internet connection.
Experience using modern CAT tools (e.g. XTM, Trados, etc.).
Adobe Acrobat on your device and the ability to use Adobe's markup tools on a PDF file.
***At least ONE (1) of the following credentials*** You will be asked to provide proof.
Current voting member in good standing of the American Translators Association (ATA).
Current member in good standing of the American Association of Language Specialists
Vietnamese translators - certified through the Society of Translators and Interpreters of British Columbia
Certified and registered on the California Judicial Council Master List
Categorized as “Certified” or “Professionally Qualified” by the Administrative Office of the United States Courts
Accreditation by a regional or national accrediting agency recognized by the United States Secretary of Education
Accreditation by the Western Association of Schools and Colleges
If you do not have at least one of the above-named certifications / accreditations, we may consider applicants with ALL of the following:
Native speaker of the target language.
Educated in both English and the other language. Your resume must outline your educational background in a language-related field of study.
5+ years of professional experience translating for local, state or federal government offices as a contractor or sub-contractor. Two (2) of the five (5) years of experience must have been acquired within the last five years. 
TASKS & RESPONSIBILITIES:
Provide written translations using a Computer-Assisted Translation (CAT) tool. We provide XTM to you.
Adhere to all project work instructions, glossaries and reference materials.
Ensure the source content's concept, style and register are culturally and linguistically appropriate for the target language.
Perform language quality check steps (before delivery) to ensure the target translation is error-free and true to the meaning of the source document.
Deliver completed translation jobs by the given deadlines and project budgets.
Review formatted target language PDF files for accuracy; concisely mark any corrections needed. Using Adobe mark-up tools is required.
Communicate in a timely and professional manner with Prisma team members.
Participate in quality management processes, e.g. Corrective Action Reports (CARs), Root Cause Analyses (RCAs), process improvement and solution implementation.
Take all measures necessary to protect and keep private sensitive information shared with you to carry out translation projects (e.g. PHI, PII, SBU).
Adhere to policies and processes defined in Prisma's Master Service Agreement (MSA) and the Prisma Vendor Guide.
OTHER ATTRIBUTES:
Familiarity with voting materials: voter information guides, ballot measures, flyers, etc.
Excellent communication skills and quick responses to Prisma requests.
Ability to accommodate fast turnarounds for last-minute urgent corrections.
High attention to detail - essential for proofreading and verifying names, numbers, dates, etc. and identifying font corruption in a non-English formatted document (PDF).
Integrity and impartiality for handling sensitive content.
Awareness to understand cultural differences; ensure translated materials are appropriate and clear to specific communities.
Advanced ability to translate complex or specialized jargon into clear, concise language.
Ability to ask clarifying questions about technical or unclear content to ensure accuracy.
See full list of minimum requirements above.
ai_core_responsibilities: Provide high-quality written translations between English and Japanese or Tagalog, ensuring cultural and linguistic appropriateness. Adhere to project instructions and deliver completed translations by deadlines.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have at least five years of professional translation experience for government offices, with two years within the last five years. High proficiency in English and native fluency in Japanese or Tagalog is required, along with experience using CAT tools.
linkedin_org_locations: 1128 Harmon Place, Suite 310, Minneapolis, 55403, US
linkedin_org_description:
Prisma is a provider of enterprise technology and language solutions. We leverage the power of culture, language and technology to globalize your brand. 

For more than 30 years Prisma has been helping clients communicate globally. Our comprehensive service offerings span from strategic to operational solutions, including the LEX™ translation management platform, translation and localization services, cultural marketing, global brand strategy, and global business consulting.

Let us help you engage your global customers – in any language, culture, and markets.

title: Administrative Assistant
description_text:
As a world class asset-based carrier, Charger Logistics specializes in delivering assets, on time and on budget. With the diverse fleet of equipment, we can handle a range of freight, including dedicated loads, specialized hauls, temperature controlled goods and HAZMAT cargo.
Charger logistics invests time and support into its employees to provide them with the room to learn and grow their expertise and work their way up. We are entrepreneurial-minded organization that welcomes and support individual idea and strategies. We are currently expanding and looking to add a motivated individual to our team based out of our Hutchins office.
Responsibilities:
Ensuring compliance of company’s safety policy and procedure by regular review meetings with staff and monitoring company’s CVOR-safety rating, driver vehicle inspections on monthly basis.
Processing IRP applications and creating truck binder for owner operators by making sure all the truck authority documents are updated.
Maintaining complete and accurate records of tractor and trailer maintenance files such as preventive maintenance bills for owner operator and company units as well.
Ensuring all accident claims are registered with updated documents, adjust physical damage claims including unreported damage claims.
Discuss accidents, damage, preventability and processes with commercial vehicle operators. Maintain files for litigation, subrogation, physical damage, liability and workers compensation claims.
Track each accident claim by updating Crash frequency register and reviewing loss run report.
Negotiates directly with insurance company adjusters, claimants, and third-party claimant representatives upon receipt of critical information.
Evaluating company’s safety rating by regularly updating and monitoring Risk factor spreadsheet.
Reviewing and persistent follow up on traffic tickets, convictions and accidents.
Regularly monitoring federal and provincial safety regulations to keep current with safety and compliance programs and to implement changes
Efficiently manage documents of all trucks and trailer files with two years of annual inspections and repair bills with attached CVOR in each vehicle file and driver file.
Reviewing CVOR, applying and renewing permits, IRP applications.
Selects, directs and manages vendor relationships including towing companies, body shops, garage and parking/storage yards to get accurate costs as needed in settlement of claims
Order police reports, Set-up new claim files, confirmation of loss to insurer & broker
Communicate with the Claims team as well as the Safety/Compliance team (and others as necessary) on any relevant items
Be available on-call for nights and weekends for serious accidents and other duties as necessary.
Perform other administrative and general tasks as they arise.
Associates Degree in a relevant field
1+ years experience working in the Safety & Compliance Department
Excellent verbal and written communication skills
Good time management with an ability to prioritize and delegate work amongst a group
Computer proficiency – excellent MS Office skills, must be comfortable with new software and interfaces
Ability to maintain a high level of confidentiality.
Able to work in a loud, fast-paced environment
Available to work on some weekends
Competitive Salary
Healthcare Benefit Package
Career Growth
ai_core_responsibilities: The Administrative Assistant will ensure compliance with safety policies and procedures, manage accident claims, and maintain accurate records of vehicle maintenance. They will also negotiate with insurance representatives and monitor safety regulations.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have an Associate's Degree and at least 1 year of experience in the Safety & Compliance Department. Excellent communication skills and proficiency in MS Office are also required.
linkedin_org_locations: 25 Production Road, Brampton, Ontario L6T4N8, CA
linkedin_org_description:
Charger Logistics'​ strives to offer the best client focused logistics solution. We start with flexibility. By offering various safe and efficient solutions for all product sizes, weights and sensitivities our limits are minimal. Additionally, our network, various locations throughout North America and fleet size allow us to offer our clients what they need every time.

Charger Logistics was founded in the early 2000's and has grown by leaps and bounds since then. From owning a single truck to owning a fleet of over eight-hundred trucks, two-thousand trailers including reefers, dry vans, chassis, flat beds, step decks and more! A lot has changed however, our commitment to our clients will never change.

title: Infrastructure Engineer (Windows/Linux/VMware skills) - Secret clearance
description_text:
We are seeking a hands-on Infrastructure Engineer with a Secret clearance to support and maintain the ServiceNow platform and its underlying infrastructure in a disconnected (dark site / air-gapped) environment. This role is responsible for designing, deploying, configuring, and maintaining secure, highly available infrastructure including servers, virtualization platforms, operating systems, and application deployments. The ideal candidate is proactive, security-focused, and experienced with VMware, Windows and Linux administration, automation, and restricted/offline environments. Basic database support may be required; deep DBA expertise is not necessary.
Infrastructure & Virtualization
Deploy, configure, and manage VMware vCenter, ESXi hosts, virtual machines, templates, and resource pools. 
Administer Windows Server and RHEL environments, including Active Directory and identity services. 
Create and maintain OVAs, golden images, and VM templates for consistent deployments. 
Automate provisioning and maintenance using PowerShell, Bash, or similar tools. 
Apply security hardening and compliance standards (CIS, NIST, STIGs). 
Support offline patching, updates, and secure change management in air-gapped environments. 
Ensure scalability, availability, and operational continuity. 
Platform & Application Support
Provide infrastructure-level support for a disconnected ServiceNow deployment. 
Assist with application deployments, configuration, and troubleshooting. 
Manage storage, basic networking, and backup/restore processes (e.g., Cohesity or similar). 
Provide light support for related systems such as MS SQL tools (WSUS, Volume Activation). 
Database (Secondary)
Perform basic MariaDB/MySQL administration including configuration, backups, monitoring, and minor tuning. 
Assist with availability and recovery activities as needed. 
Collaboration & Documentation
Partner with Architecture, DevOps, Security, and application teams to deliver compliant solutions. 
Produce and maintain infrastructure diagrams, build guides, deployment procedures, and compliance documentation. 
Contribute to incident response and root-cause analysis in secure environments. 
Proven experience as an Infrastructure Engineer or similar role. 
Strong hands-on expertise with VMware (vCenter, ESXi, templates, OVAs, cloning, deployment). 
Solid Windows Server and RHEL/Linux administration skills, including Active Directory. 
Experience building and deploying virtual images and application servers. 
Scripting/automation proficiency (PowerShell, Bash; Python a plus). 
Knowledge of security hardening and compliance frameworks (CIS, NIST, STIGs). 
Experience operating in air-gapped or disconnected environments. 
Strong troubleshooting, communication, and documentation skills. 
Ability to work both independently and within cross-functional teams in high-security settings. 
Preferred / Nice to Have
Basic MariaDB/MySQL administration. 
Familiarity with enterprise backup and disaster recovery tools (e.g., Cohesity). 
Exposure to ServiceNow infrastructure requirements. 
Basic MS SQL support experience. 
Knowledge of additional virtualization platforms (Hyper-V, KVM) or hybrid/cloud concepts. 
Certifications such as VMware VCP, RHCSA/RHCE, Microsoft Windows Server, Security+, or equivalent. 
Experience supporting secure government or large enterprise environments.
140-170K (pending experience)
Our comprehensive benefits package for full-time salaried employees is effective immediately upon the start date. Benefits include comprehensive PPO medical coverage with access to a Health Savings Account (HSA) option, a vision plan, and dental insurance with the base dental plan option paid for by PGTEK. Life Insurance, Short and Long-Term disability, and Critical Illness insurance have premiums covered. Additionally, PGTEK offers a matching 401(k) plan and a discount on pet insurance through ASPCA Pet Insurance. An Employee Assistance Program is available at no cost to all employees. PGTEK offers a generous amount of PTO and Holidays, and an Education Assistance Program is available after 12 months of employment.
ABOUT PGTEK:
PGTEK is a true consulting organization dedicated to helping clients achieve their business and technology objectives utilizing our decades of experience and business relationships. PGTEK invests in the educational advancements of our staff by providing the necessary resources to complete Professional and Business Certifications. Our company is our people, and we treat them like family.
EOE, including disability/veterans
ai_core_responsibilities: The Infrastructure Engineer will design, deploy, configure, and maintain secure infrastructure for the ServiceNow platform in a disconnected environment. Responsibilities include managing VMware, Windows, and Linux systems, as well as providing application support and ensuring compliance with security standards.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have strong hands-on experience with VMware, Windows Server, and Linux administration, along with scripting and automation skills. Familiarity with security frameworks and experience in air-gapped environments are also essential.
linkedin_org_locations: 20394 Exchange St, Suite 250, Ashburn, Virginia 20147, US
linkedin_org_description:
Our firm provides global IT infrastructure professional services to industry-leading OEM, infrastructure software, and significant private and public sector organizations. We have completed thousands of successful engagements overseen by our best-in-class project management office (PMO). Typically, each engagement involves data storage, networking, and system virtualization and is delivered by our seasoned certified technical consulting staff. We provide assessment, architecture, installation, and professional residency services for each of our IT Infrastructure focus areas. 

SOLUTIONS SUPPORTING NATIONAL DEFENSE:
PGTEK has a rich history of supporting federal civilian agencies, the United States military, and the Intelligence Community. Throughout our corporate history, we have been awarded numerous high-profile information technology federal prime and subcontracts. PGTEK’s Project Management Office (PMO) is staffed with full-time contracting officers, accountants with DCAA experience, Facility Security Officers (FSO), and seasoned program management that understands the federal government.

Our firm has unique capabilities to work with every federal agency, including the Intelligence Community. PGTEK provides services outside the continental United States (OCONUS) on an ongoing basis at military installations, hot zones, and combat theaters.

title: : Asistente de ingeniería-Bionest
locations_alt_raw: Escazu, San José Province, Costa Rica
description_text:
Nuestro cliente, Bionest, está en búsqueda de un:
ASISTENTE DE INGENIERÍA
Inicialmente se dedicará a hacer los dibujos técnicos en AutoCad para permisos en las instituciones para plantas de tratamiento. También se le entrenará para llevar el proceso y control de los permisos de ley para las plantas de tratamiento
Una vez tenga control del proceso de los permisos de las plantas de tratamiento, se le entrenará en la tecnología propietaria de tratamiento de aguas residuales y en el tratamiento en general de aguas residuales.
Una vez entrenado en la tecnología de tratamiento de aguas residuales, se preparará para tomar el Puesto de Ingeniería de la empresa, dando soporte técnico a Instaladores y Ventas.
Requisitos: 
• 1- Manejo avanzado de AUTOCAD 
• Estudiante universitario avanzado o graduado en Ingeniería Civil, Ingeniería Sanitaria o Ingeniería Mecánica. 
• 2- Preferible contar con transporte propio. 
• 3- Preferible contar con idioma inglés.
1- Ordenado • 2- Proactivo • 3- Preferiblemente que viva en San José (zona Oeste preferible)
ai_core_responsibilities: The assistant will initially focus on creating technical drawings in AutoCad for treatment plant permits. They will also be trained to manage the legal permit process and eventually support technical aspects of wastewater treatment.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have advanced AutoCad skills and be advanced students or graduates in Civil, Sanitary, or Mechanical Engineering. Preferably, candidates should have their own transportation and knowledge of English.

Doris Peters & Asociados (DPA) is a Human Resources consulting firm with 20 years in the regional market offering human capital management solutions.

DPA has a professional specialized team capable of understanding the specific needs of the client to identify the best talent by using tools and updated evaluation methods to deliver a service tailored to its requirements.

As corporate advisors we offer an extended services portfolio to provide support in human capital management both in operational management as well as in human resources processes, policies, and general management of organizations.

The extended array of services that we offer enable our clients not only to count on the best human talent but also to ensure a solid and stable organization by means of an excellent human resources management.

title: Account Manager
description_text:
Revolutionize Agency Finance. Own the Strategy. Ignite Your Career.
This isn't just an Accounting Manager role; it's a one-of-a-kind opportunity to redefine financial leadership for the fastest-growing agencies in the United States. 
Our client is a category leader, transforming complex numbers into clear, decisive strategy. This is your stage.
If you crave high-stakes challenges, love client interaction, and are driven to build and perfect financial systems, this is for you. 
You'll partner directly with ambitious agency founders, bringing unprecedented clarity and strategic advantage to their businesses.
A Day in This Role Could Look Like:
You log on, ready for impact. A founder's financial roadmap needs your expert touch. You're not just reviewing data; you're decoding the future.

title: Auto Body Repair Mechanic
description_text:
Charger Logistics Inc. is a world-class asset-based carrier with locations across North America. With over 20 years of experience providing the best logistics solutions, Charger Logistics has transformed into a world-class transport provider and continues to grow.
Charger Logistics invests time and support into its employees to provide them with the room to learn and grow their expertise and work their way up. We are an entrepreneurial-minded organization that welcomes and support individual ideas and strategies. We are currently searching for an organized, well-rounded individual to join our dynamic team as a Truck Shop Helper at our Cambridge location.
Availability: Full-time; Permanent
Responsibilities:
Operating shunt trucks to relocate trailers to designated areas within the yard.
Loading and Unloading: Assisting with the loading, tarping, and unloading of trailers on customer property.
Safety Compliance: Conducting pre- and post-trip inspections of shunt trucks to ensure safety procedures are followed at all times.
Communication: Coordinating with dispatch and yard personnel to organize loads and ensure efficient movement of trailers and containers.
Record-Keeping: Maintaining accurate logs of shipments and deliveries.
Assisting with Reefer wash job duties 
A valid driver’s license, Commercial Driver’s License (CDL) id preferred but not required.
Knowledge of truck parts and equipment and truck repairs would be considered as an asset.
Lifting 40-50 lbs
Knowledge of DOT/MTO rules and regulations is an asset
Effective problem solver with the ability to understand issues and find solutions in a timely manner
High attention to detail
Excellent time management skills with the ability to work in a fast-paced work environment
Mechanical background is an asset
ai_core_responsibilities: The Auto Body Repair Mechanic will operate shunt trucks to relocate trailers and assist with loading and unloading tasks. They will also conduct safety inspections and maintain accurate logs of shipments.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have a valid driver's license, with a Commercial Driver's License preferred. Knowledge of truck parts and equipment, as well as DOT/MTO regulations, is considered an asset.
linkedin_org_locations: 25 Production Road, Brampton, Ontario L6T4N8, CA
linkedin_org_description:
Charger Logistics'​ strives to offer the best client focused logistics solution. We start with flexibility. By offering various safe and efficient solutions for all product sizes, weights and sensitivities our limits are minimal. Additionally, our network, various locations throughout North America and fleet size allow us to offer our clients what they need every time.

Charger Logistics was founded in the early 2000's and has grown by leaps and bounds since then. From owning a single truck to owning a fleet of over eight-hundred trucks, two-thousand trailers including reefers, dry vans, chassis, flat beds, step decks and more! A lot has changed however, our commitment to our clients will never change.

title: Board Certified Physician Reviewers - Orthopedic Surgery - Connecticut License
description_text:
Dane Street is seeking experienced Board-Certified Orthopedic Surgeons to join our growing team of expert clinical reviewers. In this role, you will apply your expertise to conduct comprehensive reviews of clinical cases and provide objective, evidence-based medical opinions.
This is a telework opportunity that allows you to customize your schedule while working as a 1099 independent contractor.
Your primary responsibilities will include reviewing medical records, preparing clear and concise clinical summaries, participating in peer-to-peer discussions when appropriate, and responding to specific questions from our clients related to orthopedic care.
Join a team that values your clinical judgment and commitment to improving the quality, accuracy, and consistency of healthcare assessments. Your expertise plays a vital role in supporting high-quality, defensible medical determinations.
MD or DO with completion of an accredited Orthopedic Surgery residency
Board Certification in Orthopedic Surgery
Current, unrestricted Connecticut medical license
Minimum of 5+ years of clinical Orthopedic Surgery experience
Ability to attend all required orientation and training sessions
Maintains appropriate credentialing, state licensure, and any certifications required to perform the role
Independent consultant role offering schedule flexibility and predictable work hours
Ability to choose case types and workload based on your availability
No doctor–patient relationship established; no treatment is provided — all reviews are advisory only
Streamlined case flow through a user-friendly online work portal
Dane Street manages all administrative processes, medical record organization, and communications
Fully prepared cases with organized medical records and applicable clinical guidelines
Initial training provided, along with ongoing support and a dedicated point of contact
ai_core_responsibilities: The primary responsibilities include reviewing medical records, preparing clinical summaries, and participating in peer-to-peer discussions. The role focuses on providing objective, evidence-based medical opinions related to orthopedic care.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must be MD or DO with board certification in Orthopedic Surgery and a current Connecticut medical license. A minimum of 5 years of clinical experience in Orthopedic Surgery is required.
linkedin_org_slogan: National Provider of IME and Medical Peer Review Services
linkedin_org_locations: 7111 Fairway Drive, Suite 201, Palm Beach Gardens, FL 33418, US
linkedin_org_description:
Dane Street is the industry's fastest growing national IME and Peer Review provider with a panel of board-certified, active-practice physicians in all 50 states. Services are provided to the Workers Compensation, Pharmacy, Disability, Group Health and Auto/Liability lines of business.

Dane Street's Review and Evaluation services provide improved report quality, faster turnaround time and higher adjuster/nurse satisfaction and productivity.

title: Vietnamese Translator - Elections
description_text:
About Prisma International, Inc. - Prisma is a dynamic and growing language services provider committed to delivering exceptional translation solutions to clients worldwide. We pride ourselves on our dedication to linguistic excellence and client satisfaction. 
We are seeking experienced, professional VIETNAMESE translators to join our exclusive ELECTIONS team. In this independent contractor role, you will provide high quality written translations between English and Vietnamese. Successful applicants will have past Elections experience, exceptional time management skills and a commitment to linguistic excellence.
MINIMUM REQUIREMENTS - You must meet ALL of the following requirements:
Reside in the United States
18+ years of age
Five (5) years of professional experience providing written translations to local, state or federal U.S. government offices as a contractor, sub-contractor, employee, freelancer or volunteer. Two (2) of the five (5) years of experience must have been acquired within the last five years. Note: The resume you submit must clearly outline your five years of government-related translation experience and include dates. Elections experience is a plus. 
Availability to support Prisma after business hours (evenings) and on weekends and holidays during an Election cycle.
Willing to abide by Prisma's Master Service Agreement (MSA) and related Addenda.
High proficiency in English and a native speaker of the target language(s).
Reliable desktop PC or laptop with dedicated, secure, high-speed internet connection.
Experience using modern CAT tools (e.g. XTM, Trados, etc.).
Adobe Acrobat on your device and the ability to use Adobe's markup tools on a PDF file.
***At least ONE (1) of the following credentials*** You will be asked to provide proof.
Current voting member in good standing of the American Translators Association (ATA).
Current member in good standing of the American Association of Language Specialists
Vietnamese translators - certified through the Society of Translators and Interpreters of British Columbia
Certified and registered on the California Judicial Council Master List
Categorized as “Certified” or “Professionally Qualified” by the Administrative Office of the United States Courts
Accreditation by a regional or national accrediting agency recognized by the United States Secretary of Education
Accreditation by the Western Association of Schools and Colleges
If you do not have at least one of the above-named certifications / accreditations, we may consider applicants with ALL of the following:
Native speaker of the target language.
Educated in both English and the other language. Your resume must outline your educational background in a language-related field of study.
5+ years of professional experience translating for local, state or federal government offices as a contractor or sub-contractor. Two (2) of the five (5) years of experience must have been acquired within the last five years. 
TASKS & RESPONSIBILITIES:
Provide written translations using a Computer-Assisted Translation (CAT) tool. We provide XTM to you.
Adhere to all project work instructions, glossaries and reference materials.
Ensure the source content's concept, style and register are culturally and linguistically appropriate for the target language.
Perform language quality check steps (before delivery) to ensure the target translation is error-free and true to the meaning of the source document.
Deliver completed translation jobs by the given deadlines and project budgets.
Review formatted target language PDF files for accuracy; concisely mark any corrections needed. Using Adobe mark-up tools is required.
Communicate in a timely and professional manner with Prisma team members.
Participate in quality management processes, e.g. Corrective Action Reports (CARs), Root Cause Analyses (RCAs), process improvement and solution implementation.
Take all measures necessary to protect and keep private sensitive information shared with you to carry out translation projects (e.g. PHI, PII, SBU).
Adhere to policies and processes defined in Prisma's Master Service Agreement (MSA) and the Prisma Vendor Guide.
OTHER ATTRIBUTES:
Familiarity with voting materials: voter information guides, ballot measures, flyers, etc.
Excellent communication skills and quick responses to Prisma requests.
Ability to accommodate fast turnarounds for last-minute urgent corrections.
High attention to detail - essential for proofreading and verifying names, numbers, dates, etc. and identifying font corruption in a non-English formatted document (PDF).
Integrity and impartiality for handling sensitive content.
Awareness to understand cultural differences; ensure translated materials are appropriate and clear to specific communities.
Advanced ability to translate complex or specialized jargon into clear, concise language.
Ability to ask clarifying questions about technical or unclear content to ensure accuracy.
See full list of minimum requirements above.
ai_core_responsibilities: Provide high-quality written translations between English and Vietnamese, ensuring accuracy and cultural appropriateness. Adhere to project instructions and deliver completed translations by deadlines.
ai_requirements_summary: Applicants must have at least five years of professional translation experience, with two years within the last five years, specifically for government offices. They must also be proficient in English and a native speaker of Vietnamese, with experience using CAT tools and Adobe Acrobat.
linkedin_org_locations: 1128 Harmon Place, Suite 310, Minneapolis, 55403, US
linkedin_org_description:
Prisma is a provider of enterprise technology and language solutions. We leverage the power of culture, language and technology to globalize your brand. 

For more than 30 years Prisma has been helping clients communicate globally. Our comprehensive service offerings span from strategic to operational solutions, including the LEX™ translation management platform, translation and localization services, cultural marketing, global brand strategy, and global business consulting.

Let us help you engage your global customers – in any language, culture, and markets.

title: Carpenter
description_text:
Enterprise Precast Concrete is the nation’s premier provider of high quality architectural precast components for decades. We provide products from architectural panels and custom architectural fabrication to site furnishings and design/build consultation. The carpenter position works with the Carpenter Foreman in performing all activities related to fabrication and setup of forms for all precast concrete products fabricated in the plant, as well as performing miscellaneous carpentry work as directed by management.
Essential Duties and Responsibilities:
Utilizes approved piece tickets to construct forms for specific precast concrete panels in conformance with the architectural specifications and applicable PCI requirements.
Meets regularly with the Carpenter Foreman to discuss anticipated production sequences and potential form construction problems, and to provide input on improvement of work processes and work flow.
Provides information to the Carpenter Foreman on the inventory status of all form-related material so that supplies and materials can be ordered in a timely fashion to ensure performance of scheduled work.
Properly maintains tools and utilizes appropriate safety guards.
Minimum of 2 years of recent general carpentry experience.
Minimum of 2 years working with finish carpentry products such as cabinetry a other forms of woodworking.
Ability to read, analyze and interpret technical procedures/specifications.
Ability to read and understand construction drawings and piece tickets.
High school diploma or equivalent
FULLTIME YEAR-ROUND WORK
Medical, Dental & Vision Benefit’s after 60 Days
Competitive Compensation & Profit Sharing
Available Over Time
401K with Match
Internal promotional opportunities
Company Sustainability – Since 1940
Enterprise Properties, Inc. and its subsidiaries are an equal opportunity employer that complies with EEOC rules and regulations. Enterprise Precast Concrete is part of the Enterprise Properties, Inc Family Pre-Employment Drug Screen, Criminal Background check, Reference check & Employment Verification are all part of our hiring process.
#EPCTX2021
ai_core_responsibilities: The carpenter works with the Carpenter Foreman to fabricate and set up forms for precast concrete products. They also perform miscellaneous carpentry work as directed by management.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have a minimum of 2 years of general carpentry experience and 2 years working with finish carpentry products. They should also be able to read and interpret technical specifications and construction drawings.
linkedin_org_locations: 10220 F STREET, Omaha, NE 68127, US
10220 F St, Omaha, NE 68127, US
linkedin_org_description:
Building a Family of Premier Precast Concrete Companies
Architectural | Structural | Utility

Enterprise Properties serves as the corporate foundation for a diverse family of industry-leading precast concrete production facilities. We specialize in providing high-quality architectural and structural precast products designed to meet a wide range of challenging applications.

Every day, our companies exceed the expectations of architects, engineers, developers, and commercial contractors by delivering reliable, innovative, and cost-effective precast concrete solutions.

Our goal is to be your comprehensive partner for precast components—from initial design and specifications, to production and delivery. Our products can be found in everything from high-rise buildings and parking structures to bridges and residential developments. We also proudly supply precast solutions for the utility, infrastructure, and railroad industries, along with top-tier precast stone for the masonry sector.

Established in 1940, Enterprise Properties is proud of its rich history and is poised to lead our family of companies well into the 21st century.

Ready to Deliver. Ready to Build.

At an Enterprise company, you’ll experience our commitment to getting your project off the ground—quickly and efficiently. We take pride in our responsiveness, ensuring we’re there when you need us to kick-start your project. Just tell us when, and we’ll make it happen!

title: Paid Advertising Lead (Remote - U.S.)
description_text:
FourFront’s paid advertising business has grown quickly—and so has the complexity of our client work.
We manage sophisticated, high-spend accounts across multiple platforms, support demanding clients who care deeply about outcomes, and operate in an environment where strategy, responsiveness, and clarity matter as much as execution.
We’re hiring a Paid Advertising Lead to take ownership of this function.
This is a senior, hands-on role for someone who can confidently lead paid advertising strategy, oversee execution across platforms, manage and hold freelancers accountable, and represent FourFront with credibility and calm in front of clients. You will run some accounts directly and serve as the strategic authority across others.
If you enjoy fixing messy systems, raising standards, and helping clients understand what’s working, what’s not, and what to do next, this role may be a great fit.
What You’ll Do
Own Paid Advertising Strategy & Performance
Lead paid advertising strategy across platforms including Google Ads, Microsoft/Bing Ads, Meta, YouTube, and Spotify
Build campaigns from scratch, take over and optimize existing accounts, and audit/fix inherited issues
Analyze performance data to identify insights, diagnose problems, and recommend strategic changes
Forecast budgets and expected outcomes; manage pacing and efficiency
Stay current on platform changes and emerging opportunities, including new advertising channels
Lead Client Communication & Reporting
Serve as the paid advertising expert on client calls
Lead reporting conversations that go beyond numbers to explain why performance changed and what comes next
Confidently answer client questions in real time, using data to support recommendations
Communicate clearly and concisely—without rambling, deflecting, or hiding behind metrics
Build trust with clients who are curious, sophisticated, and sometimes under pressure
Oversee Freelancers & Execution Quality
Manage and oversee freelance paid media specialists across platforms
Set clear expectations for responsiveness, quality, and deliverables
Review work, provide feedback, and hold freelancers accountable while maintaining productive relationships
Identify, vet, onboard, and manage new freelancers as needed
Ensure consistency in execution, reporting, and client experience across accounts
Collaborate Across Channels & Partners
Work closely with FourFront’s SEO teams to ensure paid advertising efforts align with broader digital strategy and business goals
Partner with internal teams and freelancers to ensure insights, reporting, and initiatives feel connected and not siloed
Use cross-channel data and insights to inform paid strategy and help clients understand how paid advertising supports and amplifies other marketing efforts
Contribute to a cohesive client experience where recommendations across channels reinforce one another
Improve Systems & Scale the Function
Help refine processes, templates, and standards for campaign management and reporting
Partner with Project Managers and Analytics to improve visibility into performance
Contribute to the evolution of FourFront’s paid advertising approach and differentiation
Support long-term growth of the paid advertising service, including potential future in-house expansion
What We’re Looking For
This role is best suited for someone who is fully baked—comfortable taking the lead immediately and operating with autonomy.
You likely bring:
7+ years of hands-on paid advertising experience, including significant agency experience
Deep experience managing campaigns in Google Ads and Meta, plus familiarity with other platforms
Experience overseeing large budgets (six- and seven-figure annual spend)
Strong understanding of conversion tracking and how platforms connect to GA4 and GTM (you don’t need to build everything yourself, but you understand how it works)
Proven experience leading client conversations and explaining performance under pressure
Experience managing freelancers, vendors, or junior practitioners
A strategic, analytical mindset paired with strong execution
The ability to work independently while collaborating closely with internal teams
How You Show Up
You care about client outcomes—not just dashboards
You’re confident without being aggressive
You can “read the room” and adjust your communication style
You hold people accountable without creating unnecessary friction
You’re decisive, thoughtful, and adaptable
You dislike rigid thinking and cookie-cutter strategies
Dealbreakers for this role include:
Regurgitating metrics without insight
Being overly long-winded or evasive in conversation
Struggling to explain performance clearly to non-specialists
Coming on too strong with clients or collaborators
About FourFront
FourFront is an award-winning digital marketing and data analytics company that transforms websites into powerful front-end marketing channels. We combine marketing expertise with deep analytics to help clients improve leads, increase sales, and align digital strategy with business goals.
We’re known for being smart, collaborative, and genuinely helpful—and we take pride in being experts who clients enjoy working with.
Work Environment & Compensation
Remote position, U.S.-based
Workday aligned to Eastern Time
Competitive salary, commensurate with experience 
Full benefits package, flexible work hours, generous PTO
Company-wide winter break to reset and recharge
How to Apply
Attention to detail matters here. As part of the application, you’ll be asked a few brief questions to help us understand your experience and approach. Complete and thoughtful answers are a positive sign.
FourFront is an equal opportunity employer.

title: Office Manager
description_text:
About Nation Security
Nation Security is a premier provider of professional guarding and protective services. We pride ourselves on being an agile, high-performance organization that values accountability, professional growth, and elite service. Unlike massive global conglomerates where employees are just numbers, we offer a dynamic environment where our Office Manager plays a critical role in our daily success and company culture.
The Role
We are seeking a high-energy Office Manager to serve as the operational "Command Center" for our Miami branch. This is a multi-faceted role that blends Office Administration, HR/Recruitment, and Logistics. You will be the first point of contact for our clients and the primary advocate for our security personnel.
Key Responsibilities
1. Recruitment & Talent Pipeline
Source and recruit high-quality security personnel through various job boards and community outreach.
Conduct initial phone screenings and in-person interviews to vet candidates for professionalism and required licensing.
Manage the applicant tracking process from initial contact to hire.
2. Onboarding & Compliance
Lead the orientation process for all new hires, ensuring they understand company protocols and site-specific duties.
Maintain 100% compliant employee files, ensuring all state-mandated security licenses and background checks are up to date.
Issue uniforms and equipment to new officers, ensuring they are field-ready on day one.
3. Front Desk & Reception
Greet visitors, clients, and applicants with a high level of professionalism.
Manage the main phone line and route inquiries to the appropriate departments (Sales, Operations, or Dispatch).
Maintain a clean, organized, and welcoming front office environment.
4. Procurement & Ordering
Monitor and restock office supplies and essential field gear.
Manage relationships with vendors and uniform suppliers to ensure timely delivery and cost-efficiency.
5. General Administration
Support the Operations Manager with administrative tasks related to scheduling and payroll verification.
Coordinate office meetings, employee appreciation initiatives, and safety briefings.
Qualifications
Experience: 3+ years in Office Management, HR, or Administrative Leadership (Security or Logistics industry experience is a major plus).
Licensing Knowledge: Familiarity with state security licensing requirements is preferred.
Tech Skills: Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Excel, Word, Outlook, Google Suite).
Communication: Exceptional verbal and written communication skills; bilingual (English/Spanish) is a MUST.
Aptitude: A "problem-solver" mindset with the ability to multitask in a fast-paced environment.
Why Join Nation Security
Opportunities for professional growth and advancement
Supportive, team-oriented work environment
Competitive pay and on-the-job training
If you’re dependable, bilingual in both English and Spanish, and ready to contribute to a professional and energetic workplace, we’d love to hear from you! Apply today!
ai_core_responsibilities: The Office Manager will oversee recruitment, onboarding, and compliance for security personnel while managing front desk operations and general administration tasks. This role is critical for maintaining the operational efficiency and culture of the Miami branch.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have 3+ years of experience in Office Management, HR, or Administrative Leadership, preferably in the security or logistics industry. Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite and exceptional communication skills, including bilingual capabilities in English and Spanish, are essential.

title: Maintenance Worker
description_text:
Since 1905, The Bradley Center has continually served children and families in need. Today, the residential treatment facility provides 24 hour care and trauma-informed treatment to children who have a history of trauma and who may present with a wide range of challenges. 
The Maintenance Worker will perform and need to be proficient in all general maintenance areas including:
Carpentry skills
Drywall installation and repair
HVAC
Plumbing
Electrical
Other duties as assigned
High school or equivalent preferred
Relevant experience is required
Shifts are 7am - 3pm, 3pm - 11pm, or 11pm-7am including some weekends and holidays
Medical, dental and vision coverage
$1500 Sign on Bonus
$2000 relocation assistance
Paid Time Off 
Accommodation of school schedules
Referral bonuses 
401(k)
Life insurance
Free parking and meals
We are committed to advocacy for children with special needs, care and compassion, and focusing on the inherent strengths of children, youth and families without regard to race, religion, sexual orientation, national origin, age, or gender. The Bradley Center is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All applicants will be considered for employment without attention to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, veteran or disability status.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Maintenance Worker will perform general maintenance tasks including carpentry, drywall installation and repair, HVAC, plumbing, and electrical work. Additional duties may be assigned as needed.
ai_requirements_summary: A high school diploma or equivalent is preferred, along with relevant experience in maintenance. Proficiency in various maintenance areas is required.
linkedin_org_locations: 5180 Campbells Run Raod, Pittsburgh, PA 15205, US
linkedin_org_description:
For over 100 years Bradley has been providing hope to children and families in need of comprehensive and caring services. 

Bradley was founded by The United Methodist Women in 1905 as The Elizabeth A. Bradley Home for Children and operated for many years as an orphanage and as interim housing for children whose parents were separated or divorced. In 1972, it was incorporated as The Bradley Center to serve abused, neglected and dependent children. 

In 1991, Bradley was reorganized by a new non-denominational, community-based Board of Trustees and executive management. 

The Bradley Center has since evolved into an accredited, regional behavioral healthcare and child welfare system that provides hope to young girls and boys. 

Bradley is committed to advocacy for children and dedicated to the restoration of productive relationships among children, their families and the community whenever possible. 

The Bradley Center implements the Sanctuary Model of Trauma Informed Care developed by Dr. Sandra Bloom. Sanctuary is a treatment and organizational model that is based on understanding trauma. Every staff and resident in care focus on "SELF"​ - Safety (physical, psychological, social and moral), Emotion management, Loss (traumatic experiences and separation), and the Future (how can things get better?). 

Bradley has earned the quality distinction of accreditation by the Joint Commission on Accreditation of Healthcare Organizations. It is also licensed by the Pennsylvania Departments of Education and Welfare. 

Bradley's Vision is to be recognized nationally by families, communities and professionals as a premier behavioral healthcare, education and child welfare organization that promotes the physical, social, emotional, educational and spiritual growth of the children, youth and families that it serves.

title: AI Enablement Consultant
locations_alt_raw: New York, New York, United States
locations_derived: New York, New York, United States
description_text:
The AI Enablement Consultant helps clients successfully adopt and use Vivaldi’s AI platform. This role sits between technology, strategy consulting, and training. You will configure AI agents, guide clients through onboarding, help structure their data, and train teams to work effectively with AI in their daily workflows.
This is a hands-on role for someone excited about AI, learning systems, and helping people change how they work. You must be able to work out of our office in Flatiron, NYC at least 3 days a week. 
Key Responsibilities
Lead client onboarding for the AI platform
Configure and customize AI agents for client needs
Guide clients through data setup (projects, people, time, expenses)
Train client teams on how to use agents effectively
Translate client workflows into agent skills and tools
Troubleshoot early adoption issues and usage gaps
Partner with product and engineering to surface improvement needs
Act as the day-to-day AI guide for clients during transition
What This Is Not
Not just a tech strategy role
Not a pure technical engineering role
Not traditional customer support
Ideal Background
2–4 years experience in consulting, ops, customer success, or tech-enabled services
Strong communicator, comfortable leading client sessions
Curious about AI and fast learner with new tools
Organized, structured, and detail-oriented
Excited to teach, configure, and improve systems
Comfortable being “on the field” with clients
Nice to Have
Experience with SaaS onboarding or implementations
Exposure to AI tools, automation, or analytics
Interest in product thinking
The base salary range for this position is $85,000 - $105,000 per year, which is determined by a number of factors including prior experience, education, skills, and geographic location. In addition to our base compensation, we offer a comprehensive benefits package including performance based bonuses to full-time employees.
Why Vivaldi
We offer you a learning environment to grow and build your career. You will work with our international project teams to help businesses, brands and people find untapped potential and smart solutions to create sustainable future growth. We cultivate a space for you to grow beyond yourself – carving out new learning opportunities you uncover and owning personal initiatives.
Does that sound like a growing opportunity to you? Become a Vivaldi family member.

As we continue to grow, we’re looking for a Business Development Representative (BDR) who’s excited to break into sales and build a long-term career. This is a great opportunity for someone who wants to learn sales from the ground up while earning uncapped commission—no closing experience required.

title: Delivery Manager - GxP Software Services
description_text:
ETHICS CODE is looking for a qualified Delivery Manager – GxP Software Services to join our team of experts. As the Delivery Manager, you will oversee the delivery of software services in compliance with Good Practice (GxP) guidelines, ensuring that our solutions meet the highest quality and regulatory standards within the pharmaceutical and life sciences industries. You will lead cross-functional teams through all project phases, from planning through execution, ensuring customer satisfaction and project success.
Key Responsibilities
Oversee the complete lifecycle of GxP software service delivery projects, ensuring adherence to quality and regulatory standards.
Coordinate with internal teams, including engineering, product management, and regulatory compliance, to deliver projects on time and within budget.
Develop and maintain project plans, including scope, schedule, resource allocation, and risk management strategies.
Serve as the primary point of contact for clients, maintaining strong relationships and effectively communicating project status and outcomes.
Facilitate project meetings, documentation, and reporting, ensuring all stakeholders are kept informed of progress and challenges.
Identify and resolve project challenges, promptly addressing any issues that arise to keep projects on track.
Mentor and guide team members, promoting best practices in project management and GxP compliance.
Qualifications
Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Life Sciences, or a related field.
5+ years of experience in project management, with a focus on software delivery in regulated environments, preferably in the life sciences or pharmaceutical industry.
Strong knowledge of GxP regulations and related industry standards (e.g., FDA, EMA, ISO).
Proven track record of successfully managing software delivery projects, ensuring compliance and quality.
Experience with project management methodologies, such as Agile and Waterfall.
Excellent leadership skills, with the ability to motivate and engage cross-functional teams.
Exceptional communication skills, both written and verbal, with experience presenting to senior stakeholders.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with a focus on delivering results.
ai_core_responsibilities: Oversee the complete lifecycle of GxP software service delivery projects, ensuring adherence to quality and regulatory standards. Coordinate with internal teams to deliver projects on time and within budget while maintaining strong client relationships.
ai_requirements_summary: A bachelor's degree in a relevant field and 5+ years of experience in project management, particularly in regulated environments, are required. Strong knowledge of GxP regulations and proven success in managing software delivery projects are essential.

title: Account Director at email marketing agency
description_text:
Account Director (Retention Marketing – Klaviyo Expert)
Remote | Full-Time | Competitive Pay + Bonus
Redwood is a retention-first email agency partnering with fast-moving 8 - 9 figure DTC brands. We’re looking for a senior Account Director to lead customer lifecycle strategy across multiple client accounts.
This is a strategic leadership role. You should have experience working with large brands, know what great lifecycle marketing looks like, and be able to guide both planning and execution from end to end, on enterprise level builds.
What You’ll Do
Own retention strategy a range of high-growth DTC brands
Strategize, build and optimize full lifecycle systems
Lead client strategy calls and oversee all retention performance
Guide internal teams on execution and campaign production
Spot gaps, bring ideas, and push the work forward
What We’re Looking For
5+ years in retention or lifecycle marketing
Real experience with larger or complex brands
Deep Klaviyo expertise, beyond the standard sets of plug-and-play flows
Experience with enterprise retention and marketing automations
Strong strategic thinking and client leadership skills
Bonus If:
You’ve worked in an agency or across multiple accounts
Why Redwood
Smart team, strong clients, no fluff
Real ownership, fast pace, full support
Remote Friendly
Real room to grow, on a fast timeline
Who This Is Not For
This isn’t the right role if your experience is limited to:
Running retention for small brands only (<$1m a month ecom)
Installing standard pre-built flows without deeper structural or strategic thinking
Knowing your way around Klaviyo, but not being able to go toe-to-toe on details with experts in the space
Working on a few accounts with light involvement, without full ownership
We’re looking for someone who has led retention programs from the inside—who's been in the room, shaped the plan, and seen it through.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Account Director will own the retention strategy for a range of high-growth DTC brands and lead client strategy calls while overseeing retention performance. They will guide internal teams on execution and campaign production, identifying gaps and pushing the work forward.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have 5+ years of experience in retention or lifecycle marketing, with real experience working with larger or complex brands. Deep expertise in Klaviyo and strong strategic thinking and client leadership skills are essential.
