title: Translator - Elections
description_text:
About Prisma International, Inc. - Prisma is a dynamic and growing language services provider committed to delivering exceptional translation solutions to clients worldwide. We pride ourselves on our dedication to linguistic excellence and client satisfaction. 
We are seeking experienced, professional Translators to join our exclusive ELECTIONS team. In this independent contractor role, you will provide high quality written translations between English and your native language. Successful applicants will have past Elections experience, exceptional time management skills and a commitment to linguistic excellence.
NATIVE LANGUAGES: HINDI, KOREAN, TAGALOG & THAI
If your native language is not listed above, you are welcome to apply. However, you must meet all minimum requirements to be considered.
MINIMUM REQUIREMENTS - You must meet ALL of the following requirements:
Reside in the United States
18+ years of age
Five (5) years of professional experience providing written translations to local, state or federal U.S. government offices as a contractor, sub-contractor, employee, freelancer or volunteer. Two (2) of the five (5) years of experience must have been acquired within the last five years. Note: The resume you submit must clearly outline your five years of government-related translation experience and include dates. Elections experience is a plus. 
Availability to support Prisma after business hours (evenings) and on weekends and holidays during an Election cycle.
Willing to abide by Prisma's Master Service Agreement (MSA) and related Addenda.
High proficiency in English and a native speaker of the target language(s).
Reliable desktop PC or laptop with dedicated, secure, high-speed internet connection.
Experience using modern CAT tools (e.g. XTM, Trados, etc.).
Adobe Acrobat on your device and the ability to use Adobe's markup tools on a PDF file.
***At least ONE (1) of the following credentials*** You will be asked to provide proof.
Current voting member in good standing of the American Translators Association (ATA).
Current member in good standing of the American Association of Language Specialists
Vietnamese translators - certified through the Society of Translators and Interpreters of British Columbia
Certified and registered on the California Judicial Council Master List
Categorized as “Certified” or “Professionally Qualified” by the Administrative Office of the United States Courts
Accreditation by a regional or national accrediting agency recognized by the United States Secretary of Education
Accreditation by the Western Association of Schools and Colleges
If you do not have at least one of the above-named certifications / accreditations, we may consider applicants with ALL of the following:
Native speaker of the target language.
Educated in both English and the other language. Your resume must outline your educational background in a language-related field of study.
5+ years of professional experience translating for local, state or federal government offices as a contractor or sub-contractor. Two (2) of the five (5) years of experience must have been acquired within the last five years. 
TASKS & RESPONSIBILITIES:
Provide written translations using a Computer-Assisted Translation (CAT) tool. We provide XTM to you.
Adhere to all project work instructions, glossaries and reference materials.
Ensure the source content's concept, style and register are culturally and linguistically appropriate for the target language.
Perform language quality check steps (before delivery) to ensure the target translation is error-free and true to the meaning of the source document.
Deliver completed translation jobs by the given deadlines and project budgets.
Review formatted target language PDF files for accuracy; concisely mark any corrections needed. Using Adobe mark-up tools is required.
Communicate in a timely and professional manner with Prisma team members.
Participate in quality management processes, e.g. Corrective Action Reports (CARs), Root Cause Analyses (RCAs), process improvement and solution implementation.
Take all measures necessary to protect and keep private sensitive information shared with you to carry out translation projects (e.g. PHI, PII, SBU).
Adhere to policies and processes defined in Prisma's Master Service Agreement (MSA) and the Prisma Vendor Guide.
OTHER ATTRIBUTES:
Familiarity with voting materials: voter information guides, ballot measures, flyers, etc.
Excellent communication skills and quick responses to Prisma requests.
Ability to accommodate fast turnarounds for last-minute urgent corrections.
High attention to detail - essential for proofreading and verifying names, numbers, dates, etc. and identifying font corruption in a non-English formatted document (PDF).
Integrity and impartiality for handling sensitive content.
Awareness to understand cultural differences; ensure translated materials are appropriate and clear to specific communities.
Advanced ability to translate complex or specialized jargon into clear, concise language.
Ability to ask clarifying questions about technical or unclear content to ensure accuracy.
See full list of minimum requirements above.
ai_core_responsibilities: Provide high-quality written translations between English and the target language using CAT tools. Ensure accuracy and cultural appropriateness of translations while adhering to project deadlines.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have at least five years of professional translation experience, including two years within the last five years, and must reside in the United States. High proficiency in English and a native speaker of the target language are required, along with specific credentials or educational background.

If you do not have at least one of the above-named certifications / accreditations, we may consider applicants with ALL of the following:
Native speaker of the target language.
Educated in both English and the other language. Your resume must outline your educational background in a language-related field of study.
5+ years of professional experience translating for local, state or federal government offices as a contractor or sub-contractor. Two (2) of the five (5) years of experience must have been acquired within the last five years. 
TASKS & RESPONSIBILITIES:
Provide written translations using a Computer-Assisted Translation (CAT) tool. We provide XTM to you.
Adhere to all project work instructions, glossaries and reference materials.
Ensure the source content's concept, style and register are culturally and linguistically appropriate for the target language.
Perform language quality check steps (before delivery) to ensure the target translation is error-free and true to the meaning of the source document.
Deliver completed translation jobs by the given deadlines and project budgets.
Review formatted target language PDF files for accuracy; concisely mark any corrections needed. Using Adobe mark-up tools is required.
Communicate in a timely and professional manner with Prisma team members.
Participate in quality management processes, e.g. Corrective Action Reports (CARs), Root Cause Analyses (RCAs), process improvement and solution implementation.
Take all measures necessary to protect and keep private sensitive information shared with you to carry out translation projects (e.g. PHI, PII, SBU).
Adhere to policies and processes defined in Prisma's Master Service Agreement (MSA) and the Prisma Vendor Guide.
OTHER ATTRIBUTES:
Familiarity with voting materials: voter information guides, ballot measures, flyers, etc.
Excellent communication skills and quick responses to Prisma requests.
Ability to accommodate fast turnarounds for last-minute urgent corrections.
High attention to detail - essential for proofreading and verifying names, numbers, dates, etc. and identifying font corruption in a non-English formatted document (PDF).
Integrity and impartiality for handling sensitive content.
Awareness to understand cultural differences; ensure translated materials are appropriate and clear to specific communities.
Advanced ability to translate complex or specialized jargon into clear, concise language.
Ability to ask clarifying questions about technical or unclear content to ensure accuracy.
See full list of minimum requirements above.
ai_core_responsibilities: Provide high-quality written translations between English and the target language using CAT tools. Ensure translations are culturally and linguistically appropriate, error-free, and delivered by deadlines.
ai_requirements_summary: Applicants must reside in the U.S., be at least 18 years old, and have five years of professional translation experience, including two years within the last five years. They must also demonstrate high proficiency in English and their native language, and have relevant certifications or educational background.

title: Sales Representative
description_text:
Teltonika aspires to become one of the global leaders in providing unique IoT solutions that contribute to making people’s lives easier. Since 1998, we have gained considerable experience and competence in electronics development and production. Today, our areas of expertise include telematics, networking, telemedicine, EV charging, and electronics manufacturing. While proactively searching for new ideas and ways to create more value for our clients, we also hope to create a better future for our employees and the communities we operate in. Our organizational culture is based on mutual trust and respect as well as creating synergy with our employees and clients.
Currently, Teltonika US is looking for an ambitious Regional IoT Sales Specialist to join our office in Dallas, Texas. In this position, you will belong to Networks Sales team. Teltonika’s networking solutions business unit develops and manufactures industrial and enterprise connectivity equipment, primarily routers, switches, gateways, and modems. Our networking devices serve clients in the most complex areas of Industry 4.0, Smart City, and Green Energy.
If you are well-organized and are motivated in prospecting and developing long-term relationships with B2B clients, see yourself in a start-up environment (that has strong support from HQ) which requires proactiveness, then we are on the same way!
In this role, you will:
Contribute to market research, generate leads and build relationships by organizing daily work schedule to call to potential clients
Reach out to new potential clients through daily cold calling, email, and LinkedIn communication
Arrange presentation meetings with potential clients and present our solutions
Prepare commercial offers and ensure contracts to achieve assigned sales goals
Build and maintain long term relationships with new and existing clients through daily contact and frequent visits
Attend exhibitions/meetings to represent Teltonika and its products with solutions
Work with an in-house built CRM System to log-in accounts, leads, contacts, and opportunities according to best practices
Have 2+ years of B2B sales experience in the North American market
Are result-oriented and challenge driven
Are highly self-motivated, with excellent communication, presentation and organizational skills
Have good understanding of customer relations and huge motivation to work in B2B sales
Have the ambition to generate strong earnings by expanding local B2B opportunities with an innovative product from Lithuania
Are able to travel
Work type: Hybrid (at least 3 days from office); Office location: 5000 Riverside Dr., Irving, TX, 75039
Training: onboarding on Teltonika‘s internal systems, Sales process, product related topics
Career: opportunities to increase your seniority level in the role, or become a Team Lead in the long-term
Benefits: Company funded health insurance; 401k; 3 weeks of Paid Vacation; 6 Sick days + day off on your Birthday
Work Environment: Multicultural and diverse environment which highly values knowledge and exchange of ideas, supportive and positive attitude in the team
Salary:
Competitive base salary depending on experience + uncapped commissions. Real potential to develop career opportunities in IoT industry, incentives + referral bonuses.
*Only qualified candidates will be considered and contacted for the first round of interviews.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Sales Representative will contribute to market research, generate leads, and build relationships with potential clients. They will also prepare commercial offers, ensure contracts, and maintain long-term relationships with clients.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have 2+ years of B2B sales experience in the North American market and possess excellent communication and organizational skills. A strong motivation to work in B2B sales and the ability to travel are also required.

linkedin_org_description: Teltonika Networks is a rapidly growing technology company, manufacturing professional network connectivity equipment for international markets. Through long-term experience and research and development of industrial network devices for IoT and M2M communication, we have developed a wide portfolio of products for the most complex areas of Industry 4.0, Smart City, and Green Energy.

title: Sales Analyst
description_text:
veritree and Job overview
veritree is an award-winning climate tech start-up based in Vancouver. Launched in 2021, our technology measures and verifies the impact of global restoration efforts from the ground up. We are on a big mission to plant 1 billion verified trees by 2030 with our community of businesses, planting organizations, and consumers who believe in the transformative power of verified restoration projects to create real and meaningful impact for the planet, nature, and people.
We’re hiring a Sales Analyst to build the intelligence engine behind veritree’s outbound and partner expansion efforts across both Enterprise and Commercial accounts.
This is a highly data- and AI-driven role. You’ll work with large datasets, enrich and structure information at scale, and produce prioritized account lists, opportunity sizing models, and partner expansion insights that directly drive revenue. You’ll also set up monitoring/alerts for new tree planting commitments so our team can move quickly as the market evolves.
This role is internal-facing and execution-focused: your work will show up in the CRM, dashboards, target lists, enrichment fields, and weekly prioritization inputs for Sales.
6–12 Month Outcomes (6-12kMOs) 
Identify, qualify, and maintain a market intelligence dataset of at least 1,500 net-new companies engaging in environmental and alternative give-back initiatives enabling Sales to prioritize companies with high-intent 
Implement AI-driven enrichment and intent-based outbound automation across email and LinkedIn that surfaces relevant company signals and automatically populates CRM-ready fields used in outbound sequences.
Identify and size expansion opportunities within existing partners, including alternative give-back initiatives, foundation work, carbon programs, new departments, and geographies, and deliver prioritized expansion insights to Sales.
Enable measurable improvements in Sales performance and pipeline quality, contributing to:
a 20% increase in meetings booked, and
a 10% increase in meetings progressing into the deal pipeline,
through improved targeting, timing, personalization, and trust driven messaging.

What You’ll Do:
Build and maintain structured market datasets and keep them current through monitoring and alerts.
Pull, clean, and analyze large datasets to enrich accounts with program details and estimate annual opportunity size.
Use AI workflows (ChatGPT/Gemini/Perplexity/n8n + enrichment tools) to accelerate research, extract structured insights, and create HubSpot/CRM-ready fields at scale.
Segment and prioritize account lists for Enterprise vs Commercial motions and deliver prioritized targets to Sales
Identify expansion opportunities within existing partners (new departments, geos, carbon programs, foundation giving, external impact initiatives).
Surface trends from existing partners and market signals to inform who to target and which business use cases to lead with, especially for companies new to restoration/giveback programs.
Requirements
3+ years in sales analytics, market intelligence, revenue operations, research, or GTM strategy roles.
Advanced Excel / Google Sheets (large datasets, pivots, models, structured outputs).
Strong AI fluency — able to use tools like ChatGPT, Gemini, and/or Perplexity to accelerate research, enrichment, classification, and analysis.
Experience with enrichment/prospecting tools such as Clay, Unify, Apollo, or similar platforms.
Proven ability to turn messy information into clean datasets, prioritized target lists, and clear next actions for Sales.
High ownership, strong attention to detail, and comfort building systems from scratch.
Preferred
CRM experience (HubSpot or equivalent) and strong data hygiene habits.
Experience supporting outbound motions across Enterprise and Commercial segments.
Familiarity with corporate impact, sustainability, or giving programs.
Benefits & Compensation Overview
At veritree, we recognize that compensation and performance development are key to attracting and retaining top talent. Our approach is grounded in fairness, transparency, and creating opportunities for growth as both our people and business scale.
Some highlights you can expect include:
Competitive salary with a performance-driven framework that ensures fair and consistent compensation reviews, tied to individual impact and business growth
Extended health, dental, and vision benefits
Additional Health Spending Account (HSA) / Lifestyle Spending Account (LSA) to support your well-being and lifestyle interests
A variety of time-off programs, including vacation, personal days, and a 4-week remote work program each year
Employee discount with tentree, our sister company
A flexible, hybrid work environment designed for collaboration and focus-driven impact
veritree ensures that compensation is reviewed fairly and consistently, with opportunities for salary progression tied to sustained performance. As such, the salary range for this role is $65,000 to $85,000 CAD. 

This range is positioned around the 50th-75th percentile of market data we’ve collected, reflecting our commitment to competitive pay. Final offer amounts are based on individual experience and skillset of the candidate demonstrated during the recruitment process.
We reserve the >75th percentile of our salary ranges for internal employees who demonstrate sustained high performance and impact at veritree.
veritree is an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to building a team that represents diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills. All employment decisions are made on the basis of qualifications, merit, and business needs.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Sales Analyst will build and maintain structured market datasets and analyze large datasets to enrich accounts. They will also identify expansion opportunities within existing partners and deliver prioritized insights to the Sales team.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have 3+ years of experience in sales analytics or related fields and possess advanced skills in Excel and AI tools. Strong data management and organizational skills are essential for this role.

Our technology provides transparency throughout the lifecycle of restorative projects, matches these projects with mission aligned corporate partners, and ensures the projects’ ongoing impacts are authentic and reported. Our platform provides these corporate partners with unique digital experiences for their customers to showcase the size and scope of their global restorative impact. Our solutions are designed to harness the power of businesses and individuals to scale the restorative economy.

title: US Tax and Compliance Specialist for US Agency (Remote)
description_text:
Paired is a global staffing and recruiting agency that specializes in pairing remote work with top-tier talent!
We are dedicated to connecting talented individuals with remarkable companies in need of their skills. Our goal is to create fulfilling job opportunities for professionals around the world.

Our client is an advisory firm built on a family-office model, providing integrated tax, tax planning, financial planning, and investment planning services. Their clients include high-net-worth (HNW) and ultra-high-net-worth (UHNW) individuals, business owners, professional athletes, coaches, entertainers, real estate investors, and high W-2 earners.
As they grow into a regional and national presence, we are seeking experienced tax professionals who thrive in a consultative environment and enjoy building long-term client relationships while delivering proactive, strategic tax solutions.

The Tax Manager / Senior Tax Professional will play a critical role in managing complex tax engagements, advising high-profile clients, and driving proactive tax planning strategies.
This individual will serve as both a technical expert and a strategic partner to clients, with exposure to individual, business, nonprofit, and partnership taxation.
The ideal candidate is highly skilled in compliance and planning, comfortable working with sophisticated clients, and capable of analyzing financial data to uncover meaningful tax-saving opportunities.
Key Responsibilities
Tax Compliance & Review
Prepare and review complex federal and state tax returns, including:
Individual returns (Form 1040)
C-Corporations (Form 1120)
S-Corporations (Form 1120-S)
Partnerships (Form 1065)
Nonprofits (Form 990)
Ensure accuracy, completeness, and timely filing of all returns
Manage multi-entity structures and consolidated tax considerations
Tax Planning & Strategy
Proactively identify tax planning opportunities for HNW and UHNW clients
Develop and implement tax strategies related to:
Business ownership and entity structuring
Real estate investments and cost segregation
Compensation planning and high W-2 income strategies
Estimated tax payments and cash flow planning
Analyze client accounts, financial statements, and investment activity to support tax-efficient decision-making
Collaborate with internal advisors on integrated tax, financial, and investment strategies
Client Advisory & Relationship Management
Serve as a trusted advisor to business owners and high-profile individuals
Communicate complex tax concepts clearly and eƯectively
Participate in client meetings and strategic planning discussions
Provide white-glove service consistent with a family-oƯice experience
Accounting & Financial Reporting (Business Clients)
Oversee or assist with monthly accounting and bookkeeping engagements
Prepare and review financial statements for business clients
Use financial data to support tax planning, projections, and estimated payments
Coordinate audit and tax insights to improve accuracy and efficiency
Ideal Candidate Profile
Technical Expertise
USA CPA tax or Business management Experience
Strong background in both tax compliance and tax planning/strategy
Experience working with HNW and UHNW individuals and closely held businesses
Proficiency in individual, partnership, corporate, and nonprofit taxation
Audit experience is a strong plus, particularly for business and multi-entity clients
Professional Experience
5+ years of public accounting or family office experience preferred but not required
Experience managing complex client engagements independently
Prior exposure to athletes, entertainers, real estate professionals, or high-profile individuals is highly desirable
Skills & Attributes
Highly analytical with strong attention to detail
Proactive, strategic thinker who anticipates client needs
Comfortable working directly with sophisticated clients
Able to manage multiple deadlines in a fast-paced, growing environment
Strong written and verbal communication skills
Fully remote, flexible work environment
High autonomy and ownership over your work
Stable, full-time contractor role
Compensarion flexible for top talent
Long-term opportunity with a growing client base

This role includes preparing and reviewing tax returns, identifying tax planning opportunities, and serving as a trusted advisor to clients.

Experience with high-net-worth individuals and the ability to manage complex client engagements independently is preferred.

title: Licensed Mental Health Therapist - Outpatient Services - Full Time
description_text:
The Bradley Center is excited to announce that our Outpatient Program is growing. 
We provide therapy as well as psychiatric and med management services to children, adults, and families. School-Based Mental Health Services are provided to students in several school districts in Allegheny and surrounding counties. 
We are looking for confident licensed clinicians who have the ability to assess, plan, and provide compassionate therapy to those we serve. Therapists manage their caseloads and function independently, while also collaborating with others in a team-based approach to treatment. All activities are in accordance with outpatient regulations, The Joint Commission standards, and trauma-informed treatment. 
A valid PA counseling license, inclusive of LCSW, LMFT, LPC, or psychologist
Master’s degree in a generally recognized clinical discipline, with a degree program that includes clinical practicum
NCC or LSW preferred
Experience diagnosing and providing therapy is preferred
Must be at least 21 years of age
Valid PA driver’s license or ability to obtain such license, with proof of auto insurance coverage
Qualifications best suited for the position:
Strong interpersonal skills
Excellent verbal and written communication and listening skills
Ability to communicate with compassion and empathy
Ability to build rapport quickly
Effective listening, interpersonal, critical thinking, and problem-solving skills. 
Professional demeanor, sound judgment, dependability, and a strong work ethic
Detail oriented with a strong ability to manage time and prioritize tasks
Initiative
Primary Responsibilities:
Establish a positive, trusting rapport with clients
Diagnose and treat mental health disorders
Create individualized treatment plans according to client needs
Conduct ongoing assessments of client progress
Maintain thorough records and completes documentation in a timely manner
Student loan repayment program (We contribute directly to employee's student loan lenders monthly)
$1500 sign on bonus
$2000 relocation assistance
Referral bonuses
Generous Paid Time Off in the first year of employment
Health, dental and vision Coverage
401(k)
Life insurance
Advanced training opportunities and continuing education credits
We are committed to advocacy for children with special needs, care and compassion, and focus on the inherent strengths of children, youth and families without regard to race, religion, sexual orientation, national origin, age, or gender. The Bradley Center is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All applicants will be considered for employment without attention to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, veteran or disability status.
ai_core_responsibilities: Establish a positive, trusting rapport with clients and diagnose and treat mental health disorders. Create individualized treatment plans according to client needs and conduct ongoing assessments of client progress.
ai_requirements_summary: A valid PA counseling license is required, along with a Master's degree in a recognized clinical discipline. Experience diagnosing and providing therapy is preferred, and candidates must be at least 21 years of age.
linkedin_org_locations: 5180 Campbells Run Raod, Pittsburgh, PA 15205, US
linkedin_org_description:
For over 100 years Bradley has been providing hope to children and families in need of comprehensive and caring services. 

Bradley was founded by The United Methodist Women in 1905 as The Elizabeth A. Bradley Home for Children and operated for many years as an orphanage and as interim housing for children whose parents were separated or divorced. In 1972, it was incorporated as The Bradley Center to serve abused, neglected and dependent children. 

In 1991, Bradley was reorganized by a new non-denominational, community-based Board of Trustees and executive management. 

The Bradley Center has since evolved into an accredited, regional behavioral healthcare and child welfare system that provides hope to young girls and boys. 

Bradley is committed to advocacy for children and dedicated to the restoration of productive relationships among children, their families and the community whenever possible. 

The Bradley Center implements the Sanctuary Model of Trauma Informed Care developed by Dr. Sandra Bloom. Sanctuary is a treatment and organizational model that is based on understanding trauma. Every staff and resident in care focus on "SELF"​ - Safety (physical, psychological, social and moral), Emotion management, Loss (traumatic experiences and separation), and the Future (how can things get better?). 

Bradley has earned the quality distinction of accreditation by the Joint Commission on Accreditation of Healthcare Organizations. It is also licensed by the Pennsylvania Departments of Education and Welfare. 

Bradley's Vision is to be recognized nationally by families, communities and professionals as a premier behavioral healthcare, education and child welfare organization that promotes the physical, social, emotional, educational and spiritual growth of the children, youth and families that it serves.

title: Content & Social Executive
description_text:
Pass the Keys is the UK’s leading short-let and Airbnb management franchise, helping hundreds of property owners unlock more bookings, higher income, and hands-free hosting. With 50+ franchise partners nationwide, we combine powerful technology, exceptional service, and local expertise to deliver the best in short-let management.
We’re looking for a highly driven Content Executive to help plan, create, publish and optimise high-quality content across our website, email, video and social channels.
This is a hands-on role suited to someone who loves storytelling, understands performance, and is excited by how AI and new tools are reshaping content marketing. You’ll work closely with internal subject-matter experts and own the execution of our content engine end-to-end.
Content Creation & Publishing
Publish 3+ high-quality pieces of content per week across blog, web and owned channels 
Interview internal subject-matter experts and translate insights into compelling, audience-led content 
Produce premium content assets including whitepapers, guides, ebooks and long-form resources 
Write, edit and optimise content to a consistently high editorial standard 
Video Content
Produce two videos per week, including short-form and long-form formats 
Write video scripts and storyboards aligned to brand tone and channel goals 
Ensure all video content is optimised for YouTube and social platforms 
Track and report on video performance, engagement and reach 
SEO, Analytics & Performance
Oversee SEO execution in line with best practices (on-page, content optimisation, internal linking) 
Take ownership of content analytics and reporting, tracking performance and insights across channels 
Use data to continuously improve content quality, reach and engagement 
Email Marketing
Manage company email marketing efforts, including content creation, scheduling and optimisation 
Support campaign planning and performance tracking 
Social Media & Community
Manage social media community engagement and long-term content planning 
Support content distribution and amplification across social channels 
Maintain a consistent brand voice across all platforms 
Website & Tools
Handle general website content updates and maintenance 
Leverage AI tools to support content ideation, production, optimisation and efficiency 
Contribute to improving workflows, processes and content systems
Essential
Impeccable writing and editorial skills 
Strong understanding of common editorial and style guides 
Ability to tell compelling stories using words, images and audio 
Excellent people skills and confidence interviewing internal stakeholders 
Strong project management skills to manage editorial schedules and deadlines 
Highly detail-oriented with strong organisational skills 
Solid understanding of social media platforms and content formats 
Understanding of the importance of tracking content and video marketing metrics 
Strong grasp of branding fundamentals and consistency
Desirable
Passion for new technology and experimenting with AI tools for content and social media outreach 
Familiarity with SEO best practices and performance reporting 
Experience working with CMS platforms, email tools and analytics software
At Pass the Keys, we are committed to fostering an environment where success is both achievable and sustainable. We believe in empowering our teams with the tools, support, and knowledge they need to thrive. As part of our team, you will benefit from:
A Global Vision: Join an ambitious company aspiring to be the global leader in short-let and holiday-let property management.
A Strong Company Culture: Our four core values—Drive, Evolve, Take Pride, and Together—guide everything we do.
Autonomy & Independence: We manage by results, not input. No micromanagement—just accountability and impact.
Professional Growth Opportunities: We encourage responsibility and development, allowing you to expand your skills as much as you can handle.
Flexible Work Arrangements: We understand the importance of work-life balance and support flexible scheduling where possible.
Competitive Salary: While there may be places that pay more, we offer competitive wages with strong career progression opportunities.
A Great Team: Work alongside inspiring colleagues in a supportive and collaborative environment.
Hybrid Work Model: For London-based employees, we require a minimum of three days in the office—currently Monday, Tuesday, and Thursday. Please note, occasional weekend work may be required to meet operational demand. 
UK-Specific Benefits:
22 days of holiday plus an extra day off for your birthday.
Pension scheme with salary sacrifice, matching contributions up to 7%.
Enhanced maternity pay—up to 90% of salary for six months.

title: Founding Sales Representative
locations_derived: New York, New York, United States
description_text:
About Rezora
Rezora IO is an AI voice platform built specifically for real estate professionals. We automatically call leads, qualify intent, and book meetings directly into agents’ calendars. Our customers are real estate agents, teams, commercial buyers, and property investors who rely on speed and follow up to win deals.
We are early stage and founder-led. This is our first sales hire, and you will work closely with the founding team to help build our sales pipeline, systems, and early revenue engine.
Role overview
We are hiring a full time founding sales representative to grow Rezora’s sales pipeline through high-volume cold calling to real estate professionals. This role is focused on starting conversations, qualifying interest, and closing new subscriptions.
Product demos will be handled by the founding team. Your primary responsibility will be prospecting, qualification, and closing. You will play a key role not just in selling, but in helping us build and improve the sales systems and processes as we scale.
This is a commission only role with meaningful long term upside.
Responsibilities
Cold call real estate agents, commercial buyers, and brokerages using provided lead lists
Qualify prospects and handle objections using your real estate sales experience
Close new subscriptions and earn recurring commission on active customers
Work closely with the founders to refine scripts, messaging, and targeting
Identify gaps in the sales process and help build scalable systems and workflows
Provide ongoing feedback from the market to inform product and go-to-market decisions
Compensation
Commission only, full-time role
Earn 25 percent recurring commission on the monthly subscription revenue for each customer you close
Commission continues for as long as the customer remains active

title: NewForce Entry Level Data Analyst (2026)
description_text:
About Inspira Analytics
Inspira Analytics is a data consulting firm based in Charleston, WV, helping small to mid-sized businesses unlock the power of their data. From basic reporting to advanced visualization and forecasting, we make complex information simple and strategic. We serve a wide range of industries including healthcare, logistics, and retail—providing clean, clear, and customized analytics that meet our clients where they are. We are also passionate about workforce development in Appalachia. Our team includes local talent trained in modern analytics tools and techniques, and we invest heavily in early-career professionals through mentorship and hands-on learning.
Mission: To empower organizations with accessible, accurate, and actionable data insights that drive smarter decisions and sustainable growth. 
Vision: To be the most trusted partner in transforming raw data into meaningful intelligence—one insight at a time. 
What we do:
Custom dashboards and reporting (Tableau, Power BI)
SQL and Python data pipelines
Data cleaning and integration
Data analytics training and upskilling
Job Summary
We are seeking a highly motivated and detail-oriented Junior Data Analyst to join our data-driven team. As a Junior Data Analyst, you will play a crucial role in supporting data-related activities within our organization. You will work closely with senior data analysts and other team members to collect, analyze, and present data insights that inform decision-making and drive business growth. This is an excellent opportunity for a recent graduate or entry-level professional to gain valuable experience in the field of data analysis.
Junior Data Analyst Duties and Responsibilities
Data Collection and Cleaning: Collect and compile data from various sources, ensuring data accuracy and completeness. Perform data cleaning and transformation as necessary to prepare it for analysis.
Data Analysis: Assist in conducting data analysis using statistical methods, data visualization tools, and data analysis software. Summarize findings in clear and concise reports.
Data Visualization: Create visually appealing and informative charts, graphs, and dashboards to present data insights to stakeholders. Use tools such as Tableau, Power BI, or Excel for visualization.
Report Generation: Prepare regular and ad-hoc reports to communicate key metrics and findings to the team and management. Ensure reports are accurate and delivered on time.
Data Quality Assurance: Help maintain data quality standards by identifying and resolving data discrepancies or inconsistencies. Collaborate with other team members to establish data quality protocols.
Collaboration: Work closely with cross-functional teams, including data engineers, business analysts, and stakeholders, to understand data requirements and deliver actionable insights.
Research and Learning: Stay up-to-date with industry trends and best practices in data analysis. Continuously expand your knowledge and skills in data analytics techniques and tools.
Strong analytical skills and a passion for working with data.
Proficiency in data analysis tools and programming languages such as Excel, SQL, Python, or R.
Familiarity with data visualization tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI) is a plus.
Excellent communication skills, with the ability to translate complex data into understandable insights.
Detail-oriented and capable of maintaining data accuracy and integrity.
Strong problem-solving skills and the ability to think critically.
Highly organized and able to manage multiple tasks and deadlines effectively.
Team player with a collaborative mindset and the ability to work well in a diverse team environment.
Eagerness to learn and adapt to new tools, techniques, and technologies in the field of data analysis.
401(k)
Dental insurance
Disability insurance
Health insurance
Paid time off
Vision insurance
Bonus opportunities

ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should possess strong analytical skills and proficiency in data analysis tools such as Excel, SQL, and Python. Excellent communication skills and a detail-oriented mindset are essential for translating complex data into understandable insights.

title: Concierge (Full-Time) | Luxury High-Rise - Downtown Austin
description_text:
LOCATION: Downtown Austin - Rainey Street District (Near Trailhead Park)
SCHEDULE: Full-Time | Morning shift: 7:00 am - 3:00 pm | *Availability to work one weekend day per week*
ABOUT US
Worth Ross Management Company (WRMC) is an award-winning leader in luxury multifamily and HOA management. We are known for delivering exceptional service, maintaining high standards, and creating welcoming communities where residents truly feel at home. Our teams are the heart of our success, and we value professionalism, attention to detail, and a genuine commitment to service.
ABOUT THE ROLE

We are seeking a polished and service-oriented Concierge to be the welcoming presence of our luxury multifamily community. This role is ideal for a professional who takes pride in delivering elevated resident experiences, values discretion, and thrives in a fast-paced, high-touch service environment.
As a Concierge, you will greet residents, guests, and vendors, manage building access, and serve as a trusted point of contact for daily needs while ensuring privacy, professionalism, and exceptional service at all times.
What You’ll Do
Provide warm, professional service to residents, guests, and vendors.
Manage building access and guest/vendor check-ins.
Assist residents with questions, requests, and coordination of authorized vendor access.
Maintain confidentiality and uphold resident privacy at all times.
Respond effectively to resident concerns and emergency situations, escalating when needed.
Log daily activity, packages, and deliveries accurately in BuildingLink.
Contribute ideas to enhance services and improve the resident experience.
Support resident relations through timely follow-up and issue resolution.
Education & Experience
High school diploma or equivalent required.
Prior experience in hospitality, luxury residential, concierge, or customer service roles preferred.
What You Need to Succeed
Strong customer service and hospitality knowledge, ideally in a luxury environment.
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
High attention to detail and ability to multitask independently.
Comfort using technology such as email and concierge or property management systems.
Professional judgment, discretion, and a calm, solution-oriented mindset.
Flexibility to work varying schedules, including weekends and holidays.
Medical, Dental, and Vision Insurance
Short-Term and Long-Term Disability
Employee Assistance Program (EAP)
Identity Theft Protection
Pet Insurance
Retirement Plan
Paid Time Off (PTO)
401(k)

Serving Communities with Sophistication, Integrity, and Resourcefulness

At WRMC, we know communities are not just buildings – they are the people who live, work, and thrive in them. Our “clients for life” approach stems from a simple philosophy: put people first, and healthy communities will follow.

This is the heart of Stewardship by WRMC.

title: Senior Data Processor
description_text:
Required for processing and analyzing data to provide actionable insights into market trends, consumer behavior, and competitive dynamics. This role involves ensuring data accuracy through cleaning and integration. The analyst uses statistical methods and visualization tools to interpret data and create detailed reports and dashboards. They collaborate with cross-functional teams to support strategic decision-making and continuously improve research methodologies. Strong analytical skills, proficiency in statistical software, and the ability to communicate findings effectively are essential for this position. This position will be a hybrid position, with an option to work remotely, but will also require the candidate to come into the office, to foster team relationships and advance productivity.
KEY JOB RESPONSIBILITIES:
· Perform data checks using Quantum, SPSS and Excel
· Check Max-diff, Conjoint and segmentation setups
· Prepare tables in Quantum.
· Writing data cleaning scripts
· Do data manipulation like labeling, stacking, recoding etc.
· Resolving client queries and updating their requirements
· Communicate and coordinate with clients and internal teams
· Manage direct reports
CORE COMPETENCIES:
· Have knowledge of Quantum and SPSS; renaming, recoding, labelling, stacking data and other similar types of data manipulation required by clients
· Know how to QC tables
· Adapt to working in high pressure environments with strict deadlines and multiple deliverables
· Good English communication skills to communicate and coordinate with client and internal teams
QUALIFICATIONS
Education:
· Bachelor’s degree
Experience:
· Minimum 3-5 years of experience in Data processing within the Market Research industry
Computer Skills:
· Microsoft Office
· SPSS
· Quantum
Free Gourmet Tea/Coffee on the house.
Pick and Drop Cab facility for up to 20 Kms from Office location.
Extensive Mediclaim Benefit

They ensure data accuracy through cleaning and integration, and collaborate with cross-functional teams to support strategic decision-making.

When businesses have questions, we find answers. We help you understand your customers as people — their wants, needs and pain points. title: Stage - Health Data Scientist (H/F)
description_text:
Chez Withings, nous souhaitons redonner aux individus le contrôle de leur santé.
Nous avons l’obsession de créer des produits beaux et intuitifs, afin que chacun puisse les utiliser facilement au quotidien; nos balances connectées, montres hybrides, tensiomètres, moniteurs de sommeil et tous les dispositifs de notre gamme sont aujourd’hui utilisés par des millions d’utilisateurs.
Notre objectif : permettre la prévention, le dépistage et l’accompagnement d’un certain nombre de maladies chroniques via des produits et des services innovants, afin de révolutionner la manière dont on prend soin de notre santé.
Sur ce poste à mi-chemin entre l’analyse de données et les biostatistiques, tes responsabilités seront les suivantes :
Développement de modèles de Machine Learning de détection précoce de pathologies (SQL et Python)
Collaborer avec les équipes produits et cliniques, ainsi qu'avec des institutions de recherche externes ou des médecins
S’assurer que la recherche médicale de Withings est de haute qualité
Obtenir des certification et publier des études (journaux scientifiques, conférences)
Fortes compétences en traitement des données : SQL, Python, biostatistiques, Machine Learning…
Appétence forte pour les challenges techniques : stack on-premise, outils open-source, contraintes physiques des serveurs, sécurité forte liée à l’utilisation de données de santé…
Appétence forte pour le domaine de la santé : suivi et prévention de maladies chroniques, médecine prédictive et personnalisée…
Rigueur, autonomie, prise d'initiatives, curiosité
Maîtrise parfaite de la communication en français et en anglais, aussi bien à l’écrit qu’à l’oral
Stage de césure ou stage de fin d’études uniquement
Rejoindre l’aventure Withings, c’est :
Intégrer un des pionniers et leaders mondiaux de la santé connectée, plusieurs fois primé au Consumer Electronic Show
Contribuer à des projets innovants et ambitieux pour la santé de demain dans un environnement agile et en constante évolution
Intégrer une entreprise internationale, membre de la FrenchTech 120, dont les équipes sont basées à Issy-les-Moulineaux, Boston, Hong-Kong et Shenzhen
Participer à l’amélioration continue de nos produits et services en les bêta-testant avant leur sortie, notamment lors de nos nombreuses sessions sportives entre collègues
Bénéficier de nombreux avantages : Réductions pour des activités culturelles et sportives, restaurant d’entreprise, et bien plus encore
Collaborer avec des collègues passionnés et célébrer ensemble chacune de nos réussites !
Toutes les candidatures reçues sont étudiées indépendamment de l’origine ethnique, des croyances, de la religion, du genre, de l’orientation sexuelle ou de la santé des candidats. Withings aspire à offrir et garantir l’égalité des chances aux candidats et seules les personnes habilitées (RH et Management) auront accès aux informations concernant votre candidature.
ai_core_responsibilities: Develop machine learning models for early detection of pathologies and collaborate with product and clinical teams. Ensure high-quality medical research and publish studies in scientific journals and conferences.
ai_requirements_summary: Strong skills in data processing, including SQL and Python, along with a solid understanding of biostatistics and machine learning are required. Candidates should also have a keen interest in health challenges and possess excellent communication skills in both French and English.
linkedin_org_slogan: Create technology that people love, to make better health part of daily life.
linkedin_org_locations: 2, Rue Maurice Hartmann, Issy-les-Moulineaux, Ile-de-France 92130, FR
linkedin_org_description: Withings created the first smart scale in 2009, and has been the pioneer in connected health ever since. Its clinically validated and multi-award winning range is used by millions worldwide, and includes smart scales, hybrid watches, blood pressure monitors, sleep analyzers and more. The team of engineers, data scientists, and healthcare professionals at Withings work alongside clinical experts to make it possible to take medical-grade measurements at home. Withings was the first to bring measurements for pulse wave velocity and electrodermal activity into the home – finding ever more precise ways to measure our health. Each device is designed to fit beautifully into daily life, supported by an app that unlocks long-term health insights and offers programs to create meaningful change. At Withings we’re dedicated to creating a world where people can see their path to better health, and feel supported to pursue it. Find out more at withings.com and connect with us on Facebook, Instagram or Twitter.

title: Jefatura de RRHH (HR Operations Lead)
description_text:
🌱 ¡Sé parte del cambio con Birdman! 🌱

En Birdman, creamos alimentos de origen vegetal que cuidan del planeta y de las personas. 💚
About the Company
En los últimos diez años hemos experimentado un crecimiento extraordinario año tras año, posicionándonos como líderes en el sector de alimentos de origen vegetal. Nuestro objetivo es brindar a todas las personas acceso a un estilo de vida saludable, sostenible y libre de crueldad, a través de nuestras deliciosas y nutritivas propuestas. A medida que el movimiento global de alimentos plant-based se acelera, buscamos un dinámico HRBP & Operations Leadque se una a nuestro destacado equipo y contribuya a construir el próximo unicornio mexicano.

🎯El Rol
Este rol garantiza el funcionamiento eficiente, ordenado y humano de la operación de personas en sitio. Será responsable de crear un entorno de trabajo seguro, productivo y alineado a la cultura Birdman, integrando procesos operativos de RH con una visión estratégica de negocio.
Actúa como líder de operaciones de RH en sitio y como socio estratégico de los líderes, asegurando cumplimiento, eficiencia y una excelente experiencia para nuestros colaboradores.
🧩 Responsabilidades
🔧 HR Operations / Administración de RH
Asegurar la ejecución correcta y oportuna de los procesos transaccionales de RH del sitio: altas, bajas, movimientos, contratos, renovaciones, promociones, transferencias y actualizaciones de expediente. 
Administrar y validar incidencias de nómina del personal operativo y técnico (asistencias, tiempo extra, vacaciones, incapacidades, permisos, bonos e incentivos), coordinando con nóminas central para cierres sin errores. 
Garantizar el control documental conforme a políticas internas y requerimientos legales (expedientes físicos y digitales, firmas, actas administrativas, avisos y evidencias de capacitación). 
Operar y dar seguimiento a los módulos de HRIS/ERP (SAP, Workday, Oracle u otros), asegurando calidad de datos, trazabilidad y reporteo confiable. 
Atender solicitudes de colaboradores y líderes (constancias laborales, aclaraciones de nómina, beneficios, cambios de datos, etc.) bajo SLAs definidos. 
Coordinar auditorías internas y externas relacionadas con procesos de RH (nómina, expedientes, STPS, IMSS, INFONAVIT), asegurando cero hallazgos críticos y cierre oportuno de acciones. 
Identificar oportunidades de simplificación y automatización de procesos, estandarizando mejores prácticas para mejorar la eficiencia operativa. 
🤝 HR Business Partner
Socio estratégico del negocio
Asesorar a líderes del sitio en planeación de plantilla, estructura organizacional y prioridades de talento. 
Traducir necesidades operativas en iniciativas de RH basadas en datos. 
Monitorear KPIs de personas y ejecutar planes de mejora para cumplir objetivos del sitio. 
Relaciones laborales
Gestionar relaciones laborales y sindicales (si aplica), garantizando cumplimiento legal y consistencia. 
Liderar negociaciones y gestión del CCT, así como comités laborales. 
Conducir procesos disciplinarios, investigaciones y desvinculaciones conforme a la ley y a los valores de la compañía. 
Atracción y gestión de talento
Coordinar la atracción y selección de perfiles operativos, técnicos y mandos medios. 
Asegurar un onboarding efectivo, alineado a la cultura, seguridad y objetivos del negocio. 
Identificar talento crítico y desarrollar planes de sucesión para posiciones clave. 
Desarrollo organizacional y capacitación
Definir y ejecutar el plan anual de capacitación del sitio. 
Implementar programas de desarrollo técnico y de liderazgo. 
Impulsar una cultura de mejora continua, engagement y liderazgo en piso. 
Desempeño, compensaciones y bienestar
Administrar el ciclo de desempeño y planes de mejora individual. 
Garantizar la aplicación correcta y equitativa de compensaciones e incentivos. 
Participar en revisiones salariales y análisis de competitividad interna. 
Ejecutar iniciativas de clima laboral, bienestar, inclusión y comunicación interna. 
Facilitar mecanismos de escucha activa y diálogo con colaboradores.
💼 Experiencia Profesional
4 a 6 años de experiencia en Recursos Humanos generalista o como HR Business Partner, preferentemente en plantas productivas o entornos industriales. 
Experiencia sólida gestionando personal operativo, esquemas de turnos y procesos masivos. 
Manejo de relaciones laborales; experiencia en entornos sindicalizados es altamente deseable. 
Participación activa en auditorías laborales y de cumplimiento (STPS, NOMs, IMSS, INFONAVIT). 
Fuerte enfoque a negocio y orientación a resultados, con capacidad para priorizar en entornos dinámicos. 
Habilidad para influir, asesorar y retar constructivamente a líderes operativos. 
Experiencia en manejo de conflictos, negociación y toma de decisiones bajo presión. 
Comunicación clara, cercana y efectiva con personal de piso, mandos medios y liderazgo. 
Alto sentido de integridad, confidencialidad y apego a procesos. 
🛠️ Habilidades Técnicas
Conocimiento sólido de la Legislación Laboral Mexicana (LFT) y normativas STPS / NOMs. 
Experiencia en atracción de talento operativo y técnico, onboarding, capacitación y gestión del desempeño. 
Administración de compensaciones, incentivos y análisis de headcount. 
Excel intermedio–avanzado y manejo de HRIS/ERP (SAP, Workday, Oracle, Runa, Workable u otros). 
Deseable experiencia en metodologías de mejora continua, Lean o cultura de eficiencia operativa. 
🤝 Habilidades Blandas
Excelentes habilidades de comunicación, influencia y persuasión. 
Capacidad analítica y pensamiento crítico para la toma de decisiones basada en datos. 
Alta organización y habilidad para gestionar múltiples prioridades y proyectos simultáneamente. 
Fuerte orientación a las personas, con habilidades interpersonales y de servicio bien desarrolladas. 
🌍 Idiomas
Español: nativo 
Inglés: intermedio–avanzado (deseable)
Planta ubicada en parque San José del Astillero 2
Horario: Lunes a Viernes de 9:00 am a 6:00 pm

¡Postúlate ahora y ayuda a dar forma al futuro de la innovación plant-based! 🦅💻📊

title: Indigenous Relations Advisor
description_text:
Our Indigenous Relations team is currently looking to add an Indigenous Relations Advisor to our growing team, based in Nova Scotia or New Brunswick.
Reporting to the Senior Director, Indigenous Participation, this position will support the successful delivery of the Wasoqonatl Transmission Line by facilitating the delivery of Valard’s Indigenous engagement and participation plan. We are seeking a trusted advisor who has proven experience with environmental stewardship, cultural competency and awareness, training and employment and Indigenous procurement. We focus on building and maintaining respectful, collaborative relationships with Indigenous communities, leadership, and key partners throughout the project area.
Please Note: This position will be based in Nova Scotia or New Brunswick and will involve frequent travel to including to Indigenous communities and remote locations.
Duties and Responsibilities
Work closely with Valard’s Indigenous Relations team, Project Management Team (PMT), Employee Services and Procurement to support Indigenous engagement objectives, workforce participation, community communications, and business opportunities
Build and maintain strong, respectful relationships with Indigenous communities, leadership, and stakeholders, ensuring meaningful engagement through meetings, feedback, and ongoing communication
Identify, escalate, and support the resolution of issues, risks, or community concerns, collaborating with project leadership and internal teams as required
Maintain awareness of local and regional social, political, and economic considerations affecting the project and engagement approach
Prepare and distribute community communications and notifications throughout the project lifecycle, including activity updates
Support preparation and distribution of project milestone updates, lookahead schedules (as appropriate), and construction progress notices
Develop project information packages and community-facing materials for leadership, members, businesses, and other stakeholders
Produce regular reporting including tracking and summarizing hiring, workforce hours, spend, and procurement
Ensure all communications, engagement activities, commitments, and issues are recorded and tracked per project requirements
Coordinate and participate in community events, open houses, career fairs, and other engagement activities to strengthen relationships and support workforce and business objectives
Support workforce communications and respond to employee or community concerns related to project employment and engagement
Assist in developing engagement plans and supporting achievable Indigenous participation targets (employment and subcontracting)
Represent Valard professionally in community settings and on project-related matters
Post-secondary education in business administration, communications, Indigenous studies, public relations, or a related field is preferred (or equivalent experience)
5 years of related experience in Indigenous relations, community engagement, stakeholder relations, or similar roles
Proven ability to work independently while coordinating effectively with a broader project team
Strong understanding of Indigenous community and cultural considerations, including the importance of protocols and trust-building
Preferred Skills / Qualifications
Experience in construction, energy, utilities, infrastructure, or linear projects 
Job Posting End Date: March 9, 2026
We offer a comprehensive and competitive total rewards package that incorporates a complete range of employee benefits, including an RRSP matching program, to ensure you have the tools necessary to manage, maintain, and improve your health and wellbeing. 
About our Business
Valard Construction is Canada’s premier utility contractor providing comprehensive EPC+ (engineering, procurement, construction) and maintenance services to utilities, independent power producers, mining, oil and gas industries and rural electric associations. We have the capabilities, knowledge, and resources to take on any project, any size, any complexity, anywhere. We are also part of Quanta Services, the largest electrical power contractor in North America.
Valard Construction is an equal opportunity employer.
Learn more
Visit us at www.valard.com and connect with us on our social media channels for our latest news, employee stories, community activities, and other updates.
Depending on qualifications, the successful candidate may be offered a position at a more appropriate level.
Applicants must have legal authorization to work in Canada with no restrictions.
Valard Construction is committed to providing employment opportunities to all qualified individuals, without regard to race, religion, age, sex, color, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, veteran status, or disability. Accommodation for applicants with disabilities is available on request during the recruitment process.

This includes building relationships with Indigenous communities and stakeholders, managing communications, and addressing community concerns.

A strong understanding of Indigenous cultural considerations and the ability to work independently while coordinating with a project team is essential.

Please Note: This position will be based in Nova Scotia or New Brunswick and will involve frequent travel to including to Indigenous communities and remote locations.
Duties and Responsibilities
Work closely with Valard’s Indigenous Relations team, Project Management Team (PMT), Employee Services and Procurement to support Indigenous engagement objectives, workforce participation, community communications, and business opportunities
Build and maintain strong, respectful relationships with Indigenous communities, leadership, and stakeholders, ensuring meaningful engagement through meetings, feedback, and ongoing communication
Identify, escalate, and support the resolution of issues, risks, or community concerns, collaborating with project leadership and internal teams as required
Maintain awareness of local and regional social, political, and economic considerations affecting the project and engagement approach
Prepare and distribute community communications and notifications throughout the project lifecycle, including activity updates
Support preparation and distribution of project milestone updates, lookahead schedules (as appropriate), and construction progress notices
Develop project information packages and community-facing materials for leadership, members, businesses, and other stakeholders
Produce regular reporting including tracking and summarizing hiring, workforce hours, spend, and procurement
Ensure all communications, engagement activities, commitments, and issues are recorded and tracked per project requirements
Coordinate and participate in community events, open houses, career fairs, and other engagement activities to strengthen relationships and support workforce and business objectives
Support workforce communications and respond to employee or community concerns related to project employment and engagement
Assist in developing engagement plans and supporting achievable Indigenous participation targets (employment and subcontracting)
Represent Valard professionally in community settings and on project-related matters
Post-secondary education in business administration, communications, Indigenous studies, public relations, or a related field is preferred (or equivalent experience)
5 years of related experience in Indigenous relations, community engagement, stakeholder relations, or similar roles
Proven ability to work independently while coordinating effectively with a broader project team
Strong understanding of Indigenous community and cultural considerations, including the importance of protocols and trust-building
Preferred Skills / Qualifications
Experience in construction, energy, utilities, infrastructure, or linear projects 
Job Posting End Date: March 9, 2026
We offer a comprehensive and competitive total rewards package that incorporates a complete range of employee benefits, including an RRSP matching program, to ensure you have the tools necessary to manage, maintain, and improve your health and wellbeing. 
About our Business
Valard Construction is Canada’s premier utility contractor providing comprehensive EPC+ (engineering, procurement, construction) and maintenance services to utilities, independent power producers, mining, oil and gas industries and rural electric associations. We have the capabilities, knowledge, and resources to take on any project, any size, any complexity, anywhere. We are also part of Quanta Services, the largest electrical power contractor in North America.
Valard Construction is an equal opportunity employer.
Learn more
Visit us at www.valard.com and connect with us on our social media channels for our latest news, employee stories, community activities, and other updates.
Depending on qualifications, the successful candidate may be offered a position at a more appropriate level.
Applicants must have legal authorization to work in Canada with no restrictions.
Valard Construction is committed to providing employment opportunities to all qualified individuals, without regard to race, religion, age, sex, color, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, veteran status, or disability. Accommodation for applicants with disabilities is available on request during the recruitment process.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Indigenous Relations Advisor will support the delivery of the Wasoqonatl Transmission Line by facilitating Valard’s Indigenous engagement and participation plan. This includes building relationships with Indigenous communities and stakeholders, managing communications, and addressing community concerns.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have post-secondary education in a relevant field and at least 5 years of experience in Indigenous relations or community engagement. A strong understanding of Indigenous cultural considerations and the ability to work independently while coordinating with a project team is essential.
linkedin_org_slogan: Any Utility Project, Any Size, Any Complexity, Any Place.
