title: Nursing Clinical Instructor
organization: ATS Institute of Technology, Nursing School
description_text:
As a Nursing Clinical Instructor at ATS Institute of Technology, you will play a crucial role in shaping the future of nursing by educating and mentoring aspiring nurses in real-world clinical settings. You will be responsible for guiding students as they transition from theoretical knowledge to practical application, ensuring they develop the competencies necessary to excel in their nursing careers.
ATS Institute of Technology has a longstanding tradition of excellence in nursing education since 1998, and we are currently looking for passionate and skilled Nursing Clinical Instructors to join our team.
Key Responsibilities:
Deliver comprehensive clinical instruction to nursing students in diverse healthcare environments.
Supervise and evaluate students’ clinical performance and provide constructive feedback.
Collaborate with clinical site staff to facilitate student learning experiences.
Participate in faculty meetings and professional development activities.
Benefits of Joining ATS Institute of Technology:
Flexible scheduling options, including evening and weekend hours.
Professional development opportunities and mentoring support.
A collaborative and supportive work environment focused on student success.
Provide suupor while transitioning into novice nurse educator roles.
About the Company:
At ATS Institute of Technology, we prioritize creating an environment of trust and support. Our primary goal is to empower students to succeed and continuously improve our educational offerings. We are proud of our dedicated team who share a common mission: to equip our students with the essential knowledge, skills, and values to thrive in today’s dynamic nursing landscape.
Bachelor’s degree in nursing from an accredited institution; a Master’s degree in nursing education or related field is preferred.
Current and valid Registered Nurse (RN) license in Illinois.
Minimum of 3 years of clinical nursing experience.
Recent direct patient care experience within the last year.
Pay: $55/hour
Dedicated Paid Time Off: Competitive PTO plan including vacation, personal days, sick leave, and holidays to recharge and focus on personal well-being.
Professional Development: Access to training, CE credits, and opportunities to grow your expertise in nursing education.
Rewarding Work Environment: Join a mission-driven team where your efforts directly impact the success of aspiring nurses and the future of healthcare.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Nursing Clinical Instructor will deliver clinical instruction and supervise nursing students in healthcare settings. They will also evaluate student performance and collaborate with clinical site staff.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have a Bachelor’s degree in nursing and a current RN license in Illinois. A minimum of 3 years of clinical nursing experience is required, along with recent direct patient care experience.

title: Bookkeeper - MS Dynamics - Part-Time Leading to Full-Time
description_text:
CHANGE DEPARTMENT TO USE
Do you have excellent bookkeeping skills? Are you detailed oriented with strong follow through? Do you enjoy working in a collaborative work environment? If yes, consider City Wide!
City Wide Facility Solutions - Torontois seeking a part-time (leading to full-time) Bookkeeper to join our team!
City Wide, North America's leading Professional Management company in the building maintenance industry, provides solutions to a wide range of issues clients face at their facilities. In this position, you will serve the City Wide Facility Solutions - Toronto, one of more than 110 markets covered across the United States and Canada. 
Why City Wide?
City Wide Facility Solutions is a leader in the building maintenance industry, with a locations that serve communities across the U.S. and Canada. Our company mission is to create a ripple effect...by positively impacting the community of people we serve. Our vision is the be the first choice for our clients, contractors and employees . Our values of community, accountability and professionalism are how we model success. At City Wide, you will find a high-energy work environment that rewards performance within a collaborative, inclusive and fun environment. Are you ready to join?
What you will do...
The Part-Time Charge Bookkeeper is responsible for recording timely and accurate financial transactions for Accounts Receivables, Accounts Payable, Payroll, Account Reconciliations and Financials Statements.
Daily Customer Invoicing
Account collections and customer service
Vendor management including credit cards, insurance, facilities and supply houses
Facilitate cash management
Contract management, setup and monthly billing
Independent contractor management, setup, and monthly payments
Ensure accuracy of financial records – reconciling all balance sheet accounts monthly.
Create reports and financial statements, explain to management variances.
Comply with local, state, and federal government reporting requirements (ie sales tax)
Provide clerical and administrative support to management as requested
What is required...
Minimum 3 years in a bookkeeping, accounting or related role
Basic understanding and application of basic accounting rules
Proficient in MS Business Central (or a strong willingness to learn - training provided) accounting software
Strong attention to detail and follow up/follow through
Associates or Bachelor's Degree in Accounting or Finance, preferred...
City Wide Facility Solutions offers a competitive compensation and benefits. 
More on City Wide...
City Wide Facility Solutions is a fast-growing company with over 110 franchise locations across the United States and Canada. Founded in 1961, City Wide Facility Solutions is the largest management company in the building maintenance industry. We have over 60 years in the business and continue to experience high growth and profitability across our franchise businesses. Our culture supports the company’s Mission to create a Ripple Effect by positively impacting the people and communities they serve. Read more about City Wide at www.gocitywide.com
City Wide is an Equal Opportunity Employer.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Part-Time Charge Bookkeeper is responsible for recording timely and accurate financial transactions, including Accounts Receivables, Accounts Payable, and Payroll. Additional duties include customer invoicing, vendor management, and ensuring the accuracy of financial records.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have a minimum of 3 years in a bookkeeping or accounting role and a basic understanding of accounting rules. Proficiency in MS Business Central is preferred, along with strong attention to detail.

title: Customer Experience Coordinator
locations_alt_raw: West Palm Beach, Florida, United States
locations_derived: West Palm Beach, Florida, United States
description_text:
Ironwear is an established and growing safety solution provider. For over 35 years we have manufactured, developed, and reengineered Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) for all industrial markets around the globe. The Customer Experience Coordinator will assist as a direct link between the Customer, Sales Team, and internal personnel. The position includes a limited sales capacity, maintaining customer relationships, providing ongoing support, addressing various customer needs, addressing inquiries regarding products, and helping them make informed decisions about products to purchase. 
Responsibilities:
Answer incoming phone calls, customer support emails and customer chats while providing a first-class customer experience.
Evaluate and write up incoming orders and present them to the Processing Department.
Assist customers with product inquiries regarding inventory, pricing, incoming orders, back orders, and returned goods.
Have a basic understanding of company product knowledge to properly address customer questions and concerns. 
Offer customers alternative products or additional products to boost sales and provide first-class customer service. 
Provide customer outreach when necessary to relay and evaluate any pending issues. 
Communicate cross-functionally with processing team/Account Managers to ensure orders are processed accurately inclusive of; price validation, shipping methods/costs, backorders etc. 
Communicate with account managers to address any customer concerns. 
Process all paperwork for customer orders/RMAs/backorders etc.
Compile pertinent customer data in provided documentation and flag any concerns/patterns to management.
Maintain and collect accurate customer data in our ticketing, ERP and CRM platforms 
Required Skills/Abilities:
Superior verbal and written communication skills.
Proficient in Microsoft Office Suite.
Ability to multi-task in a demanding environment
Organized and meticulous.
Strong interpersonal skills.
CX ticketing platform experience is a plus (Zendesk/Gorgias/Kustomer/similar).
Education and Experience:
High school degree or GED required.
A minimum of two year’s related experience
Health Care (Medical, Dental & Vision)
401(k) Plan with Company Match
Paid Time Off (Vacation, Sick & Public Holidays)
Convenient Location with Free Parking
Company Provided Beverages and Snacks
Dog Friendly Work Atmosphere
Company Gatherings and Events

With a robust and versatile line of products, Ironwear will help define the necessary item for the task at hand. If we do not manufacture a specific product for the intended application, we will re-engineer it or create a custom design suited for the job.

Beyond manufacturing, Ironwear is a leading resource for safety solutions. Through 46 years of industry experience and product knowledge, Ironwear educates the market while staying ahead of current standards and recent innovations.

title: Sr Acquisition Analyst
description_text:
Position Title: Sr Acquisition analyst
Location: Silver Spring, MD
Category: Funded
Schedule (FT/PT): FT
Travel Required: NA
Shift: Day
Remote Type: Hybrid
Clearance required: Public Trust
Division: international Cooperation
In this role, the Sr. Acquisition Analyst shall provide procurement/acquisition/contracting expertise sufficient to identify, understand, analyze, articulate and ensure compliance with acquisition/contracting regulations, guidelines, policies, processes, and procedures as defined by the Federal Government, DoD, MHS, Army/ MRMC, and DHA. Specifically, you shall provide expertise with requirements of the Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) and Department of Defense Federal Acquisition Regulation Supplement (DFARS).
Primary Responsibilities:
Assist Contracting Officer Representatives (COR’s) in drafting and developing Statements of Work (SOWs), Statements of Objectives (SOOs), Performance Work Statements (PWS) and Performance-Based SOWs (PBSOWs).
Communicate/Interact with COR’s on a daily basis, and have knowledge of COR functions.
Prepare Procurement Requests (PR) packages and monitor their status throughout the DHA Contracting Office Division (COD), and the United States Army Medical Research Acquisition Activity (USAMRAA) procurement process.
Process PR packages through the DHA Defense Agency Initiative (DAI) or government designated Financial Management Systems.
Assist in drafting special clauses and provisions to address DHA, USAMRAA, or unique program situations.
Assist COR’s in writing sole source justifications.
Support the development of acquisition strategies and plans for DHA procurements.
Assist COR’s in developing the Independent Government Cost Estimates (IGCE’s).
Assist COR’s in preparing and drafting Market Research documents.
Assist COR’s in Quality Assurance Surveillance Plan (QASP) development.
Track contract expenditures (i.e. burn rate) on contracts.
Assist the DHA COR’s with Contract Closeout.
Assist COR’s with invoice certification.
Generate source selection plans, evaluation worksheets, and templates; and monitor the Source Selection Evaluation Board (SSEB) Process.
Communicate and interact with the DHA and the USAMRAA Contracting Activity in tracking status on acquisition procurements.
Assist in the preparation of Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) and Administrative Policies in acquisition support.
Participate in reporting and analysis activities.
Familiarity with the FAR and DFAR 
Ability to work independently and with minimal direct supervision
Experience with Microsoft Office products
Ability to present and communicate materials to various audiences
Medical/Dental/Vision Insurance 
ACT1 Employee Stock Ownership Plan (ESOP) 
Company Paid Life and AD&D Insurance 
Company Paid Short-Term Disability 
Voluntary Long-Term Disability 
Flexible Spending Accounts (FSA) 
Health Savings Account (HSA) 
401K with employer match 
Paid Time Off 
Paid Holidays 
Parental Leave 
Military Leave 
Education, Training & Professional Development 
Voluntary Accidental Injury/Critical Illness/Hospital Care 
Voluntary Pet Insurance, Legal Resources, and Identity Protection 

https://act1federal.com/careers/ 
Salaries are commensurate with experience and qualifications, as well as market and business considerations. New Jersey Pay Transparency Range: $90,000-$128,000
All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to disability, status as a protected veteran or any other status protected by applicable federal, state, local, or international law.

title: Child Development Specialist FT/PRN
description_text:
Who you are:
Do you have a passion and a calling for helping children? Are you a motivated, compassionate person with a desire to make a positive impact on children’s lives? 
Experience in the mental health field is not required, we are looking for individuals who are passionate about our mission and want to Build Lives of Possibility. Every day will not be easy, but what you do will matter and your reach will impact the children and families you work with for years to come and touch thousands of lives. 
We are looking for individuals with initiative, creativity, and a desire to help children overcome their challenges. The Bradley Center is a great place to launch your career and gain valuable experience. 
Come be the drop in the pond that creates ripples of hope beyond even our imagination. Join our team today!
Where you will make a difference:
Since 1905, The Bradley Center has continually served children and families in need. Today, the residential treatment facility provides 24 hour care and trauma-informed treatment to children who have a history of trauma and who may present with a wide range of challenges. 
Bachelor’s degree in social work, psychology or other related field preferred
OR a minimum of 48 college credits in social work, psychology or other related field required
Must be at least 21 years of age
Valid driver's license and car insurance
Qualifications Best Suited for the Position
Initiative
Ability to build rapport with children that leads to pro-social development
Patience and creativity while working with residents at their own pace
Excellent verbal and written communication skills
Professional demeanor, sound judgment, dependability, and a strong work ethic
Problem-solving skills
What we offer:
Excellent benefits for Full Time staff including: 
$20/hr starting wage
Medical, dental and vision coverage
$1500 Sign on Bonus
$2000 relocation assistance
Shift differential for weekend and overnight shifts
Four weeks of Paid Time Off in the first year of employment 
Accommodation of school schedules
Referral bonuses 
401(k)
Life insurance
Free parking and meals
Advanced training opportunities
Continuing education credits
Full time and PRN positions available for all shifts.
We are committed to advocacy for children with special needs, care and compassion, and focusing on the inherent strengths of children, youth and families without regard to race, religion, sexual orientation, national origin, age, or gender. The Bradley Center is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All applicants will be considered for employment without attention to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, veteran or disability status.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Child Development Specialist will work with children who have a history of trauma, providing support and guidance to help them overcome challenges. The role involves creating a positive impact on the lives of children and families through compassionate care.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have a bachelor's degree in social work, psychology, or a related field, or at least 48 college credits in these areas. Applicants must be at least 21 years old and possess a valid driver's license and car insurance.
linkedin_org_locations: 5180 Campbells Run Raod, Pittsburgh, PA 15205, US
linkedin_org_description:
For over 100 years Bradley has been providing hope to children and families in need of comprehensive and caring services. 

Bradley was founded by The United Methodist Women in 1905 as The Elizabeth A. Bradley Home for Children and operated for many years as an orphanage and as interim housing for children whose parents were separated or divorced. In 1972, it was incorporated as The Bradley Center to serve abused, neglected and dependent children. 

In 1991, Bradley was reorganized by a new non-denominational, community-based Board of Trustees and executive management. 

The Bradley Center has since evolved into an accredited, regional behavioral healthcare and child welfare system that provides hope to young girls and boys. 

Bradley is committed to advocacy for children and dedicated to the restoration of productive relationships among children, their families and the community whenever possible. 

The Bradley Center implements the Sanctuary Model of Trauma Informed Care developed by Dr. Sandra Bloom. Sanctuary is a treatment and organizational model that is based on understanding trauma. Every staff and resident in care focus on "SELF"​ - Safety (physical, psychological, social and moral), Emotion management, Loss (traumatic experiences and separation), and the Future (how can things get better?). 

Bradley has earned the quality distinction of accreditation by the Joint Commission on Accreditation of Healthcare Organizations. It is also licensed by the Pennsylvania Departments of Education and Welfare. 

Bradley's Vision is to be recognized nationally by families, communities and professionals as a premier behavioral healthcare, education and child welfare organization that promotes the physical, social, emotional, educational and spiritual growth of the children, youth and families that it serves.

title: Stadium Operations & Events Technician
description_text:
The Stadium Operations & Events Technician plays a key role in supporting stadium preparation to ensure it is game-day ready and operating smoothly. This is a highly hands-on position focused on physical labour, problem-solving, and contributing to the setup, execution, and teardown of games and events. You’ll be part of the crew that fixes, builds, moves, paints, lifts, and prepares which often happens behind the scenes, but is essential to the fan experience. 
This role requires flexibility, mechanical aptitude, and a willingness to jump in wherever help is needed, especially leading up to and during game days. 
Term: May 5, 2026 – December 11, 2026 
Key Responsibilities: 
Assist with game-day field set-up, in-game operations, and post-game teardown 
Support field preparation activities, including field painting for games and special events 
Assist with event set-up and tear-down for stadium events such as Party in the Park and other activations 
Operate equipment and machinery including forklifts and other stadium equipment (training provided as needed) 
Perform basic repairs and troubleshooting on equipment such as generators, inflatable fans, and event assets 
Support general stadium maintenance, repairs, and manual labour tasks 
Assist with loading, unloading, moving, and staging equipment and materials 
Identify and problem-solve operational issues quickly and safely 
Travel to support off-site or out-of-town events as required 
Work collaboratively with Stadium Operations, Game Entertainment & Community, and other departments 
Complete other duties as assigned to support overall stadium operations 
What You’ll Need: 
A self-motivated, hardworking individual who takes pride in physical work 
Strong problem-solving skills and the ability to adapt quickly in a fast-paced environment 
Willingness to take initiative and go beyond assigned tasks when needed 
Valid driver’s license 
Ability to safely operate heavy machinery, including forklifts (or willingness to be trained) 
Mechanical aptitude and comfort working with tools and equipment 
Ability to lift a minimum of 50 lbs, stand or work on your feet for extended periods, and work long hours 
Availability to work weekdays, evenings, weekends, and statutory holidays 
Comfortable working outdoors in varying weather conditions 
What do we commit to you? 
Opportunity to contribute to the success of one of Canada’s largest brands and an organization that is a cornerstone of our province and Rider Nation. 
The passion of being part of something much bigger than all of us. The Saskatchewan Roughriders have a dedicated and passionate fan base, and at the Riders, we strive to work hard to support and grow the team and organization. 
Gain valuable hands-on experience in a fast-paced organization and team. 
Unbeatable work location. You will be set up directly out of Mosaic Stadium and be fully immersed in the Rider experience. 
Wondering what to expect when working for the Riders? 
Our employees are empowered to make a difference not only in the community, but in our business operations as well. It is our “secret” to a happy workplace. To do that, the Riders foster a culture that values respect, teamwork, hustle, hard work and fun (it is football after all!). 
The Saskatchewan Roughriders are advocates for equity and inclusion and want our workforce to reflect the diversity that makes our province strong. YOU are Rider Nation, and we want the growth of our team to reflect all the amazing talents Saskatchewan has to offer. 
We acknowledge the time and effort that is required to apply and thank all applicants for their interest, however only those chosen for an interview will be contacted.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Stadium Operations & Events Technician assists with game-day field set-up, in-game operations, and post-game teardown. They support general stadium maintenance and repairs while collaborating with various departments.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should be self-motivated and possess strong problem-solving skills, with the ability to adapt in a fast-paced environment. A valid driver's license and the ability to lift at least 50 lbs are also required.
linkedin_org_slogan:
Home of your Saskatchewan Roughriders on LinkedIn.
Building. Let's go 1-0 this week.
linkedin_org_locations: 1734 Elphinstone St, Regina, Saskatchewan, CA
linkedin_org_description: The Saskatchewan Roughriders are a Canadian Football League Team with over 100 years of history.  In recent years the Roughriders (Riders)  have routinely led the CFL in revenue generation and fan support including ticket sales, social media engagement and TV viewership.  Roughrider home games are played at Mosaic Stadium where a sea of fans dressed in green cover over 33,000 seats. The Riders sell more branded merchandise than all of the other CFL teams combined and with a following across Canada and the US, the Riders regularly sell out home games and draw large crowds at away games.  The success of the Roughriders comes despite being the smallest metro area in the North America to have a franchise among top 6 professional sports leagues (CFL, NFL, MLB, NHL, MLS, NBA).

title: Branch Custodian
description_text:
Pay
Hiring Range: $15.30 - $18.51 per hour
Pay Range: $15.30 - $21.72 per hour
Description
The branch custodian keeps Library spaces and grounds clean, welcoming, and in good repair for our staff and community members. Working under the direct supervision of the Facilities Assistant Manager, and with an unwavering commitment to diversity, equity, and inclusion, the Custodian will:
Clean and disinfect everywhere including restrooms; replenish and maintain supplies. Vacuum carpets, use floor scrubber, mop, and dry mop to clean hard floors, empty trash, and replace light bulbs and tubes. Spot clean carpets as necessary. Empties and cleans waste receptacles including recycle containers. Does emergency clean-up which may include interacting with hazardous materials.
Set up and take down chairs, tables, and equipment in all meeting rooms.
If assigned to a branch, be responsive to requests from appropriate onsite staff.
Maintain equipment and machinery used by Facilities staff, such as vacuum cleaners, floor buffers, snow blowers, etc.
Minor repairs to equipment, book shelving, office furniture, and building components. Some furniture and shelving assembly required. Perform preventive maintenance tasks such as filter changes and attend to unforeseen building maintenance situations as they occur.
Secure library buildings and set alarms as needed. Maintains patron and staff access to building, parking areas, and grounds.
Empty curbside book/video drops. Receive deliveries of Library materials throughout the day.
Replenish and maintain supplies. Fill out forms, reports, and requisitions for the Facilities Manager.
Maintains cleanliness of library grounds, including ice and snow removal. 
Perform other duties as assigned.
Education and Experience
High School diploma or equivalent required. 
Custodial/maintenance experience preferred.
Hours
Part Time, 20 hour workweek
Partial days, evenings, and weekends may be required.
Requirements
Reliable transportation, and reachable by telephone in emergency situations.
Dependability, punctuality, trustworthiness, good attendance and work habits.
Working knowledge of standard maintenance routines.
Organizational skills and understanding of good cleaning practices.
Independent judgment related to emergency and safety matters.
Maintain Library in a safe, secure, clean, and orderly condition.
Ability to:
Work harmoniously and cooperatively with supervisor, co-workers and patrons.
Understand and carry out verbal and written instructions.
Stand and walk for long periods of time; reach and bend and engage in strenuous physical activities on a routine basis and lift and move heavy objects (50 lbs).
Effectively and safely use cleaning materials, supplies, and equipment, including vacuum and floor scrubber. Recognize and follow-through with detailed cleanliness.
Safely apply methods and techniques of standard maintenance routines
Accept and manage change.
Work independently and collaboratively.
Exercise initiative, be enthusiastic, creative, flexible, energetic, value diversity, and possess a sense of humor.
Dental and Vision Insurance Plans
Hoosier S.T.A.R.T 457(b) Employee Funded Retirement Plan
Flexible Spending Account (FSA)
Employee Assistance Program
16 Vacation Hours Annually
40 Sick Time Hours Annually 
10 Paid Holidays
ai_core_responsibilities: The branch custodian is responsible for keeping library spaces and grounds clean and in good repair. This includes cleaning, disinfecting, performing minor repairs, and maintaining equipment.
ai_requirements_summary: A high school diploma or equivalent is required, along with custodial or maintenance experience preferred. The candidate must demonstrate reliability, good attendance, and the ability to work independently and collaboratively.
linkedin_org_slogan: The St. Joe County Public Library connects members of our community to learning, resources, culture, and each other.
linkedin_org_locations: 304 S. Main St., South Bend, IN 46601, US
linkedin_org_description:
The St. Joe County Public Library (SJCPL) has been serving our community since 1889. We’re a dynamic, creative organization that believes libraries should spark curiosity, build community, and make learning feel fun.

Across 10 locations, we offer everything from book clubs and author talks to digital media production, maker tools, and hands-on learning spaces. Our culture is collaborative, innovative, and full of people who genuinely love what they do.

If you’re looking for a workplace where ideas matter, creativity is celebrated, and every day brings something new, SJCPL is a place to belong. Learn more at sjcpl.org.

description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Franchise Business Coach - REMOTE. In this role, you will play a crucial part in supporting a portfolio of franchise locations throughout the United States. Your expertise will empower franchise owners to enhance their operations, boost revenue growth, and align their strategies with brand standards. As a trusted advisor, you will be instrumental in driving performance improvements through structured guidance, meaningful communication, and data-driven insights. This position requires both a solid foundation in business acumen and the ability to build strong relationships with stakeholders.

Accountabilities
Serve as the point of contact for operational guidance and business support to franchise owners
Coach owners and their teams on leadership, marketing, sales, service delivery, and financials
Organize and lead meetings around best practices to drive brand growth and revenue
Build and maintain strong relationships and consistent lines of communication with franchisees
Develop and deliver training and growth initiatives through meetings, workshops, and virtual sessions
Contribute to the creation of marketing, sales, operational, financial and training strategies
Facilitate monthly collaborative owner forums to foster continuous improvement and accountability
Inspire and motivate franchisees to achieve success in their businesses
Plan and execute franchise meetings and regional sessions to support network-wide engagement
Conduct regular check-in calls and field visits, documenting action items and coaching opportunities
Review P&Ls, forecasts, and KPIs with owners; support actionable improvement plans
Facilitate regional and national training programs and cooperative initiatives

Requirements
Bachelor’s degree required
5+ years of operational leadership or business management experience required
3+ years of franchise operations experience required
Demonstrated sales and revenue-growth experience required
Proven ability to guide and influence business owners and leadership teams required
Willingness/ability to travel up to 50% within the US required

Competitive salary ranging from $90,000 to $110,000 + Bonus (up to 30%)

Flexible working hours with a remote work option

title: Web Developer (Contract)
description_text:
Epoch AI is looking for a web development contractor to expand our capacity during a website refresh.

About the role
This is an independent contractor role. Your main responsibility will be to implement existing Figma designs in React for an Astro website, though no prior familiarity with Astro is necessary. You will coordinate with our web development team to achieve our web redesign, enabling us to better communicate years’ worth of research on AI trends. This role is fully remote, and we are able to offer contracts in many countries, although we prefer candidates in time-zones overlapping with our existing team (between UTC+1 and UTC-7). Please do not include a cover letter, photograph, or headshot of yourself, or any personal information that is not relevant to the role for which you're applying (including marital status, age, identity traits, etc.). We are moving quickly to fill this role, apply soon. 

\n

What we're looking for
Required: Prior experience in React 
Required: Professional web development experience
Nice to have: Familiarity with Astro

Compensation
Hourly compensation at $60 USD. Full-time is preferred, though we are open to part-time. Wages are not restricted to USD, and contracts and payments are usually in local currencies. Conversions are based on average exchange rates.
Fully remote environment, including flexible work hours. 

\n
Additional Information
While we welcome applicants from all time zones, we prefer candidates who can overlap with UTC–8 (Pacific Time) and UTC (Greenwich Mean Time), as most of our staff work in this range of time zones. 
Please submit all of your application materials in English and note that we require professional level English proficiency.
Epoch is committed to building an inclusive, equitable, and supportive community for you to thrive and do your best work. We’re committed to finding the best people for our team, so please don’t hesitate to apply for a role regardless of your age, gender identity/expression, political identity, personal preferences, physical abilities, veteran status, neurodiversity or any other background. Please email careers@epoch.ai if you have any questions about this role, accessibility requests, or if you want to request an extension to the application deadline. However, we will not review applications submitted to this email address; please submit your application through the link on this page. 

About Epoch AI
Epoch AI is a research institute that investigates trends in machine learning and the economic consequences of AI. Our mission is to develop a comprehensive, publicly accessible knowledge base on AI that informs policymakers, industry leaders, and society at large.
We strive to achieve both rigor and accessibility to our work, as exemplified by some of our most successful projects, including our database of AI models and our AI trends dashboard. Our body of research includes our work on compute trends (IJCN 2022), data scarcity (ICML 2024), and algorithmic progress (NeurIPS 2024). You can read more about our work and mission on our website and in this Time profile.
ai_core_responsibilities: The main responsibility is to implement existing Figma designs in React for an Astro website. You will coordinate with the web development team to achieve the web redesign.
ai_requirements_summary: Prior experience in React and professional web development experience are required. Familiarity with Astro is a nice-to-have but not necessary.

title: Remote Sr. Engineer
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Remote Software Engineer. In this role, you will be integral to connecting and managing blockchain nodes that interface with various platforms. You will contribute to building a robust infrastructure that enhances the overall efficiency and reliability of our blockchain services. Our aim is to create seamless connections across diverse financial ecosystems while pushing the boundaries of what's possible within the blockchain domain. Join us to make a meaningful impact and help shape the future of the financial system through innovative technology solutions.
\n

Accountabilities
Upgrade blockchain nodes and ensure data consistency.
Implement automation to reduce operational toil and enhance service effectiveness.
Manage and improve blockchain traffic to facilitate seamless connectivity.
Ensure scalability and reliability across the blockchain platform.
Pioneer technologies to decouple node storage and computing.

Requirements
At least 5 years of experience in software engineering.
Experience designing and scaling production services.
Proficient in writing high-quality, well-tested code.
Strong interest in building an open financial system.
Ability to identify and streamline inefficiencies.

Benefits
Opportunity to work with a talented and driven team.
Flexibility of remote work with in-person collaboration events.
Competitive salary and benefits, including medical, dental, and vision.
Participation in employee equity programs.
Support for continuous learning and professional development.

\n
Why Apply Through Jobgether?
We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.
We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

title: Roofing Sales Representative
description_text:
We are a residential roofing company providing high quality home services to the local community. We succeed by providing a premier customer experience. Our mission is to provide quality, with integrity, at a value. From lifetime material and labor warranties to certified factory-trained installers, our goal is to turn our customer's Dreams into reality.
Our culture is fast-paced, diverse, and rewarding. We gauge our success on the success of our employees.
\n

What you will do:
Conduct 10 to 15 pre-scheduled and confirmed in-home appointments per week - No cold calling or door knocking required
Earn more commission for self-generated business
Drive to pre-set appointments
Complete a thorough three week training program with our dedicated sales training team
Attend weekly sales meetings
Additional responsibilities as assigned by leadership

Rewards
Base salary plus commission during the first 6 months. Fully commission after the first 6 month of employment.
Uncapped commission program with ability to earn over six figures in your first year
Promotions from within
Competitive sales contests with cash prizes and other awards
Ongoing classroom style training and in-home sales training
Travel opportunities
Work with supporting departments to take job through completion following point of sale

Must Haves:
Must have reliable vehicle and a valid driver's license
Ability to work a 5 day work week between Monday-Saturday, Saturdays are mandatory
In home sales experience is preferred but not required
Is coachable and eager to learn
Self disciplined and money motivated
Proficient in using technology; laptop, mobile device, tablet etc.
Outstanding negotiation and communication skills

Schedule:
Monday through Friday appointments are scheduled at 10am, 2pm, 6pm
Saturday appointments are scheduled at 10am, and 2pm.

Plusses:
In home sales experience
One call close experience

\n
$125,000 - $250,000 a year
$125,000 - $250,000 a year

Benefits:
Health/Dental/Vision insurance
401(k)
Referral program
Paid time off (PTO)
W-2 Pay Structure
Career advancement opportunities
\n
Allstar Home Services is committed to protecting its employees, customers, and others while conducting company business. Employment for this position is contingent upon a valid driver's license, a review of the candidate's motor vehicle record (MVR), and successful completion of a background investigation conducted in accordance with Company policies and applicable laws. The Company may conduct MVR checks prior to hire and periodically thereafter. Eligibility to drive a company vehicle is determined based on these reviews, and an unsatisfactory MVR or background investigation may affect driving privileges, vehicle assignment, or employment eligibility. Offers of employment are contingent upon the results of these checks, and any information obtained will be used for employment purposes as permitted by federal and state law and in accordance with Allstar Home Services' Equal Employment Opportunity Policy.

Our reputation has been built over time by doing things the right way—clear communication, quality materials, skilled professionals, and a customer-first approach from consultation through completion. title: Senior Data Engineer (REMOTE)
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Data Engineer. In this role, you will play a crucial part in transforming legacy systems into cloud-native architectures, facilitating the extraction, transformation, and loading (ETL) of complex data from various sources. Your expertise in Python and AWS will support the design and implementation of robust solutions that enable comprehensive data analysis and reporting. The impact of your work will be seen across various analytics and decision-making processes, making this role critical for our partner’s operations. Given the evolution of data demands, your contributions will help set new standards in data management and accessibility.
\n

Accountabilities
Collaborate with data scientists, analysts, and other stakeholders to understand data requirements and deliver tailored data solutions.
Develop efficient data processing and transformation workflows to support analytics and reporting needs.
Design, build, and maintain data pipelines and automated ETL processes using tools like Python, R, and Jupyter Notebooks.
Integrate disparate structured and unstructured data from APIs, databases, and cloud storage into unified datasets.
Implement processes for data cleaning, transformation, and validation to ensure data accuracy and compliance with security policies.
Develop dashboards and visualizations to support operational decision-making.
Provide full lifecycle assistance in deploying, optimizing, and maintaining complex code with data processing routines.

Requirements
5+ years of experience as a Data Engineer, Data Scientist, or Backend Software Engineer.
Bachelor’s degree in computer science, statistics, mathematics, economics, or related field.
Strong skills in Python and SQL, with proficiency in other data engineering languages (e.g., R, SAS, Scala).
Experience handling large-scale data projects involving data integration, cleaning, ETL, and reporting.
Experience developing and implementing data reliability, efficiency, and quality checks.
Experience leveraging AWS services (e.g., S3, EMR, Glue, Lambda) to build data solutions in a cloud-native environment.
Familiarity with various database systems including Oracle, PostgreSQL, MySQL, and MongoDB.

Benefits
Medical, Vision, and Dental Insurance.
401(k) Savings Plan.
Flexible Spending Accounts (FSA).
Education and Professional Training.
Paid Time Off (PTO).
Employee Assistance and Identity Theft Protection.
Employee Referral and Merit Recognition Programs.
Paid Holidays: 11 per year.

\n

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

title: Remote Field Operations Manager
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Field Operations Manager - REMOTE. In this role, you will serve as the operational and analytical backbone for the Strategic sales segment, supporting the sales team aligned to the largest and most complex customers and prospects. Your insights will drive critical business decisions that enhance the overall health of the organization. By analyzing essential metrics and leading strategic initiatives, you will play a pivotal role in optimizing the sales process. You will also drive collaboration across various teams to ensure seamless execution of operational goals while being integral to the company’s mission. This remote opportunity provides the flexibility to impact operations from anywhere.
\n

Accountabilities
Report and analyze key Strategic segment metrics to understand sales funnel metrics, revenue growth, pipeline quality, and overall health of the business.
Lead key annual planning workstreams for the Strategic segment, including book building, territory design, and geo analyses.
Drive Strategic forecasting accuracy, providing the data and analysis leaders need to run effective forecast calls.
Lead the design and build of reporting and dashboarding for the Strategic segment.
Collaborate with Systems to enhance CRM hygiene and scalability for Strategic account reporting.
Analyze whitespace, product penetration, and account potential within Strategic accounts to inform targeting and resourcing.
Build and execute operational processes tailored to Strategic motions to increase efficiency.
Act as a thought partner to cross-functional leadership, providing structured analyses and clear recommendations.

Requirements
5+ years of experience in Sales Operations, Field Operations, Revenue Operations, Business Operations, or Sales Strategy, preferably in B2B SaaS.
Proven experience building strong relationships with sales leadership and executives.
Advanced analytical skills with a track record of working with large and unstructured data sets and building complex models.
Significant experience with Salesforce reporting and dashboarding.
Excellent communication skills and ability to navigate complex ideas effectively.
Proven ability to manage projects from planning to execution in environments with competing priorities.
Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, action-oriented work environment filled with ambiguity.

Benefits
Competitive compensation package based on location, level, and experience.
Comprehensive benefits including Medical, Vision, and Dental coverage.
Generous time-off policy.
Opportunity to contribute to a 401k plan.
Home office improvement stipend.
Annual education and wellness stipends.
Vibrant company culture with regular events.
Daily provision of healthy lunches.

title: Director, Business Development
description_text:
About Us 
Hawthorne Health is a leading community site network and home research visit solution provider, with 20+ sites and more than 2,000 research experienced healthcare professionals across the U.S. Hawthorne leverages convenient, trusted, healthcare locations, identified patient data, and intelligent technology to accelerate patient access, improve retention, and reduce study costs. By embedding high-quality research within local communities, Hawthorne makes clinical trials more accessible and convenient for patients. Our community-based model shortens timelines and helps bring better treatments to market, faster. 

About the Role
Hawthorne Health is seeking a successful hunter and closer who thrives on engaging prospects to become clients, relentlessly working to grow revenue and customer success.

This role will help grow the core business with our current enterprise customers and expand with new customers. The individual must have a proven track record of success at closing complex solutions into the healthcare space with clinical trial sponsors. They must understand the stakeholder ecosystem critical to the success of our organization, including doctors, patients, sponsors, and development partners.

This person will be a key player in the commercial team and will be responsible for identifying, segmenting, engaging, and converting qualified enterprise prospects into paying customers according to a quarterly and annual quota. We are looking for a skilled communicator and presenter who can find the best fit between client and product. The ideal candidate will be organized, passionate about client relations, and focused on enhancing the buyer experience. This role will report to the Chief Commercial Officer.
\n

What You'll Be Doing | The Role
Drive the growth by identifying, segmenting, engaging, and converting new qualified enterprise prospects into paying customers according to a quota
Serve as a subject matter expert in community-based clinical trials
Collaborate with the commercial and clinical operations teams throughout the sales process from lead to post-sales support
Excite our customers in how we are transforming the way clinical trials are conducted in the patient’s home and community
Partner with Project Managers to identify and pursue new business opportunities within current accounts
Respond to RFI’s and RFP’s that illustrate Hawthorne Health’s unique value prop
Be a trusted partner to the product team. Relay customer, sales and market insights, to help inform product roadmap decisions and strategy

What You Bring | The Person
5+ years of successful enterprise Sales or Business Development selling to pharma and biotech clinical operations decision makers

title: Roofing Sales Representative
description_text:
We are a residential roofing company providing high quality home services to the local community. We succeed by providing a premier customer experience. Our mission is to provide quality, with integrity, at a value. From lifetime material and labor warranties to certified factory-trained installers, our goal is to turn our customer's Dreams into reality.
Our culture is fast-paced, diverse, and rewarding. We gauge our success on the success of our employees.
\n

What you will do:
Conduct 10 to 15 pre-scheduled and confirmed in-home appointments per week - No cold calling or door knocking required
Earn more commission for self-generated business
Drive to pre-set appointments
Complete a thorough three week training program with our dedicated sales training team
Attend weekly sales meetings
Additional responsibilities as assigned by leadership

Rewards
Base salary plus commission during the first 6 months. Fully commission after the first 6 month of employment.
Uncapped commission program with ability to earn over six figures in your first year
Promotions from within
Competitive sales contests with cash prizes and other awards
Ongoing classroom style training and in-home sales training
Travel opportunities
Work with supporting departments to take job through completion following point of sale

Must Haves:
Must have reliable vehicle and a valid driver's license
Ability to work a 5 day work week between Monday-Saturday, Saturdays are mandatory
In home sales experience is preferred but not required
Is coachable and eager to learn
Self disciplined and money motivated
Proficient in using technology; laptop, mobile device, tablet etc.
Outstanding negotiation and communication skills

Schedule:
Monday through Friday appointments are scheduled at 10am, 2pm, 6pm
Saturday appointments are scheduled at 10am, and 2pm.

Plusses:
In home sales experience
One call close experience

\n
$125,000 - $250,000 a year
$125,000 - $250,000 a year

Benefits:
Health/Dental/Vision insurance
401(k)
Referral program
Paid time off (PTO)
W-2 Pay Structure
Career advancement opportunities
\n
Allstar Home Services is committed to protecting its employees, customers, and others while conducting company business. Employment for this position is contingent upon a valid driver's license, a review of the candidate's motor vehicle record (MVR), and successful completion of a background investigation conducted in accordance with Company policies and applicable laws. The Company may conduct MVR checks prior to hire and periodically thereafter. Eligibility to drive a company vehicle is determined based on these reviews, and an unsatisfactory MVR or background investigation may affect driving privileges, vehicle assignment, or employment eligibility. M&M Home Remodeling Services is a trusted, BBB-accredited home improvement company with decades of experience serving homeowners with integrity, craftsmanship, and consistency. For more than 50 years, we have specialized in roofing, siding, windows, gutters, and exterior remodeling solutions designed to protect homes and deliver long-term value.

Our reputation has been built over time by doing things the right way—clear communication, quality materials, skilled professionals, and a customer-first approach from consultation through completion. We are proud recipients of multiple Better Business Bureau recognitions, including the 2025 BBB Complaint-Free Award, reflecting our ongoing commitment to excellence and customer satisfaction.

Guided by our core values—Drive, Integrity, Generosity, Growth, and Excellence—we remain focused on earning trust, building relationships, and delivering results homeowners can confidently stand behind.

title: Software Engineer (Remote)
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Remote Software Engineer. In this role, you will be integral to connecting and managing blockchain nodes that interface with various platforms. You will contribute to building a robust infrastructure that enhances the overall efficiency and reliability of our blockchain services. Our aim is to create seamless connections across diverse financial ecosystems while pushing the boundaries of what's possible within the blockchain domain. Join us to make a meaningful impact and help shape the future of the financial system through innovative technology solutions.
\n

Accountabilities
Upgrade blockchain nodes and ensure data consistency.
Implement automation to reduce operational toil and enhance service effectiveness.
Manage and improve blockchain traffic to facilitate seamless connectivity.
Ensure scalability and reliability across the blockchain platform.
Pioneer technologies to decouple node storage and computing.

Requirements
At least 5 years of experience in software engineering.
Experience designing and scaling production services.
Proficient in writing high-quality, well-tested code.
Strong interest in building an open financial system.
Ability to identify and streamline inefficiencies.

Benefits
Opportunity to work with a talented and driven team.
Flexibility of remote work with in-person collaboration events.
Competitive salary and benefits, including medical, dental, and vision.
Participation in employee equity programs.
Support for continuous learning and professional development.

\n

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.
We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

title: Strategic Operations Manager - Remote
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Field Operations Manager - REMOTE. In this role, you will serve as the operational and analytical backbone for the Strategic sales segment, supporting the sales team aligned to the largest and most complex customers and prospects. Your insights will drive critical business decisions that enhance the overall health of the organization. By analyzing essential metrics and leading strategic initiatives, you will play a pivotal role in optimizing the sales process. You will also drive collaboration across various teams to ensure seamless execution of operational goals while being integral to the company’s mission. This remote opportunity provides the flexibility to impact operations from anywhere.
\n

Accountabilities
Report and analyze key Strategic segment metrics to understand sales funnel metrics, revenue growth, pipeline quality, and overall health of the business.
Lead key annual planning workstreams for the Strategic segment, including book building, territory design, and geo analyses.
Drive Strategic forecasting accuracy, providing the data and analysis leaders need to run effective forecast calls.
Lead the design and build of reporting and dashboarding for the Strategic segment.
Collaborate with Systems to enhance CRM hygiene and scalability for Strategic account reporting.
Analyze whitespace, product penetration, and account potential within Strategic accounts to inform targeting and resourcing.
Build and execute operational processes tailored to Strategic motions to increase efficiency.
Act as a thought partner to cross-functional leadership, providing structured analyses and clear recommendations.

Requirements
5+ years of experience in Sales Operations, Field Operations, Revenue Operations, Business Operations, or Sales Strategy, preferably in B2B SaaS.
Proven experience building strong relationships with sales leadership and executives.
Advanced analytical skills with a track record of working with large and unstructured data sets and building complex models.
Significant experience with Salesforce reporting and dashboarding.
Excellent communication skills and ability to navigate complex ideas effectively.
Proven ability to manage projects from planning to execution in environments with competing priorities.
Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, action-oriented work environment filled with ambiguity.

Benefits
Competitive compensation package based on location, level, and experience.
Comprehensive benefits including Medical, Vision, and Dental coverage.
Generous time-off policy.
Opportunity to contribute to a 401k plan.
Home office improvement stipend.
Annual education and wellness stipends.
Vibrant company culture with regular events.
Daily provision of healthy lunches.

\n
Why Apply Through Jobgether?
We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

title: Sr. Franchise Business Coach - REMOTE
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Franchise Business Coach - REMOTE. In this role, you will play a crucial part in supporting a portfolio of franchise locations throughout the United States. Your expertise will empower franchise owners to enhance their operations, boost revenue growth, and align their strategies with brand standards. As a trusted advisor, you will be instrumental in driving performance improvements through structured guidance, meaningful communication, and data-driven insights. This position requires both a solid foundation in business acumen and the ability to build strong relationships with stakeholders.
\n

Accountabilities
Serve as the point of contact for operational guidance and business support to franchise owners
Coach owners and their teams on leadership, marketing, sales, service delivery, and financials
Organize and lead meetings around best practices to drive brand growth and revenue
Build and maintain strong relationships and consistent lines of communication with franchisees
Develop and deliver training and growth initiatives through meetings, workshops, and virtual sessions
Contribute to the creation of marketing, sales, operational, financial and training strategies
Facilitate monthly collaborative owner forums to foster continuous improvement and accountability
Inspire and motivate franchisees to achieve success in their businesses
Plan and execute franchise meetings and regional sessions to support network-wide engagement
Conduct regular check-in calls and field visits, documenting action items and coaching opportunities
Review P&Ls, forecasts, and KPIs with owners; support actionable improvement plans
Facilitate regional and national training programs and cooperative initiatives

Requirements
Bachelor’s degree required
5+ years of operational leadership or business management experience required
3+ years of franchise operations experience required
Demonstrated sales and revenue-growth experience required
Proven ability to guide and influence business owners and leadership teams required
Willingness/ability to travel up to 50% within the US required

Benefits
Competitive salary ranging from $90,000 to $110,000 + Bonus (up to 30%)
Medical, Dental, Vision insurance
401k plan with company match
Parental Leave
Paid Time Off (PTO) and Holidays
Flexible working hours with a remote work option

title: Remote Data Engineer Role
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Data Engineer. In this role, you will play a crucial part in transforming legacy systems into cloud-native architectures, facilitating the extraction, transformation, and loading (ETL) of complex data from various sources. Your expertise in Python and AWS will support the design and implementation of robust solutions that enable comprehensive data analysis and reporting. The impact of your work will be seen across various analytics and decision-making processes, making this role critical for our partner’s operations. Given the evolution of data demands, your contributions will help set new standards in data management and accessibility.
\n

Accountabilities
Collaborate with data scientists, analysts, and other stakeholders to understand data requirements and deliver tailored data solutions.
Develop efficient data processing and transformation workflows to support analytics and reporting needs.
Design, build, and maintain data pipelines and automated ETL processes using tools like Python, R, and Jupyter Notebooks.
Integrate disparate structured and unstructured data from APIs, databases, and cloud storage into unified datasets.
Implement processes for data cleaning, transformation, and validation to ensure data accuracy and compliance with security policies.
Develop dashboards and visualizations to support operational decision-making.
Provide full lifecycle assistance in deploying, optimizing, and maintaining complex code with data processing routines.

Requirements
5+ years of experience as a Data Engineer, Data Scientist, or Backend Software Engineer.
Bachelor’s degree in computer science, statistics, mathematics, economics, or related field.
Strong skills in Python and SQL, with proficiency in other data engineering languages (e.g., R, SAS, Scala).
Experience handling large-scale data projects involving data integration, cleaning, ETL, and reporting.
Experience developing and implementing data reliability, efficiency, and quality checks.
Experience leveraging AWS services (e.g., S3, EMR, Glue, Lambda) to build data solutions in a cloud-native environment.
Familiarity with various database systems including Oracle, PostgreSQL, MySQL, and MongoDB.

Benefits
Medical, Vision, and Dental Insurance.
401(k) Savings Plan.
Flexible Spending Accounts (FSA).
Education and Professional Training.
Paid Time Off (PTO).
Employee Assistance and Identity Theft Protection.
Employee Referral and Merit Recognition Programs.
Paid Holidays: 11 per year.

title: Remote Franchise Management Consultant
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Franchise Business Coach - REMOTE. In this role, you will play a crucial part in supporting a portfolio of franchise locations throughout the United States. Your expertise will empower franchise owners to enhance their operations, boost revenue growth, and align their strategies with brand standards. As a trusted advisor, you will be instrumental in driving performance improvements through structured guidance, meaningful communication, and data-driven insights. This position requires both a solid foundation in business acumen and the ability to build strong relationships with stakeholders.
\n

Accountabilities
Serve as the point of contact for operational guidance and business support to franchise owners
Coach owners and their teams on leadership, marketing, sales, service delivery, and financials
Organize and lead meetings around best practices to drive brand growth and revenue
Build and maintain strong relationships and consistent lines of communication with franchisees
Develop and deliver training and growth initiatives through meetings, workshops, and virtual sessions
Contribute to the creation of marketing, sales, operational, financial and training strategies
Facilitate monthly collaborative owner forums to foster continuous improvement and accountability
Inspire and motivate franchisees to achieve success in their businesses
Plan and execute franchise meetings and regional sessions to support network-wide engagement
Conduct regular check-in calls and field visits, documenting action items and coaching opportunities
Review P&Ls, forecasts, and KPIs with owners; support actionable improvement plans
Facilitate regional and national training programs and cooperative initiatives

Requirements
Bachelor’s degree required
5+ years of operational leadership or business management experience required
3+ years of franchise operations experience required
Demonstrated sales and revenue-growth experience required
Proven ability to guide and influence business owners and leadership teams required
Willingness/ability to travel up to 50% within the US required

Benefits
Competitive salary ranging from $90,000 to $110,000 + Bonus (up to 30%)
Medical, Dental, Vision insurance
401k plan with company match
Parental Leave
Paid Time Off (PTO) and Holidays
Flexible working hours with a remote work option

title: Sr. Field Operations Manager (Remote)
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Field Operations Manager - REMOTE. In this role, you will serve as the operational and analytical backbone for the Strategic sales segment, supporting the sales team aligned to the largest and most complex customers and prospects. Your insights will drive critical business decisions that enhance the overall health of the organization. By analyzing essential metrics and leading strategic initiatives, you will play a pivotal role in optimizing the sales process. You will also drive collaboration across various teams to ensure seamless execution of operational goals while being integral to the company’s mission. This remote opportunity provides the flexibility to impact operations from anywhere.
\n

Accountabilities
Report and analyze key Strategic segment metrics to understand sales funnel metrics, revenue growth, pipeline quality, and overall health of the business.
Lead key annual planning workstreams for the Strategic segment, including book building, territory design, and geo analyses.
Drive Strategic forecasting accuracy, providing the data and analysis leaders need to run effective forecast calls.
Lead the design and build of reporting and dashboarding for the Strategic segment.
Collaborate with Systems to enhance CRM hygiene and scalability for Strategic account reporting.
Analyze whitespace, product penetration, and account potential within Strategic accounts to inform targeting and resourcing.
Build and execute operational processes tailored to Strategic motions to increase efficiency.
Act as a thought partner to cross-functional leadership, providing structured analyses and clear recommendations.

Requirements
5+ years of experience in Sales Operations, Field Operations, Revenue Operations, Business Operations, or Sales Strategy, preferably in B2B SaaS.
Proven experience building strong relationships with sales leadership and executives.
Advanced analytical skills with a track record of working with large and unstructured data sets and building complex models.
Significant experience with Salesforce reporting and dashboarding.
Excellent communication skills and ability to navigate complex ideas effectively.
Proven ability to manage projects from planning to execution in environments with competing priorities.
Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, action-oriented work environment filled with ambiguity.

Benefits
Competitive compensation package based on location, level, and experience.
Comprehensive benefits including Medical, Vision, and Dental coverage.
Generous time-off policy.
Opportunity to contribute to a 401k plan.
Home office improvement stipend.
Annual education and wellness stipends.
Vibrant company culture with regular events.
Daily provision of healthy lunches.

\n
Why Apply Through Jobgether?
We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.
We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!
Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.
#LI-CL1

ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have over 5 years of experience in relevant operations roles, preferably in B2B SaaS. Advanced analytical skills and significant experience with Salesforce are required, along with excellent communication and project management abilities.

title: Remote Operations Manager
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Field Operations Manager - REMOTE. In this role, you will serve as the operational and analytical backbone for the Strategic sales segment, supporting the sales team aligned to the largest and most complex customers and prospects. Your insights will drive critical business decisions that enhance the overall health of the organization. By analyzing essential metrics and leading strategic initiatives, you will play a pivotal role in optimizing the sales process. You will also drive collaboration across various teams to ensure seamless execution of operational goals while being integral to the company’s mission. This remote opportunity provides the flexibility to impact operations from anywhere.
\n

Accountabilities
Report and analyze key Strategic segment metrics to understand sales funnel metrics, revenue growth, pipeline quality, and overall health of the business.
Lead key annual planning workstreams for the Strategic segment, including book building, territory design, and geo analyses.
Drive Strategic forecasting accuracy, providing the data and analysis leaders need to run effective forecast calls.
Lead the design and build of reporting and dashboarding for the Strategic segment.
Collaborate with Systems to enhance CRM hygiene and scalability for Strategic account reporting.
Analyze whitespace, product penetration, and account potential within Strategic accounts to inform targeting and resourcing.
Build and execute operational processes tailored to Strategic motions to increase efficiency.
Act as a thought partner to cross-functional leadership, providing structured analyses and clear recommendations.

Requirements
5+ years of experience in Sales Operations, Field Operations, Revenue Operations, Business Operations, or Sales Strategy, preferably in B2B SaaS.
Proven experience building strong relationships with sales leadership and executives.
Advanced analytical skills with a track record of working with large and unstructured data sets and building complex models.
Significant experience with Salesforce reporting and dashboarding.
Excellent communication skills and ability to navigate complex ideas effectively.
Proven ability to manage projects from planning to execution in environments with competing priorities.
Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, action-oriented work environment filled with ambiguity.

title: Sr. Data Engineer (Remote)
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Data Engineer. In this role, you will play a crucial part in transforming legacy systems into cloud-native architectures, facilitating the extraction, transformation, and loading (ETL) of complex data from various sources. Your expertise in Python and AWS will support the design and implementation of robust solutions that enable comprehensive data analysis and reporting. The impact of your work will be seen across various analytics and decision-making processes, making this role critical for our partner’s operations. Given the evolution of data demands, your contributions will help set new standards in data management and accessibility.
\n

Accountabilities
Collaborate with data scientists, analysts, and other stakeholders to understand data requirements and deliver tailored data solutions.
Develop efficient data processing and transformation workflows to support analytics and reporting needs.
Design, build, and maintain data pipelines and automated ETL processes using tools like Python, R, and Jupyter Notebooks.
Integrate disparate structured and unstructured data from APIs, databases, and cloud storage into unified datasets.
Implement processes for data cleaning, transformation, and validation to ensure data accuracy and compliance with security policies.
Develop dashboards and visualizations to support operational decision-making.
Provide full lifecycle assistance in deploying, optimizing, and maintaining complex code with data processing routines.

Requirements
5+ years of experience as a Data Engineer, Data Scientist, or Backend Software Engineer.
Bachelor’s degree in computer science, statistics, mathematics, economics, or related field.
Strong skills in Python and SQL, with proficiency in other data engineering languages (e.g., R, SAS, Scala).
Experience handling large-scale data projects involving data integration, cleaning, ETL, and reporting.
Experience developing and implementing data reliability, efficiency, and quality checks.
Experience leveraging AWS services (e.g., S3, EMR, Glue, Lambda) to build data solutions in a cloud-native environment.
Familiarity with various database systems including Oracle, PostgreSQL, MySQL, and MongoDB.

Benefits
Medical, Vision, and Dental Insurance.
401(k) Savings Plan.
Flexible Spending Accounts (FSA).
Education and Professional Training.
Paid Time Off (PTO).
Employee Assistance and Identity Theft Protection.
Employee Referral and Merit Recognition Programs.
Paid Holidays: 11 per year.

\n
Why Apply Through Jobgether?
We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Remote Software Engineer. In this role, you will be integral to connecting and managing blockchain nodes that interface with various platforms. You will contribute to building a robust infrastructure that enhances the overall efficiency and reliability of our blockchain services. title: Senior Franchise Business Coach (Remote)
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Franchise Business Coach - REMOTE. In this role, you will play a crucial part in supporting a portfolio of franchise locations throughout the United States. Your expertise will empower franchise owners to enhance their operations, boost revenue growth, and align their strategies with brand standards. As a trusted advisor, you will be instrumental in driving performance improvements through structured guidance, meaningful communication, and data-driven insights. This position requires both a solid foundation in business acumen and the ability to build strong relationships with stakeholders.
\n

Accountabilities
Serve as the point of contact for operational guidance and business support to franchise owners
Coach owners and their teams on leadership, marketing, sales, service delivery, and financials
Organize and lead meetings around best practices to drive brand growth and revenue
Build and maintain strong relationships and consistent lines of communication with franchisees
Develop and deliver training and growth initiatives through meetings, workshops, and virtual sessions
Contribute to the creation of marketing, sales, operational, financial and training strategies
Facilitate monthly collaborative owner forums to foster continuous improvement and accountability
Inspire and motivate franchisees to achieve success in their businesses
Plan and execute franchise meetings and regional sessions to support network-wide engagement
Conduct regular check-in calls and field visits, documenting action items and coaching opportunities
Review P&Ls, forecasts, and KPIs with owners; support actionable improvement plans
Facilitate regional and national training programs and cooperative initiatives

Requirements
Bachelor’s degree required
5+ years of operational leadership or business management experience required
3+ years of franchise operations experience required
Demonstrated sales and revenue-growth experience required
Proven ability to guide and influence business owners and leadership teams required
Willingness/ability to travel up to 50% within the US required

Benefits
Competitive salary ranging from $90,000 to $110,000 + Bonus (up to 30%)
Medical, Dental, Vision insurance
401k plan with company match
Parental Leave
Paid Time Off (PTO) and Holidays
Flexible working hours with a remote work option
