title: Senior Data Engineer (Remote Location)
locations_derived: New York, New York, United States
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Data Engineer. In this role, you will play a crucial part in transforming legacy systems into cloud-native architectures, facilitating the extraction, transformation, and loading (ETL) of complex data from various sources. Your expertise in Python and AWS will support the design and implementation of robust solutions that enable comprehensive data analysis and reporting. The impact of your work will be seen across various analytics and decision-making processes, making this role critical for our partner’s operations. Given the evolution of data demands, your contributions will help set new standards in data management and accessibility.
\n

Accountabilities
Collaborate with data scientists, analysts, and other stakeholders to understand data requirements and deliver tailored data solutions.
Develop efficient data processing and transformation workflows to support analytics and reporting needs.
Design, build, and maintain data pipelines and automated ETL processes using tools like Python, R, and Jupyter Notebooks.
Integrate disparate structured and unstructured data from APIs, databases, and cloud storage into unified datasets.
Implement processes for data cleaning, transformation, and validation to ensure data accuracy and compliance with security policies.
Develop dashboards and visualizations to support operational decision-making.
Provide full lifecycle assistance in deploying, optimizing, and maintaining complex code with data processing routines.

Requirements
5+ years of experience as a Data Engineer, Data Scientist, or Backend Software Engineer.
Bachelor’s degree in computer science, statistics, mathematics, economics, or related field.
Strong skills in Python and SQL, with proficiency in other data engineering languages (e.g., R, SAS, Scala).
Experience handling large-scale data projects involving data integration, cleaning, ETL, and reporting.
Experience developing and implementing data reliability, efficiency, and quality checks.
Experience leveraging AWS services (e.g., S3, EMR, Glue, Lambda) to build data solutions in a cloud-native environment.
Familiarity with various database systems including Oracle, PostgreSQL, MySQL, and MongoDB.

Benefits
Medical, Vision, and Dental Insurance.
401(k) Savings Plan.
Flexible Spending Accounts (FSA).
Education and Professional Training.
Paid Time Off (PTO).
Employee Assistance and Identity Theft Protection.
Employee Referral and Merit Recognition Programs.
Paid Holidays: 11 per year.

\n

title: Senior Operations Manager (Remote)
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Field Operations Manager - REMOTE. In this role, you will serve as the operational and analytical backbone for the Strategic sales segment, supporting the sales team aligned to the largest and most complex customers and prospects. Your insights will drive critical business decisions that enhance the overall health of the organization. By analyzing essential metrics and leading strategic initiatives, you will play a pivotal role in optimizing the sales process. You will also drive collaboration across various teams to ensure seamless execution of operational goals while being integral to the company’s mission. This remote opportunity provides the flexibility to impact operations from anywhere.
\n

Accountabilities
Report and analyze key Strategic segment metrics to understand sales funnel metrics, revenue growth, pipeline quality, and overall health of the business.
Lead key annual planning workstreams for the Strategic segment, including book building, territory design, and geo analyses.
Drive Strategic forecasting accuracy, providing the data and analysis leaders need to run effective forecast calls.
Lead the design and build of reporting and dashboarding for the Strategic segment.
Collaborate with Systems to enhance CRM hygiene and scalability for Strategic account reporting.
Analyze whitespace, product penetration, and account potential within Strategic accounts to inform targeting and resourcing.
Build and execute operational processes tailored to Strategic motions to increase efficiency.
Act as a thought partner to cross-functional leadership, providing structured analyses and clear recommendations.

Requirements
5+ years of experience in Sales Operations, Field Operations, Revenue Operations, Business Operations, or Sales Strategy, preferably in B2B SaaS.
Proven experience building strong relationships with sales leadership and executives.
Advanced analytical skills with a track record of working with large and unstructured data sets and building complex models.
Significant experience with Salesforce reporting and dashboarding.
Excellent communication skills and ability to navigate complex ideas effectively.
Proven ability to manage projects from planning to execution in environments with competing priorities.
Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, action-oriented work environment filled with ambiguity.

Benefits
Competitive compensation package based on location, level, and experience.
Comprehensive benefits including Medical, Vision, and Dental coverage.
Generous time-off policy.
Opportunity to contribute to a 401k plan.
Home office improvement stipend.
Annual education and wellness stipends.
Vibrant company culture with regular events.
Daily provision of healthy lunches.

\n

The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.
We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

title: Senior Research Manager - REMOTE
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Senior Research Manager - REMOTE. In this role, you will join a dynamic contractor team dedicated to enhancing customer experience and human-centered design for a federal agency. By leading mixed-methods research efforts, you'll generate data-driven insights that inform service design, product development, and policy decisions. You will collaborate closely with stakeholders and cross-functional teams to ensure that customer perspectives are integrated throughout project lifecycles, ultimately advocating for improvements that enhance service quality and outcomes.
\n

Accountabilities
Design and lead quantitative and qualitative research studies including surveys, interviews, usability tests, and ethnographic methods.
Lead survey strategy and create, deploy, and analyze results to uncover actionable insights and measure customer sentiment.
Ensure research practices comply with ethical standards, including informed consent and protection of personally identifiable information.
Synthesize qualitative and quantitative data into clear, compelling insights that inform design, product, and policy decisions.
Translate findings into research artifacts such as reports, personas, journey maps, service blueprints, and data visualizations.
Provide strategic direction to analysts on data visualization approaches, ensuring insights are understandable and actionable.
Present findings to stakeholders across government agencies, including senior leaders, advocating for customer-centered improvements.
Partner with CX strategists, service designers, data scientists, and technologists to validate concepts, improve usability, and enhance service delivery.
Apply Human-Centered Design and service design principles across digital and non-digital touchpoints.
Contribute to Voice of the Customer (VoC) programs by analyzing feedback channels, comment streams, and behavioral data.
Collaborate with stakeholders to define CX success metrics and evaluate the impact of research-driven recommendations on service quality and outcomes.
Support continuous improvement efforts by linking research insights to service performance and outcomes.
Thrive in a fast-paced contractor environment, adapting to evolving priorities and collaborating across multiple teams and disciplines.

Requirements
US Citizenship is required by the federal government for this position.
Must be able to obtain Public Trust clearance.
MUST RESIDE IN THE United States (U.S.) and the work MUST BE PERFORMED in the United States (U.S.).
7+ years of experience in UX/CX research, market research, human factors, behavioral science, or related disciplines.
Bachelor’s degree in Psychology, Sociology, Anthropology, Human Factors, HCI, Behavioral Science, UX Research, Service Design, Public Policy, or related field.

Benefits
Opportunity to work remotely from the United States.
Collaborative and supportive team environment.
Contributions to meaningful projects that impact customer experience.
Professional development opportunities and career growth potential.
Access to cutting-edge technologies and methodologies in research.

title: Senior Franchise Consultant - REMOTE
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Franchise Business Coach - REMOTE. In this role, you will play a crucial part in supporting a portfolio of franchise locations throughout the United States. Your expertise will empower franchise owners to enhance their operations, boost revenue growth, and align their strategies with brand standards. As a trusted advisor, you will be instrumental in driving performance improvements through structured guidance, meaningful communication, and data-driven insights. This position requires both a solid foundation in business acumen and the ability to build strong relationships with stakeholders.
\n

Accountabilities
Serve as the point of contact for operational guidance and business support to franchise owners
Coach owners and their teams on leadership, marketing, sales, service delivery, and financials
Organize and lead meetings around best practices to drive brand growth and revenue
Build and maintain strong relationships and consistent lines of communication with franchisees
Develop and deliver training and growth initiatives through meetings, workshops, and virtual sessions
Contribute to the creation of marketing, sales, operational, financial and training strategies
Facilitate monthly collaborative owner forums to foster continuous improvement and accountability
Inspire and motivate franchisees to achieve success in their businesses
Plan and execute franchise meetings and regional sessions to support network-wide engagement
Conduct regular check-in calls and field visits, documenting action items and coaching opportunities
Review P&Ls, forecasts, and KPIs with owners; support actionable improvement plans
Facilitate regional and national training programs and cooperative initiatives

Requirements
Bachelor’s degree required
5+ years of operational leadership or business management experience required
3+ years of franchise operations experience required
Demonstrated sales and revenue-growth experience required
Proven ability to guide and influence business owners and leadership teams required
Willingness/ability to travel up to 50% within the US required

Benefits
Competitive salary ranging from $90,000 to $110,000 + Bonus (up to 30%)
Medical, Dental, Vision insurance
401k plan with company match
Parental Leave
Paid Time Off (PTO) and Holidays
Flexible working hours with a remote work option

In this role, you will be integral to connecting and managing blockchain nodes that interface with various platforms. You will contribute to building a robust infrastructure that enhances the overall efficiency and reliability of our blockchain services. title: Remote Senior Operations Manager
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Field Operations Manager - REMOTE. In this role, you will serve as the operational and analytical backbone for the Strategic sales segment, supporting the sales team aligned to the largest and most complex customers and prospects. Your insights will drive critical business decisions that enhance the overall health of the organization. By analyzing essential metrics and leading strategic initiatives, you will play a pivotal role in optimizing the sales process. You will also drive collaboration across various teams to ensure seamless execution of operational goals while being integral to the company’s mission. This remote opportunity provides the flexibility to impact operations from anywhere.
\n

Accountabilities
Report and analyze key Strategic segment metrics to understand sales funnel metrics, revenue growth, pipeline quality, and overall health of the business.
Lead key annual planning workstreams for the Strategic segment, including book building, territory design, and geo analyses.
Drive Strategic forecasting accuracy, providing the data and analysis leaders need to run effective forecast calls.
Lead the design and build of reporting and dashboarding for the Strategic segment.
Collaborate with Systems to enhance CRM hygiene and scalability for Strategic account reporting.
Analyze whitespace, product penetration, and account potential within Strategic accounts to inform targeting and resourcing.
Build and execute operational processes tailored to Strategic motions to increase efficiency.
Act as a thought partner to cross-functional leadership, providing structured analyses and clear recommendations.

Requirements
5+ years of experience in Sales Operations, Field Operations, Revenue Operations, Business Operations, or Sales Strategy, preferably in B2B SaaS.
Proven experience building strong relationships with sales leadership and executives.
Advanced analytical skills with a track record of working with large and unstructured data sets and building complex models.
Significant experience with Salesforce reporting and dashboarding.
Excellent communication skills and ability to navigate complex ideas effectively.
Proven ability to manage projects from planning to execution in environments with competing priorities.
Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, action-oriented work environment filled with ambiguity.

title: Remote Position: Data Engineer
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Data Engineer. In this role, you will play a crucial part in transforming legacy systems into cloud-native architectures, facilitating the extraction, transformation, and loading (ETL) of complex data from various sources. Your expertise in Python and AWS will support the design and implementation of robust solutions that enable comprehensive data analysis and reporting. The impact of your work will be seen across various analytics and decision-making processes, making this role critical for our partner’s operations. Given the evolution of data demands, your contributions will help set new standards in data management and accessibility.
\n

Accountabilities
Collaborate with data scientists, analysts, and other stakeholders to understand data requirements and deliver tailored data solutions.
Develop efficient data processing and transformation workflows to support analytics and reporting needs.
Design, build, and maintain data pipelines and automated ETL processes using tools like Python, R, and Jupyter Notebooks.
Integrate disparate structured and unstructured data from APIs, databases, and cloud storage into unified datasets.
Implement processes for data cleaning, transformation, and validation to ensure data accuracy and compliance with security policies.
Develop dashboards and visualizations to support operational decision-making.
Provide full lifecycle assistance in deploying, optimizing, and maintaining complex code with data processing routines.

Requirements
5+ years of experience as a Data Engineer, Data Scientist, or Backend Software Engineer.
Bachelor’s degree in computer science, statistics, mathematics, economics, or related field.
Strong skills in Python and SQL, with proficiency in other data engineering languages (e.g., R, SAS, Scala).
Experience handling large-scale data projects involving data integration, cleaning, ETL, and reporting.
Experience developing and implementing data reliability, efficiency, and quality checks.
Experience leveraging AWS services (e.g., S3, EMR, Glue, Lambda) to build data solutions in a cloud-native environment.
Familiarity with various database systems including Oracle, PostgreSQL, MySQL, and MongoDB.

Benefits
Medical, Vision, and Dental Insurance.
401(k) Savings Plan.
Flexible Spending Accounts (FSA).
Education and Professional Training.
Paid Time Off (PTO).
Employee Assistance and Identity Theft Protection.
Employee Referral and Merit Recognition Programs.
Paid Holidays: 11 per year.

Jobgether is an AI-powered career coach and matching platform fixing the broken job search. Remote professionals no longer waste hours applying blindly; instead, they receive a personalized job search strategy, stronger visibility, and curated matches aligned with their skills, flexibility preferences, and career goals.

We flip the hiring model by connecting talent only to roles that truly match, reducing noise for employers and eliminating wasted effort for candidates. Jobgether combines AI coaching, profile optimization, Match Score insights, and the world’s largest remote job database to help people land opportunities faster and with less bias.

Our purpose is to make remote job search guided and intentional. 
Our mission is to become the world’s reference platform for remote talent, ensuring no professional remains invisible and every match is meaningful.

title: Remote Lead Software Engineer
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Remote Software Engineer. In this role, you will be integral to connecting and managing blockchain nodes that interface with various platforms. You will contribute to building a robust infrastructure that enhances the overall efficiency and reliability of our blockchain services. Our aim is to create seamless connections across diverse financial ecosystems while pushing the boundaries of what's possible within the blockchain domain. Join us to make a meaningful impact and help shape the future of the financial system through innovative technology solutions.
\n

Accountabilities
Upgrade blockchain nodes and ensure data consistency.
Implement automation to reduce operational toil and enhance service effectiveness.
Manage and improve blockchain traffic to facilitate seamless connectivity.
Ensure scalability and reliability across the blockchain platform.
Pioneer technologies to decouple node storage and computing.

Requirements
At least 5 years of experience in software engineering.
Experience designing and scaling production services.
Proficient in writing high-quality, well-tested code.
Strong interest in building an open financial system.
Ability to identify and streamline inefficiencies.

Benefits
Opportunity to work with a talented and driven team.
Flexibility of remote work with in-person collaboration events.
Competitive salary and benefits, including medical, dental, and vision.
Participation in employee equity programs.
Support for continuous learning and professional development.

\n
Why Apply Through Jobgether?
We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.
We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!
Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

title: Remote Product Operations Manager
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Product Manager - REMOTE. In this role, you will drive the evolution of our platform, transforming user experiences into essential tools that keep users engaged over the long term. Your expertise in product innovation and cross-functional collaboration will ensure successful execution of our strategic roadmap. This position will allow you to have a significant impact on defining key metrics and fostering engagement through user-centric designs.

Define and execute the product vision and roadmap
Design and ship best-in-class features alongside UX and engineering teams
Translate user insights into intuitive experiences that drive engagement

title: Remote Field Operations Strategist
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Field Operations Manager - REMOTE. In this role, you will serve as the operational and analytical backbone for the Strategic sales segment, supporting the sales team aligned to the largest and most complex customers and prospects. Your insights will drive critical business decisions that enhance the overall health of the organization. By analyzing essential metrics and leading strategic initiatives, you will play a pivotal role in optimizing the sales process. You will also drive collaboration across various teams to ensure seamless execution of operational goals while being integral to the company’s mission. This remote opportunity provides the flexibility to impact operations from anywhere.
\n

Accountabilities
Report and analyze key Strategic segment metrics to understand sales funnel metrics, revenue growth, pipeline quality, and overall health of the business.
Lead key annual planning workstreams for the Strategic segment, including book building, territory design, and geo analyses.
Drive Strategic forecasting accuracy, providing the data and analysis leaders need to run effective forecast calls.
Lead the design and build of reporting and dashboarding for the Strategic segment.
Collaborate with Systems to enhance CRM hygiene and scalability for Strategic account reporting.
Analyze whitespace, product penetration, and account potential within Strategic accounts to inform targeting and resourcing.
Build and execute operational processes tailored to Strategic motions to increase efficiency.
Act as a thought partner to cross-functional leadership, providing structured analyses and clear recommendations.

Requirements
5+ years of experience in Sales Operations, Field Operations, Revenue Operations, Business Operations, or Sales Strategy, preferably in B2B SaaS.
Proven experience building strong relationships with sales leadership and executives.
Advanced analytical skills with a track record of working with large and unstructured data sets and building complex models.
Significant experience with Salesforce reporting and dashboarding.
Excellent communication skills and ability to navigate complex ideas effectively.
Proven ability to manage projects from planning to execution in environments with competing priorities.
Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, action-oriented work environment filled with ambiguity.

Benefits
Competitive compensation package based on location, level, and experience.
Comprehensive benefits including Medical, Vision, and Dental coverage.
Generous time-off policy.
Opportunity to contribute to a 401k plan.
Home office improvement stipend.
Annual education and wellness stipends.
Vibrant company culture with regular events.
Daily provision of healthy lunches.

\n
Why Apply Through Jobgether?
We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.
We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!
Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.
#LI-CL1

ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have over 5 years of experience in relevant operations roles, preferably in B2B SaaS. Advanced analytical skills and experience with Salesforce are essential, along with strong communication and project management abilities.

title: Remote Data Engineer III
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Data Engineer. In this role, you will play a crucial part in transforming legacy systems into cloud-native architectures, facilitating the extraction, transformation, and loading (ETL) of complex data from various sources. Your expertise in Python and AWS will support the design and implementation of robust solutions that enable comprehensive data analysis and reporting. The impact of your work will be seen across various analytics and decision-making processes, making this role critical for our partner’s operations. Given the evolution of data demands, your contributions will help set new standards in data management and accessibility.
\n

Accountabilities
Collaborate with data scientists, analysts, and other stakeholders to understand data requirements and deliver tailored data solutions.
Develop efficient data processing and transformation workflows to support analytics and reporting needs.
Design, build, and maintain data pipelines and automated ETL processes using tools like Python, R, and Jupyter Notebooks.
Integrate disparate structured and unstructured data from APIs, databases, and cloud storage into unified datasets.
Implement processes for data cleaning, transformation, and validation to ensure data accuracy and compliance with security policies.
Develop dashboards and visualizations to support operational decision-making.
Provide full lifecycle assistance in deploying, optimizing, and maintaining complex code with data processing routines.

Requirements
5+ years of experience as a Data Engineer, Data Scientist, or Backend Software Engineer.
Bachelor’s degree in computer science, statistics, mathematics, economics, or related field.
Strong skills in Python and SQL, with proficiency in other data engineering languages (e.g., R, SAS, Scala).
Experience handling large-scale data projects involving data integration, cleaning, ETL, and reporting.
Experience developing and implementing data reliability, efficiency, and quality checks.
Experience leveraging AWS services (e.g., S3, EMR, Glue, Lambda) to build data solutions in a cloud-native environment.
Familiarity with various database systems including Oracle, PostgreSQL, MySQL, and MongoDB.

Benefits
Medical, Vision, and Dental Insurance.
401(k) Savings Plan.
Flexible Spending Accounts (FSA).
Education and Professional Training.
Paid Time Off (PTO).
Employee Assistance and Identity Theft Protection.
Employee Referral and Merit Recognition Programs.
Paid Holidays: 11 per year.

\n
Why Apply Through Jobgether?
We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.
We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

title: Remote Data Engineer Senior
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Data Engineer. In this role, you will play a crucial part in transforming legacy systems into cloud-native architectures, facilitating the extraction, transformation, and loading (ETL) of complex data from various sources. Given the evolution of data demands, your contributions will help set new standards in data management and accessibility.
\n

Accountabilities
Collaborate with data scientists, analysts, and other stakeholders to understand data requirements and deliver tailored data solutions.
Develop efficient data processing and transformation workflows to support analytics and reporting needs.
Design, build, and maintain data pipelines and automated ETL processes using tools like Python, R, and Jupyter Notebooks.
Integrate disparate structured and unstructured data from APIs, databases, and cloud storage into unified datasets.
Implement processes for data cleaning, transformation, and validation to ensure data accuracy and compliance with security policies.
Develop dashboards and visualizations to support operational decision-making.
Provide full lifecycle assistance in deploying, optimizing, and maintaining complex code with data processing routines.

Requirements
5+ years of experience as a Data Engineer, Data Scientist, or Backend Software Engineer.
Bachelor’s degree in computer science, statistics, mathematics, economics, or related field.
Strong skills in Python and SQL, with proficiency in other data engineering languages (e.g., R, SAS, Scala).
Experience handling large-scale data projects involving data integration, cleaning, ETL, and reporting.
Experience developing and implementing data reliability, efficiency, and quality checks.
Experience leveraging AWS services (e.g., S3, EMR, Glue, Lambda) to build data solutions in a cloud-native environment.
Familiarity with various database systems including Oracle, PostgreSQL, MySQL, and MongoDB.

title: Franchise Business Coach - REMOTE
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Franchise Business Coach - REMOTE. In this role, you will play a crucial part in supporting a portfolio of franchise locations throughout the United States. Your expertise will empower franchise owners to enhance their operations, boost revenue growth, and align their strategies with brand standards. As a trusted advisor, you will be instrumental in driving performance improvements through structured guidance, meaningful communication, and data-driven insights. This position requires both a solid foundation in business acumen and the ability to build strong relationships with stakeholders.
\n

Accountabilities
Serve as the point of contact for operational guidance and business support to franchise owners
Coach owners and their teams on leadership, marketing, sales, service delivery, and financials
Organize and lead meetings around best practices to drive brand growth and revenue
Build and maintain strong relationships and consistent lines of communication with franchisees
Develop and deliver training and growth initiatives through meetings, workshops, and virtual sessions
Contribute to the creation of marketing, sales, operational, financial and training strategies
Facilitate monthly collaborative owner forums to foster continuous improvement and accountability
Inspire and motivate franchisees to achieve success in their businesses
Plan and execute franchise meetings and regional sessions to support network-wide engagement
Conduct regular check-in calls and field visits, documenting action items and coaching opportunities
Review P&Ls, forecasts, and KPIs with owners; support actionable improvement plans
Facilitate regional and national training programs and cooperative initiatives

Requirements
Bachelor’s degree required
5+ years of operational leadership or business management experience required
3+ years of franchise operations experience required
Demonstrated sales and revenue-growth experience required
Proven ability to guide and influence business owners and leadership teams required
Willingness/ability to travel up to 50% within the US required

Benefits
Competitive salary ranging from $90,000 to $110,000 + Bonus (up to 30%)
Medical, Dental, Vision insurance
401k plan with company match
Parental Leave
Paid Time Off (PTO) and Holidays
Flexible working hours with a remote work option

title: Remote Data Engineer - Job Opportunity
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Data Engineer. In this role, you will play a crucial part in transforming legacy systems into cloud-native architectures, facilitating the extraction, transformation, and loading (ETL) of complex data from various sources. Your expertise in Python and AWS will support the design and implementation of robust solutions that enable comprehensive data analysis and reporting. The impact of your work will be seen across various analytics and decision-making processes, making this role critical for our partner’s operations. Given the evolution of data demands, your contributions will help set new standards in data management and accessibility.
\n

Accountabilities
Collaborate with data scientists, analysts, and other stakeholders to understand data requirements and deliver tailored data solutions.
Develop efficient data processing and transformation workflows to support analytics and reporting needs.
Design, build, and maintain data pipelines and automated ETL processes using tools like Python, R, and Jupyter Notebooks.
Integrate disparate structured and unstructured data from APIs, databases, and cloud storage into unified datasets.
Implement processes for data cleaning, transformation, and validation to ensure data accuracy and compliance with security policies.
Develop dashboards and visualizations to support operational decision-making.
Provide full lifecycle assistance in deploying, optimizing, and maintaining complex code with data processing routines.

Requirements
5+ years of experience as a Data Engineer, Data Scientist, or Backend Software Engineer.
Bachelor’s degree in computer science, statistics, mathematics, economics, or related field.
Strong skills in Python and SQL, with proficiency in other data engineering languages (e.g., R, SAS, Scala).
Experience handling large-scale data projects involving data integration, cleaning, ETL, and reporting.
Experience developing and implementing data reliability, efficiency, and quality checks.
Experience leveraging AWS services (e.g., S3, EMR, Glue, Lambda) to build data solutions in a cloud-native environment.
Familiarity with various database systems including Oracle, PostgreSQL, MySQL, and MongoDB.

Benefits
Medical, Vision, and Dental Insurance.
401(k) Savings Plan.
Flexible Spending Accounts (FSA).
Education and Professional Training.
Paid Time Off (PTO).
Employee Assistance and Identity Theft Protection.
Employee Referral and Merit Recognition Programs.
Paid Holidays: 11 per year.

\n

title: Remote Business Partner for Franchises
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Franchise Business Coach - REMOTE. In this role, you will play a crucial part in supporting a portfolio of franchise locations throughout the United States. Your expertise will empower franchise owners to enhance their operations, boost revenue growth, and align their strategies with brand standards. As a trusted advisor, you will be instrumental in driving performance improvements through structured guidance, meaningful communication, and data-driven insights. This position requires both a solid foundation in business acumen and the ability to build strong relationships with stakeholders.
\n

Accountabilities
Serve as the point of contact for operational guidance and business support to franchise owners
Coach owners and their teams on leadership, marketing, sales, service delivery, and financials
Organize and lead meetings around best practices to drive brand growth and revenue
Build and maintain strong relationships and consistent lines of communication with franchisees
Develop and deliver training and growth initiatives through meetings, workshops, and virtual sessions
Contribute to the creation of marketing, sales, operational, financial and training strategies
Facilitate monthly collaborative owner forums to foster continuous improvement and accountability
Inspire and motivate franchisees to achieve success in their businesses
Plan and execute franchise meetings and regional sessions to support network-wide engagement
Conduct regular check-in calls and field visits, documenting action items and coaching opportunities
Review P&Ls, forecasts, and KPIs with owners; support actionable improvement plans
Facilitate regional and national training programs and cooperative initiatives

Requirements
Bachelor’s degree required
5+ years of operational leadership or business management experience required
3+ years of franchise operations experience required
Demonstrated sales and revenue-growth experience required
Proven ability to guide and influence business owners and leadership teams required
Willingness/ability to travel up to 50% within the US required

Benefits
Competitive salary ranging from $90,000 to $110,000 + Bonus (up to 30%)
Medical, Dental, Vision insurance
401k plan with company match
Parental Leave
Paid Time Off (PTO) and Holidays
Flexible working hours with a remote work option

title: Remote Data Engineer
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Data Engineer. In this role, you will play a crucial part in transforming legacy systems into cloud-native architectures, facilitating the extraction, transformation, and loading (ETL) of complex data from various sources. Your expertise in Python and AWS will support the design and implementation of robust solutions that enable comprehensive data analysis and reporting. The impact of your work will be seen across various analytics and decision-making processes, making this role critical for our partner’s operations. Given the evolution of data demands, your contributions will help set new standards in data management and accessibility.
\n

Accountabilities
Collaborate with data scientists, analysts, and other stakeholders to understand data requirements and deliver tailored data solutions.
Develop efficient data processing and transformation workflows to support analytics and reporting needs.
Design, build, and maintain data pipelines and automated ETL processes using tools like Python, R, and Jupyter Notebooks.
Integrate disparate structured and unstructured data from APIs, databases, and cloud storage into unified datasets.
Implement processes for data cleaning, transformation, and validation to ensure data accuracy and compliance with security policies.
Develop dashboards and visualizations to support operational decision-making.
Provide full lifecycle assistance in deploying, optimizing, and maintaining complex code with data processing routines.

Requirements
5+ years of experience as a Data Engineer, Data Scientist, or Backend Software Engineer.
Bachelor’s degree in computer science, statistics, mathematics, economics, or related field.
Strong skills in Python and SQL, with proficiency in other data engineering languages (e.g., R, SAS, Scala).
Experience handling large-scale data projects involving data integration, cleaning, ETL, and reporting.
Experience developing and implementing data reliability, efficiency, and quality checks.
Experience leveraging AWS services (e.g., S3, EMR, Glue, Lambda) to build data solutions in a cloud-native environment.
Familiarity with various database systems including Oracle, PostgreSQL, MySQL, and MongoDB.

Benefits
Medical, Vision, and Dental Insurance.
401(k) Savings Plan.
Flexible Spending Accounts (FSA).
Education and Professional Training.
Paid Time Off (PTO).
Employee Assistance and Identity Theft Protection.
Employee Referral and Merit Recognition Programs.
Paid Holidays: 11 per year.

title: Field Operations Manager (Remote)
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Field Operations Manager - REMOTE. In this role, you will serve as the operational and analytical backbone for the Strategic sales segment, supporting the sales team aligned to the largest and most complex customers and prospects. Your insights will drive critical business decisions that enhance the overall health of the organization. By analyzing essential metrics and leading strategic initiatives, you will play a pivotal role in optimizing the sales process. You will also drive collaboration across various teams to ensure seamless execution of operational goals while being integral to the company’s mission. This remote opportunity provides the flexibility to impact operations from anywhere.
\n

Accountabilities
Report and analyze key Strategic segment metrics to understand sales funnel metrics, revenue growth, pipeline quality, and overall health of the business.
Lead key annual planning workstreams for the Strategic segment, including book building, territory design, and geo analyses.
Drive Strategic forecasting accuracy, providing the data and analysis leaders need to run effective forecast calls.
Lead the design and build of reporting and dashboarding for the Strategic segment.
Collaborate with Systems to enhance CRM hygiene and scalability for Strategic account reporting.
Analyze whitespace, product penetration, and account potential within Strategic accounts to inform targeting and resourcing.
Build and execute operational processes tailored to Strategic motions to increase efficiency.
Act as a thought partner to cross-functional leadership, providing structured analyses and clear recommendations.

Requirements
5+ years of experience in Sales Operations, Field Operations, Revenue Operations, Business Operations, or Sales Strategy, preferably in B2B SaaS.
Proven experience building strong relationships with sales leadership and executives.
Advanced analytical skills with a track record of working with large and unstructured data sets and building complex models.
Significant experience with Salesforce reporting and dashboarding.
Excellent communication skills and ability to navigate complex ideas effectively.
Proven ability to manage projects from planning to execution in environments with competing priorities.
Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, action-oriented work environment filled with ambiguity.

Benefits
Competitive compensation package based on location, level, and experience.
Comprehensive benefits including Medical, Vision, and Dental coverage.
Generous time-off policy.

title: Field Operations Manager - REMOTE
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Field Operations Manager - REMOTE. In this role, you will serve as the operational and analytical backbone for the Strategic sales segment, supporting the sales team aligned to the largest and most complex customers and prospects. title: Franchise Business Advisor (Remote)
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Franchise Business Coach - REMOTE. In this role, you will play a crucial part in supporting a portfolio of franchise locations throughout the United States. Your expertise will empower franchise owners to enhance their operations, boost revenue growth, and align their strategies with brand standards. As a trusted advisor, you will be instrumental in driving performance improvements through structured guidance, meaningful communication, and data-driven insights. This position requires both a solid foundation in business acumen and the ability to build strong relationships with stakeholders.
\n

Accountabilities
Serve as the point of contact for operational guidance and business support to franchise owners
Coach owners and their teams on leadership, marketing, sales, service delivery, and financials
Organize and lead meetings around best practices to drive brand growth and revenue
Build and maintain strong relationships and consistent lines of communication with franchisees
Develop and deliver training and growth initiatives through meetings, workshops, and virtual sessions
Contribute to the creation of marketing, sales, operational, financial and training strategies
Facilitate monthly collaborative owner forums to foster continuous improvement and accountability
Inspire and motivate franchisees to achieve success in their businesses
Plan and execute franchise meetings and regional sessions to support network-wide engagement
Conduct regular check-in calls and field visits, documenting action items and coaching opportunities
Review P&Ls, forecasts, and KPIs with owners; support actionable improvement plans
Facilitate regional and national training programs and cooperative initiatives

Requirements
Bachelor’s degree required
5+ years of operational leadership or business management experience required
3+ years of franchise operations experience required
Demonstrated sales and revenue-growth experience required
Proven ability to guide and influence business owners and leadership teams required
Willingness/ability to travel up to 50% within the US required

Benefits
Competitive salary ranging from $90,000 to $110,000 + Bonus (up to 30%)
Medical, Dental, Vision insurance
401k plan with company match
Parental Leave
Paid Time Off (PTO) and Holidays
Flexible working hours with a remote work option

\n
Why Apply Through Jobgether?

title: Lead Data Engineer (Remote)
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Data Engineer. In this role, you will play a crucial part in transforming legacy systems into cloud-native architectures, facilitating the extraction, transformation, and loading (ETL) of complex data from various sources. Your expertise in Python and AWS will support the design and implementation of robust solutions that enable comprehensive data analysis and reporting. The impact of your work will be seen across various analytics and decision-making processes, making this role critical for our partner’s operations. Given the evolution of data demands, your contributions will help set new standards in data management and accessibility.
\n

Accountabilities
Collaborate with data scientists, analysts, and other stakeholders to understand data requirements and deliver tailored data solutions.
Develop efficient data processing and transformation workflows to support analytics and reporting needs.
Design, build, and maintain data pipelines and automated ETL processes using tools like Python, R, and Jupyter Notebooks.
Integrate disparate structured and unstructured data from APIs, databases, and cloud storage into unified datasets.
Implement processes for data cleaning, transformation, and validation to ensure data accuracy and compliance with security policies.
Develop dashboards and visualizations to support operational decision-making.
Provide full lifecycle assistance in deploying, optimizing, and maintaining complex code with data processing routines.

Requirements
5+ years of experience as a Data Engineer, Data Scientist, or Backend Software Engineer.
Bachelor’s degree in computer science, statistics, mathematics, economics, or related field.
Strong skills in Python and SQL, with proficiency in other data engineering languages (e.g., R, SAS, Scala).
Experience handling large-scale data projects involving data integration, cleaning, ETL, and reporting.
Experience developing and implementing data reliability, efficiency, and quality checks.
Experience leveraging AWS services (e.g., S3, EMR, Glue, Lambda) to build data solutions in a cloud-native environment.
Familiarity with various database systems including Oracle, PostgreSQL, MySQL, and MongoDB.

Benefits
Medical, Vision, and Dental Insurance.
401(k) Savings Plan.
Flexible Spending Accounts (FSA).
Education and Professional Training.
Paid Time Off (PTO).
Employee Assistance and Identity Theft Protection.
Employee Referral and Merit Recognition Programs.
Paid Holidays: 11 per year.

\n

title: Field Operations Manager - REMOTE
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Field Operations Manager - REMOTE. In this role, you will serve as the operational and analytical backbone for the Strategic sales segment, supporting the sales team aligned to the largest and most complex customers and prospects. Your insights will drive critical business decisions that enhance the overall health of the organization. By analyzing essential metrics and leading strategic initiatives, you will play a pivotal role in optimizing the sales process. You will also drive collaboration across various teams to ensure seamless execution of operational goals while being integral to the company’s mission. This remote opportunity provides the flexibility to impact operations from anywhere.
\n

Accountabilities
Report and analyze key Strategic segment metrics to understand sales funnel metrics, revenue growth, pipeline quality, and overall health of the business.
Lead key annual planning workstreams for the Strategic segment, including book building, territory design, and geo analyses.
Drive Strategic forecasting accuracy, providing the data and analysis leaders need to run effective forecast calls.
Lead the design and build of reporting and dashboarding for the Strategic segment.
Collaborate with Systems to enhance CRM hygiene and scalability for Strategic account reporting.
Analyze whitespace, product penetration, and account potential within Strategic accounts to inform targeting and resourcing.
Build and execute operational processes tailored to Strategic motions to increase efficiency.
Act as a thought partner to cross-functional leadership, providing structured analyses and clear recommendations.

Requirements
5+ years of experience in Sales Operations, Field Operations, Revenue Operations, Business Operations, or Sales Strategy, preferably in B2B SaaS.
Proven experience building strong relationships with sales leadership and executives.
Advanced analytical skills with a track record of working with large and unstructured data sets and building complex models.
Significant experience with Salesforce reporting and dashboarding.
Excellent communication skills and ability to navigate complex ideas effectively.
Proven ability to manage projects from planning to execution in environments with competing priorities.
Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, action-oriented work environment filled with ambiguity.

Benefits
Competitive compensation package based on location, level, and experience.
Comprehensive benefits including Medical, Vision, and Dental coverage.
Generous time-off policy.
Opportunity to contribute to a 401k plan.
Home office improvement stipend.
Annual education and wellness stipends.
Vibrant company culture with regular events.
Daily provision of healthy lunches.

title: Lead Data Engineer - Remote
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Data Engineer. In this role, you will play a crucial part in transforming legacy systems into cloud-native architectures, facilitating the extraction, transformation, and loading (ETL) of complex data from various sources. Your expertise in Python and AWS will support the design and implementation of robust solutions that enable comprehensive data analysis and reporting. The impact of your work will be seen across various analytics and decision-making processes, making this role critical for our partner’s operations. Given the evolution of data demands, your contributions will help set new standards in data management and accessibility.
\n

Accountabilities
Collaborate with data scientists, analysts, and other stakeholders to understand data requirements and deliver tailored data solutions.
Develop efficient data processing and transformation workflows to support analytics and reporting needs.
Design, build, and maintain data pipelines and automated ETL processes using tools like Python, R, and Jupyter Notebooks.
Integrate disparate structured and unstructured data from APIs, databases, and cloud storage into unified datasets.
Implement processes for data cleaning, transformation, and validation to ensure data accuracy and compliance with security policies.
Develop dashboards and visualizations to support operational decision-making.
Provide full lifecycle assistance in deploying, optimizing, and maintaining complex code with data processing routines.

Requirements
5+ years of experience as a Data Engineer, Data Scientist, or Backend Software Engineer.
Bachelor’s degree in computer science, statistics, mathematics, economics, or related field.
Strong skills in Python and SQL, with proficiency in other data engineering languages (e.g., R, SAS, Scala).
Experience handling large-scale data projects involving data integration, cleaning, ETL, and reporting.
Experience developing and implementing data reliability, efficiency, and quality checks.
Experience leveraging AWS services (e.g., S3, EMR, Glue, Lambda) to build data solutions in a cloud-native environment.
Familiarity with various database systems including Oracle, PostgreSQL, MySQL, and MongoDB.

Benefits
Medical, Vision, and Dental Insurance.
401(k) Savings Plan.
Flexible Spending Accounts (FSA).
Education and Professional Training.
Paid Time Off (PTO).
Employee Assistance and Identity Theft Protection.
Employee Referral and Merit Recognition Programs.
Paid Holidays: 11 per year.

title: Graphic Artist - REMOTE
locations_derived: New York, New York, United States
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Graphic Artist. As a key contributor to the Sleepwear Division, you will create and coordinate the production of graphics and all-over prints. Collaborating closely with the Art Director and design team, your work will influence brand identity and drive innovation across various popular brands. This role is crucial for delivering high-quality designs that resonate with customers while adhering to production timelines and market trends.
\n

Accountabilities
Create graphics and all-over repeat prints under the direction of the Art and Design Directors.
Research seasonal graphic trends across accounts, licenses, and markets to inspire new designs.
Create artwork in diverse illustration and graphic styles that uphold brand identity while advancing the creative vision.
Prepare user-friendly art files for Production, including repeat layouts, recoloring, technique assignment, and tech pack creation.
Update and modify graphics and prints as needed based on feedback from Sales, Buyers, Production teams, and Licensors.
Maintain technique libraries and art resources, staying current with new art methods and trends for team access.
Contribute to presentations and special projects, keeping presentation boards and visual materials up to date.
Collaborate effectively with Graphic, Design, and Sales teams, and participate in art critiques and reviews.

Requirements
4–8 years of experience in graphic and print design for branded and private-label apparel collections.
Strong illustration skills with the ability to sketch and develop creative ideas.
Extensive experience in creating graphics specifically for adult apparel in both Illustrator and Photoshop.
Proven expertise creating graphics and seamless all-over repeat patterns for adult apparel in Illustrator and Photoshop.
Collaborative mindset, working closely with Art Director, Design, and Sales teams.
Proficient in Adobe Illustrator, Photoshop, and Mac systems.
Positive, proactive team player with a passion for innovation and thinking creatively.
Strong multitasking and time management skills, able to meet multiple deadlines efficiently.
Understanding of cross-functional collaboration and product lifecycles.

Benefits
Industry-competitive salary of $85,000-$90,000.
Comprehensive benefits plan including medical, dental, and vision.
401(k) plan with matching contributions.
Summer Fridays and generous PTO.
Merchandise discounts.
Excellent career development opportunities.
Collaborative work environment focused on innovation.
Engagement in social impact programs and D&I initiatives.

title: Franchise Operations Consultant (Remote)
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Franchise Business Coach - REMOTE. In this role, you will play a crucial part in supporting a portfolio of franchise locations throughout the United States. Your expertise will empower franchise owners to enhance their operations, boost revenue growth, and align their strategies with brand standards. As a trusted advisor, you will be instrumental in driving performance improvements through structured guidance, meaningful communication, and data-driven insights. This position requires both a solid foundation in business acumen and the ability to build strong relationships with stakeholders.

Serve as the point of contact for operational guidance and business support to franchise owners

Build and maintain strong relationships and consistent lines of communication with franchisees
Develop and deliver training and growth initiatives through meetings, workshops, and virtual sessions
Contribute to the creation of marketing, sales, operational, financial and training strategies
Facilitate monthly collaborative owner forums to foster continuous improvement and accountability
Inspire and motivate franchisees to achieve success in their businesses
Plan and execute franchise meetings and regional sessions to support network-wide engagement

title: Lead Operations Manager (Remote)
locations_derived: Washington, District of Columbia, United States
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Field Operations Manager - REMOTE. In this role, you will serve as the operational and analytical backbone for the Strategic sales segment, supporting the sales team aligned to the largest and most complex customers and prospects. Your insights will drive critical business decisions that enhance the overall health of the organization. By analyzing essential metrics and leading strategic initiatives, you will play a pivotal role in optimizing the sales process. You will also drive collaboration across various teams to ensure seamless execution of operational goals while being integral to the company’s mission. This remote opportunity provides the flexibility to impact operations from anywhere.
\n

Accountabilities
Report and analyze key Strategic segment metrics to understand sales funnel metrics, revenue growth, pipeline quality, and overall health of the business.
Lead key annual planning workstreams for the Strategic segment, including book building, territory design, and geo analyses.
Drive Strategic forecasting accuracy, providing the data and analysis leaders need to run effective forecast calls.
Lead the design and build of reporting and dashboarding for the Strategic segment.
Collaborate with Systems to enhance CRM hygiene and scalability for Strategic account reporting.
Analyze whitespace, product penetration, and account potential within Strategic accounts to inform targeting and resourcing.
Build and execute operational processes tailored to Strategic motions to increase efficiency.
Act as a thought partner to cross-functional leadership, providing structured analyses and clear recommendations.

Requirements
5+ years of experience in Sales Operations, Field Operations, Revenue Operations, Business Operations, or Sales Strategy, preferably in B2B SaaS.
Proven experience building strong relationships with sales leadership and executives.
Advanced analytical skills with a track record of working with large and unstructured data sets and building complex models.
Significant experience with Salesforce reporting and dashboarding.
Excellent communication skills and ability to navigate complex ideas effectively.
Proven ability to manage projects from planning to execution in environments with competing priorities.
Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, action-oriented work environment filled with ambiguity.

Benefits
Competitive compensation package based on location, level, and experience.
Comprehensive benefits including Medical, Vision, and Dental coverage.
Generous time-off policy.
Opportunity to contribute to a 401k plan.
Home office improvement stipend.
Annual education and wellness stipends.

Daily provision of healthy lunches.

title: Data Engineer - REMOTE
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Data Engineer. In this role, you will play a crucial part in transforming legacy systems into cloud-native architectures, facilitating the extraction, transformation, and loading (ETL) of complex data from various sources. Your expertise in Python and AWS will support the design and implementation of robust solutions that enable comprehensive data analysis and reporting. The impact of your work will be seen across various analytics and decision-making processes, making this role critical for our partner’s operations. Given the evolution of data demands, your contributions will help set new standards in data management and accessibility.
\n

Accountabilities
Collaborate with data scientists, analysts, and other stakeholders to understand data requirements and deliver tailored data solutions.
Develop efficient data processing and transformation workflows to support analytics and reporting needs.
Design, build, and maintain data pipelines and automated ETL processes using tools like Python, R, and Jupyter Notebooks.
Integrate disparate structured and unstructured data from APIs, databases, and cloud storage into unified datasets.
Implement processes for data cleaning, transformation, and validation to ensure data accuracy and compliance with security policies.
Develop dashboards and visualizations to support operational decision-making.
Provide full lifecycle assistance in deploying, optimizing, and maintaining complex code with data processing routines.

Requirements
5+ years of experience as a Data Engineer, Data Scientist, or Backend Software Engineer.
Bachelor’s degree in computer science, statistics, mathematics, economics, or related field.
Strong skills in Python and SQL, with proficiency in other data engineering languages (e.g., R, SAS, Scala).
Experience handling large-scale data projects involving data integration, cleaning, ETL, and reporting.
Experience developing and implementing data reliability, efficiency, and quality checks.
Experience leveraging AWS services (e.g., S3, EMR, Glue, Lambda) to build data solutions in a cloud-native environment.
Familiarity with various database systems including Oracle, PostgreSQL, MySQL, and MongoDB.

Benefits
Medical, Vision, and Dental Insurance.
401(k) Savings Plan.
Flexible Spending Accounts (FSA).
Education and Professional Training.
Paid Time Off (PTO).
Employee Assistance and Identity Theft Protection.
Employee Referral and Merit Recognition Programs.
Paid Holidays: 11 per year.

\n

title: Field Operations Lead - Remote
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Field Operations Manager - REMOTE. In this role, you will serve as the operational and analytical backbone for the Strategic sales segment, supporting the sales team aligned to the largest and most complex customers and prospects. Your insights will drive critical business decisions that enhance the overall health of the organization. By analyzing essential metrics and leading strategic initiatives, you will play a pivotal role in optimizing the sales process. You will also drive collaboration across various teams to ensure seamless execution of operational goals while being integral to the company’s mission. This remote opportunity provides the flexibility to impact operations from anywhere.
\n

Accountabilities
Report and analyze key Strategic segment metrics to understand sales funnel metrics, revenue growth, pipeline quality, and overall health of the business.
Lead key annual planning workstreams for the Strategic segment, including book building, territory design, and geo analyses.
Drive Strategic forecasting accuracy, providing the data and analysis leaders need to run effective forecast calls.
Lead the design and build of reporting and dashboarding for the Strategic segment.
Collaborate with Systems to enhance CRM hygiene and scalability for Strategic account reporting.
Analyze whitespace, product penetration, and account potential within Strategic accounts to inform targeting and resourcing.
Build and execute operational processes tailored to Strategic motions to increase efficiency.
Act as a thought partner to cross-functional leadership, providing structured analyses and clear recommendations.

Requirements
5+ years of experience in Sales Operations, Field Operations, Revenue Operations, Business Operations, or Sales Strategy, preferably in B2B SaaS.
Proven experience building strong relationships with sales leadership and executives.
Advanced analytical skills with a track record of working with large and unstructured data sets and building complex models.
Significant experience with Salesforce reporting and dashboarding.
Excellent communication skills and ability to navigate complex ideas effectively.
Proven ability to manage projects from planning to execution in environments with competing priorities.
Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, action-oriented work environment filled with ambiguity.

Benefits
Competitive compensation package based on location, level, and experience.
Comprehensive benefits including Medical, Vision, and Dental coverage.
Generous time-off policy.
Opportunity to contribute to a 401k plan.
Home office improvement stipend.
Annual education and wellness stipends.
Vibrant company culture with regular events.
Daily provision of healthy lunches.

\n

title: Data Engineer III - REMOTE
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Data Engineer. In this role, you will play a crucial part in transforming legacy systems into cloud-native architectures, facilitating the extraction, transformation, and loading (ETL) of complex data from various sources. Your expertise in Python and AWS will support the design and implementation of robust solutions that enable comprehensive data analysis and reporting. The impact of your work will be seen across various analytics and decision-making processes, making this role critical for our partner’s operations. Given the evolution of data demands, your contributions will help set new standards in data management and accessibility.
\n

Accountabilities
Collaborate with data scientists, analysts, and other stakeholders to understand data requirements and deliver tailored data solutions.
Develop efficient data processing and transformation workflows to support analytics and reporting needs.
Design, build, and maintain data pipelines and automated ETL processes using tools like Python, R, and Jupyter Notebooks.
Integrate disparate structured and unstructured data from APIs, databases, and cloud storage into unified datasets.
Implement processes for data cleaning, transformation, and validation to ensure data accuracy and compliance with security policies.
Develop dashboards and visualizations to support operational decision-making.
Provide full lifecycle assistance in deploying, optimizing, and maintaining complex code with data processing routines.

Requirements
5+ years of experience as a Data Engineer, Data Scientist, or Backend Software Engineer.
Bachelor’s degree in computer science, statistics, mathematics, economics, or related field.
Strong skills in Python and SQL, with proficiency in other data engineering languages (e.g., R, SAS, Scala).
Experience handling large-scale data projects involving data integration, cleaning, ETL, and reporting.
Experience developing and implementing data reliability, efficiency, and quality checks.
Experience leveraging AWS services (e.g., S3, EMR, Glue, Lambda) to build data solutions in a cloud-native environment.
Familiarity with various database systems including Oracle, PostgreSQL, MySQL, and MongoDB.

Benefits
Medical, Vision, and Dental Insurance.
401(k) Savings Plan.
Flexible Spending Accounts (FSA).
Education and Professional Training.
Paid Time Off (PTO).
Employee Assistance and Identity Theft Protection.
Employee Referral and Merit Recognition Programs.
Paid Holidays: 11 per year.

\n
Why Apply Through Jobgether?
We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

title: Warehouse Associate - 1092
description_text:
Our Opportunity:
Fetch is a venture-backed company redefining multifamily living through four core services: Package Delivery, Valet Trash, Fetch Market, and Fetch Storage. From secure, direct-to-door package delivery to community-wide valet trash, 24/7 essentials delivered through Fetch Market, and flexible storage solutions, Fetch streamlines operations for property teams while creating unmatched convenience for residents. Operating nationwide with rapid growth ahead, we combine logistics, technology, and hospitality to shape the future of multifamily living.

Join the first off-site package management solution for apartment buildings. At Fetch, we communicate directly with apartment residents to coordinate direct-to-door package delivery, ensuring a stress-free and convenient experience. 
As an Warehouse Associate, you will help maintain a safe work environment in our warehouse, process packages, pick driver routes, perform inventory audits, clean workspaces, and assist delivery drivers complete and schedule delivery drops at local communities. The ideal candidate is passionate about serving others, a great communicator, proficient using smartphone applications, and a team player.
Fetch is a company of people taking care of people – just like our clients we take care of our employees. At Fetch, we’re very proud of the teams we’ve assembled and are lucky to have a group of incredibly hard workers. If you think you’re the right fit for this position, apply today. We’re always looking for awesome people to join our growing team. 

What You'll Do:
Former warehouse experience is a plus, but not required
Proficient using smartphone applications and scanning devices
Ability to thrive in a fast-paced environment and meet deadlines
Desire to help others and be a team player
Clear and concise with electronic and verbal communication

What You'll Need:
Regularly lift and move parcels 
Lift parcels that weigh up to 150lbs via material equipment or team lift
The ability to crouch, kneel and stand regularly throughout shifts
Assist with moving loads of parcels to and from delivery cargo vans and throughout communities 
Close, distance and peripheral vision along with depth perception required during the fulfillment process
Operate hand-activated pallet jacks and convertible push trucks
\n

\n
$15.50 - $15.50 an hour
\n
What We'll Deliver On:
- Competitive pay rates 
- Medical
- Dental
- Vision insurance
- 401(k)
- AD&D/STD/LTD
- Paid time off

We also pay weekly.
Fetch is an equal opportunity employer, all applicants will be considered without discrimination on the basis of race, religion, national origin, age, sex, marital status, disabilities, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, veteran status or any other characteristics protected by law.

title: Junior Creator Program Associate of Spanish Language Programming
description_text:
Vocal is seeking applicants to support influencer campaigns that reach Latino and Spanish-speaking audiences. The Jr. Creator Program Associate of Spanish Language Programming will work closely with multiple progressive organizations that work to advance the standards of living for Latino communities in the United States through electoral politics and issue advocacy. They will also support efforts to expand Vocal’s influence in this space and foster relationships with creators, influencers, and progressive organizations dedicated to driving meaningful change in Latino communities.
\n

Responsibilities
Support digital marketing campaigns in both English and Spanish across Instagram, TikTok, Twitch, Snapchat, X, etc. through creator sourcing, negotiation and other creator-facing communications.
Develop compelling written materials in both English and Spanish to achieve program goals, including creator messaging guidance and reports that are tailored to the needs of progressive organizations.
Monitor and report on program successes and challenges, providing actionable insights for improvement and proactively adjusting strategy as needed.
Stay at the forefront of emerging trends across social media and amongst Latino audiences, to ensure high-quality and timely content
Work closely with digital creators to develop creative and engaging content that meets campaign brief requirements and results in high engagement and performance— particularly Latino and Spanish-speaking creators.
Support the development of post-program reports for clients.
Report internally to Vocal leadership.

title: Researcher (Part-Time, Contract)
description_text:
We are seeking detail-oriented, proactive Researchers to support the development of a robust multimedia content library by sourcing, logging, tagging, and organizing high-quality digital assets. In this role, you will monitor breaking news, general online trends, and viral cultural moments across a wide range of topics—including U.S. politics, podcasts, lifestyle, sports, wellness, fashion, and technology—to identify timely content opportunities and build strong evergreen and archival libraries. Researchers will work 10-15 hours per week on a contract basis.

Work 10–15 hours per week;
Attend one 30-minute research team meeting each week;
Upload several folders of video assets per week to Vocal’s Digital Asset Manager, aligned with campaign editorial needs and given news cycles;
Keep in close communication with manager and other researchers via slack channel daily;
Finish research sprint tasks in a timely fashion by the end of each sprint

Research & Catalogue 
Source, log, and tag relevant content to help build and expand our multimedia library;
Track breaking political news and major market trends for rapid-response coverage;
Monitor viral and trending content across topics like lifestyle, sports, wellness, fashion, and technology;
Build libraries of archival and evergreen political and cultural video content

Documentation and Analysis

Respond quickly to asset and content requests from social media managers;
Organize assets into folders and maintain a clear, searchable content database.

Collaboration

Participate in strategy discussions and contribute research insights.
\n

Strong research skills and the ability to quickly find relevant, high-quality digital content
Highly organized with excellent attention to detail (especially when tagging, labeling, and managing assets)
Experience working with digital asset management systems, databases, or content libraries (preferred)

title: Sanitor
description_text:
*The schedule for this role is: Mon-Fri 4am-12:30pm, Saturdays as needed*

The Sanitor is responsible for maintaining cleanliness and sanitation standards throughout the production facility. This includes cleaning equipment, floors, walls, and other surfaces to ensure compliance with food safety and quality standards. The Sanitor plays a critical role in supporting the company’s Good Manufacturing Practices (GMPs), allergen control, and overall hygiene protocols.
\n

Duties/Responsibilities:
Perform routine cleaning and sanitation of production equipment, floors, walls, and facility infrastructure.
Conduct allergen washdowns and post-production sanitation procedures as directed.
Monitor and verify chemical concentrations and titrations; report and correct deviations.
Install and maintain sanitation-related equipment such as paper towel, soap, and sanitizer dispensers.
Maintain inventory of sanitation supplies and notify supervisor of restocking needs.
Follow all safety and food safety protocols including PPE usage, lockout/tagout, and chemical handling.
Complete sanitation logs and documentation accurately and on time.
Participate in sanitation audits and inspections; assist in corrective actions as needed.
Communicate with production and QA teams regarding sanitation needs or deficiencies.
Support environmental testing procedures such as ATP swabbing and pathogen sampling

Required Skills/Abilities:
Ability to follow written and verbal instructions in English or Spanish.
Basic understanding of sanitation chemicals and safe handling procedures.
Attention to detail and commitment to cleanliness and safety.
Ability to work independently and as part of a team.
Willingness to work flexible hours, including weekends and overnight shifts.

Education and Experience:
High school diploma or equivalent preferred.
Prior experience in food manufacturing or sanitation is a plus.
On-the-job training provided.

Working Conditions:
Work performed in a cold, wet, and noisy food production environment.
Must be able to stand, walk, bend, and lift up to 50 lbs for extended periods.
Exposure to cleaning chemicals and use of PPE required.

\n
$21.97 - $21.97 an hour
pay rate includes 3rd shift pay differential
\n

Charcuterie Artisans brings together two of the worlds best artisan charcuterie producers and is committed to enriching lives through charcuterie.
